The U.S. Army Corps revoked a crucial Clean Water Act permit for the proposed NewRange Copper Nickel (PolyMet) mine in northeastern Minnesota after finding it would not ensure compliance with downstream water-quality requirements of the Fond du Lac Band. The permit had been suspended previously. | The article headline says the Army Corps has pulled a key permit for a copper-nickel mine near Hoyt Lakes. Losing or delaying permitting can hinder operations, reduce near-term output visibility, and raise supply risk. That tends to pressure copper and nickel prices, while aluminium is not directly implicated. | The US Army Corps revoked a permit for the NewRange copper-nickel mine. The decision raises the risk that the project will be delayed or curtailed, tightening near-term supply expectations. That dynamic can weigh on prices for both copper and nickel within coming weeks. | The US Army Corps revokes a permit for the NewRange copper-nickel mine. The decision could delay or impede development and production, reducing near-term supply expectations. That typically supports lower pricing risk for copper and nickel as markets anticipate potential output shortfalls. | DMCI Mining’s unit Zambales Diversified Metals secured an Environmental Compliance Certificate to expand nickel-ore production capacity to 2 million WMT from 1 million WMT. ZDMC produced 599,000 WMT in Q1 (up 88%) and raised inventory 16%. DMCI targets 1.5M WMT shipments in 2023, supporting nickel supply expectations. | Reuters reports nickel has underperformed, with LME three-month nickel hitting a nine-month low as markets price an impending supply glut from Indonesia’s rapidly expanding output. Despite LME and SHFE stockpiles at multi-year lows, inventories are still falling, cushioning but not reversing bearish sentiment. | The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers revoked a federal permit for PolyMet’s copper-nickel mine. Ero Copper updated its Curaçá Valley exploration for nickel sulphide at the Umburana System, reporting new drill intercepts with up to 7.09% Ni and initial rougher metallurgical recoveries of 77–91%. While copper grades are included in NiEq, the announcement is mainly exploration progress with no production change. | Ero Copper announced updated nickel sulphide exploration results from the Curaçá Valley, including drill intercepts such as 11.2 meters at 1.86% Ni and a higher-grade interval with 3.71% Ni, plus minor copper. Australian shares rose as mining stocks gained and expectations of a pause in U.S. rate hikes improved sentiment. The key nickel link came from Nickel Industries, which announced it would raise $633 million and adjust investment in Indonesia’s Excelsior Nickel Cobalt project, a development that could support nickel supply expectations. | Nickel Industries secured a $943m placement after United Tractors agreed to invest in its Indonesia-based nickel production base, with plans for future mining and processing initiatives. Nickel concentrate will be refined at Glencore facilities and feed EV battery production. Hydropower use and planned expansions further support investment sentiment. | Copper fell 0.7% as traders worried about China’s demand outlook and awaited U.S. Fed and inflation data. Falling LME stocks offer some support, but expectations of elevated rates and China uncertainty weigh on copper and aluminium. | Canada Nickel said it has improved in-process tailings technology to capture carbon during ore processing at its Crawford nickel project, potentially lowering capital and operating costs and qualifying for refundable carbon-capture tax credits. Copper and other base metals in London climbed after China cut a key borrowing rate for the first time in 10 months to support growth. Traders expected improved industrial demand, while a weaker dollar also made greenback-priced metals cheaper for non-dollar buyers. LME aluminium, nickel, and copper rose modestly. | Copper prices rose after China cut borrowing costs for the first time in 10 months, supporting growth expectations and risk sentiment. No production or supply changes were stated. | Premium Nickel Resources announced CAD$33.99m of financing (equity and a CAD$15m term loan) to fund prepayments and exploration/resource development at the Selebi and Selkirk Ni-Cu-Co sulphide mines in Botswana. Shanghai copper rallied to a seven-week high as China’s demand outlook improved following a central bank rate cut and as tight supply conditions emerged. LME aluminium and nickel also rose. | SMM’s daily comments say base metals rose across SHFE and mostly higher on LME. The company is starting a second hole 60 metres along strike, targeting 250 metres depth. | Mink Ventures agreed to acquire a 100% stake in US Copper’s Warren copper-nickel project in Ontario via an option. The deal expands battery-metals exploration exposure but does not imply near-term production changes. | Mink Ventures agreed to acquire a 100% stake (via an option) in the drill-ready Warren copper-nickel project near Ontario. The press release describes claim ownership and related transaction terms, but provides no near-term production or market-supply impact details for nickel.

Golden Mile Resources announced “spectacular” nickel intersections at its Quicksilver project in Western Australia, including 49m at 1.74% nickel from 30m depth and higher-grade intervals. The company also noted potential for direct shipping from higher-grade zones. The update is sentimentally supportive for nickel markets. | Power Nickel reported new drill results from the Nisk project, including multiple intersections of higher-grade nickel (and accompanying copper) over drill intervals, supporting resource expansion efforts ahead of an NI 43-101 resource estimate expected in late Q3. The company also plans further geophysics and a funded drilling program. | Red floods and river overflow in Kolaka district (Southeast Sulawesi) have inundated hundreds of hectares of rice fields near a giant Indonesia nickel mine area. Damage is reported, but no direct output disruption is stated. | Merdeka Copper Gold says a drop in coal prices should lower electricity and nickel processing costs this year, improving profitability. It also notes high nickel ore costs may ease if nickel prices fall. The immediate market implication is better nickel production economics due to cheaper energy inputs. | Merdeka Copper Gold said a drop in coal prices should reduce nickel processing costs this year by cutting the cost of electricity used in its rotary kiln electric furnace. It expects easing pressure from high nickel ore costs and better profitability as input costs improve. | Canadian North Resources restarted summer diamond drilling at its Ferguson Lake project in Nunavut after a short pause, continuing a planned 20,000-meter program targeting near-surface Ni-Cu-Co-Pd-Pt massive sulphides and PGE disseminated mineralization. Assay results are pending, and there are no operational or supply changes. | Copper rallied to a five-week high on a weaker dollar and hopes that China will add economic stimulus after disappointing May output data. Copper hovered near a five-week high and was set for a third weekly gain as the dollar weakened and investors bet on additional China stimulus. Better U.S. retail-sales data boosted sentiment. The London Metal Exchange is defending itself in court against lawsuits claiming it unlawfully cancelled nickel trades during a March 8, 2022 price surge that led to a trading suspension. LME says actions were lawful to prevent clearing-house defaults. The dispute may influence market sentiment, but no new nickel fundamentals are cited. | London copper fell after a three-week rally as a firmer U.S. dollar reduced demand and data showed higher China refined copper production and rising LME inventories. Copper prices fell on concerns about the lack of clear economic stimulus in China, alongside a firmer U.S. dollar and thin trading during a U.S. holiday. Falling Shanghai Futures Exchange-monitored stocks provided limited support. Aluminium and nickel also dropped on the same day’s demand sentiment. | Reuters reports that Elliott Associates and Jane Street Global Trading are suing the LME over the cancellation of nickel trades on March 8, 2023 after nickel’s benchmark price doubled within hours. They seek $472 million in damages, arguing the exchange had less disruptive options to manage margin calls and market disorder. | Copper prices slipped on Tuesday as disappointing disappointment with China’s rate cuts hurt industrial-metal sentiment. The softer U.S. dollar provided some support, but copper still fell on the LME. Aluminium and nickel were also down, reflecting weaker broad risk appetite ahead of upcoming Chinese data and technical levels. | The piece’s headline claims LME trading “chaos” in nickel following the lifting of circuit breakers. Such actions usually reflect stressed market conditions and can amplify volatility, with a risk of short-term bearish price pressure as participants react to unstable liquidity and repricing. | Centaurus Metals agreed with Vale Base Metals to acquire offtake rights for the Jaguar nickel sulphide project in Brazil. Vale will waive its rights in exchange for higher net operating royalties linked to nickel sulphate and nickel concentrate. Centaurus cites rising battery-material demand and expects improved control over offtake options, mainly affecting nickel, with no clear immediate supply impact. | Indonesia’s President Joko Widodo said the country will stop exporting copper concentrates in 2024 once major domestic smelters (Freeport Indonesia and Amman Mineral) begin processing, targeting domestic cathode production. Indonesia recently allowed continued concentrate exports until smelter startups, implying near-term tightening for copper supply flows. | MTB Metals has begun field work to prepare drilling at its Telegraph porphyry copper-gold project in British Columbia’s Golden Triangle, using geophysics, hyperspectral analysis, and on-site XRF to rank targets. The update is exploratory and does not indicate production disruptions or supply changes, implying limited near-term price impact. | Australia’s Alliance Nickel says its NiWest nickel-cobalt project in Western Australia is positioned to meet EV battery demand. The report highlights high-grade resources, improving drilling and planning toward production, and offtake agreements (including Stellantis) that support future nickel sulfate sales, though financing remains a hurdle. | A London High Court hearing revisits the LME’s decision to cancel $12 billion of nickel trades on March 8, 2022 during extreme price volatility tied to a Tsingshan-related squeeze. Plaintiffs claim the cancellations unlawfully protected specific participants; LME cites margin and systemic-risk prevention.

The LME faces major legal claims over cancelling nickel trades after prices surged about 250% in two days in March 2022. The exchange froze billions of dollars in trades and shut nickel for over a week, citing margin and systemic risk. Ongoing legal and regulatory scrutiny may weigh on nickel sentiment. | Mallee Resources reports that clay-like minerals disrupted ore processing at its Avebury nickel mine, reducing concentrate production rates in April and May and causing downtime in the tertiary crusher. Repairs are complete and June processing is at or above targeted concentrate output. Additional filtration capacity is planned to sustain nameplate production. | POSCO plans to build large-scale nickel and nickel-precursor factories in South Korea together with China’s CNGR, with a stated investment scale of roughly 1.5 trillion won. The project signals expanded nickel-precursor supply and processing capacity, which can be supportive for near-term nickel market sentiment. | A Reuters report says a lawyer told London court that the LME had no senior management monitoring during Asian hours when nickel prices surged, before cancelling billions of dollars of trades after nickel doubled within hours in March 2022. Hedge funds seek large damages, highlighting disputed LME risk and oversight actions tied to nickel volatility. | Indonesia plans to halt exports of copper concentrates in 2024 but has advised Freeport Indonesia and Amman Mineral to continue shipping until their new smelters start in May. With Indonesia also producing large volumes of nickel and expanding processing for batteries, the move suggests near-term tightening for copper and nickel feed, supporting prices. | Reuters details a legal case involving the LME’s cancellation of nickel trades on March 8, 2022 after nickel prices surged above $100,000/t. Elliott and Jane Street sue for damages, while the LME argues margin-call risks. The piece explains margin mechanics and default handling at LME Clear. | Talon Metals applied to the Minnesota Department of Natural Resources for an environmental review of its proposed Tamarack underground nickel mine. The project targets boosting U.S. nickel supply for battery use, reducing reliance on China and Russia. Tesla has an initial U.S. nickel supply deal with Talon. | Reuters reports Elliott Associates and Jane Street suing the LME over its March 2022 cancellation of nickel trades during a spike above $100,000/t. The piece details how LME Clear margin calls and defaults work, including variation margin timing and member/clearing default procedures, framing the cause of the nickel benchmark disruption. | Minnesota’s DNR is reviewing Talon Metals’ proposed Tamarack Nickel Mine, an underground project that would produce nickel potentially used in Tesla battery supply under an MoU. The mine would transport ore to North Dakota for processing. The proposal still requires an Environmental Impact Statement. | Talon Metals submitted an Environmental Assessment Worksheet to start Minnesota’s environmental review for the Tamarack underground nickel mine, including mine design changes and planned rail loading and water treatment. The project is linked to domestic battery supply inputs. The news is early-stage permitting, offering limited immediate supply impact. | Talon Metals filed with Minnesota regulators to start an environmental review for its proposed underground nickel mine, aimed at supplying nickel for Tesla battery production. The project also received a DOE grant for an ore processing plant in North Dakota. Opposition cites possible water and community impacts, adding timeline uncertainty.

Copper prices eased in thin Chinese holiday trade as China had no fresh stimulus news and its market was closed for June 22-23. Reuters notes 3-month copper down while aluminium and nickel were slightly higher, with traders citing disappointment versus expectations and some support from a weaker dollar. | Reuters reports accelerating adoption of lithium iron phosphate (LFP) EV batteries as automakers target lower-cost vehicles. LFP is cheaper than nickel- and cobalt-based chemistries, and new U.S. production commitments support supply diversification. The shift implies reduced future need for nickel inputs, weighing on nickel demand sentiment. | Copper prices rose in tepid Chinese holiday trade as a weaker dollar and falling LME inventories supported tight supply. Three-month copper gained on the London Metal Exchange. Aluminium and nickel were also higher, reflecting broadly supportive currency and inventory dynamics despite concerns over insufficient Chinese stimulus. | Reuters discusses growing adoption of lithium iron phosphate (LFP) for EV batteries as automakers seek cheaper options. The piece notes LFP is less expensive than cobalt and nickel and can be sourced in North America with lower logistics costs, implying potential substitution away from nickel-linked battery demand. | A London court heard that the LME did not investigate Tsingshan’s large nickel positions before the March 2022 nickel surge and subsequent cancellation of billions of dollars of trades. LME says voiding deals prevented defaults and systemic risk, while claimants seek $472 million in damages. | Minnesota’s DNR is advancing the first phase of the EIS process for the proposed Tamarack Nickel Mine by Talon Metals, with ore processed in North Dakota. The project remains pre-permit and not yet producing. Because production timing is uncertain, it is unlikely to move nearby nickel prices materially. | Canada Nickel announced encouraging initial metallurgical test results from its Texmont project, showing high nickel recoveries (79–84%) and high-grade nickel concentrates (18–28%), alongside strong cobalt recoveries. The company also consolidated additional claims in the Texmont region and plans further metallurgical testing toward a PEA later in 2023. | Indonesia’s President said the country will stop exporting copper concentrates in 2024 once Freeport Indonesia and Amman Mineral complete smelters and begin local processing. Current guidance allows exports until their new smelters operational timing, reducing near-term copper concentrate availability and supporting copper prices. | Copper hit a two-month peak as LME-registered warehouse inventories fell sharply to the lowest since 2021 and cancelled warrants reduced available material. The move was reinforced by the dollar near a one-month low, improving commodity appeal. China inventories also declined. Aluminium and nickel rose modestly alongside copper. | The piece recounts the events around March 7, when nickel prices surged sharply and the market later “broke,” leading LME CEO Matthew Chamberlain to write a late-night memo to the UK financial regulator. It focuses on the nickel market’s extreme volatility, but the excerpt doesn’t show the net direction after the break. | Elliott and Jane Street seek damages totaling about $471.7 million, claiming the LME unlawfully cancelled nickel trades on March 8, 2022 when nickel prices surged above $100,000/mt. The LME argues cancellations prevented systemic risk from $19.7 billion margin calls, and disputes Elliott’s loss calculations and reference pricing. | Talon Metals filed for an environmental review to start its proposed Tamarack underground nickel mine, tied to EV-battery supply plans with Tesla and supported by a U.S. DOE grant for processing. The review faces environmental concerns and parallels delays to other Minnesota copper-nickel projects, making near-term supply impact uncertain.

Reuters reports London copper is on track for its first weekly fall in four, with the three-month contract down around 0.5% after a firmer dollar and hawkish central-bank signals boosted recession fears. Nickel and aluminium are also flagged for their biggest weekly declines, reflecting broader base-metal pressure. | Copper was set for its first weekly decline in four weeks as the dollar jumped, making dollar-priced metals costlier. LME three-month copper fell, while nickel also dropped and aluminium slid. The moves were driven by hawkish central-bank comments, risk aversion, and weak China consumer stimulus expectations post-COVID lockdown slowdown. | The article’s title says the LME faced a “death spiral,” with up to a dozen firms at risk of default during a period of “nickel chaos.” Counterparty stress and reduced market confidence can worsen liquidity and elevate risk aversion, weighing on short-term nickel pricing expectations. | Exchanges and funds are awaiting a court decision in a $472 million lawsuit against the LME over the cancellation of $12 billion in nickel trades following the March 2022 nickel surge. Outcomes could shape confidence in London’s market governance and crisis actions, keeping nickel sentiment volatile until a ruling in at least a month. | Copper prices are set for a weekly drop as hawkish rate-hike expectations and a stronger U.S. dollar weigh on growth-sensitive commodities. LME stocks keep falling, but price weakness dominates. Aluminium and nickel are also down on the week, indicating broad risk-off pressure across industrial metals. | Kosovo’s Newco Ferronikeli ferronickel plant restarted June 21 after a 20-month closure caused by soaring European energy prices. The facility had been idle since Oct 2021, with partial wage payments during downtime. The restart boosts near-term nickel production and exporter activity, though environmental concerns remain.

Amarc Resources and Freeport launched a 2023 airborne and ground exploration program across the JOY Cu-Au district in British Columbia, including surveys and road/bridge rehabilitation for planned drilling. The release is copper-specific but exploration-only, with no production changes, so any short-term impact on metal prices is likely small. | Class 1 Nickel reports completion of soil and biogeochemical sampling at its River Valley PGE project near Sudbury to define drill targets for platinum-group elements. The geology described includes contact-style PGE-Cu-Ni sulphide mineralization, but the release contains no production or supply impact that could move nickel prices soon. | Copper rose 0.4% on the LME as declining warehouse stocks and rain-impacted mining in Chile offset a strong dollar and growth concerns. The cash-to-3-month premium increased on tighter availability. Aluminium and nickel fell with other base metals on disappointing macro data, including weaker manufacturing and growth outlooks.

Copper prices rose as low supplies outweighed tepid demand concerns. The LME cash premium to the three-month contract widened, while on-warrant copper inventories fell to the lowest level since Oct 2021. Chinese SHFE and bonded inventories also declined. Aluminium and nickel rose too, suggesting broader support.

LG Chem began mass production in Korea of single-crystal high-nickel cathodes for next-generation batteries, with first shipments to clients in July. Production is set to expand to additional capacity (Gumi plant) by 2027 and exceed 50,000 tons annually. Cathodes rely on nickel, boosting demand outlook. | A blaze at Indonesia’s Gunbuster Nickel Industry (GNI) smelter in Central Sulawesi killed one worker and injured six. The 1.8 million tonne-per-year plant has faced prior deadly fires and riots. Indonesia’s push for downstream nickel continues, but safety scrutiny and disruption risk could affect near-term nickel supply. | Canada Nickel’s June 2023 investor presentation reviews the Crawford nickel sulphide project, including a preliminary economic assessment, permitting progress, and expected next study milestones (feasibility later in 2023). By emphasizing potential future supply outside Indonesia/China, it can support sentiment for nickel prices, though details are forward-looking. | Nickel 28 reported Q1 results for its Ramu nickel-cobalt joint venture, including 9,016 tonnes of contained nickel production and 7,914 tonnes sold. It also noted actual cash costs net of by-product sales fell 32% to US$3.12/lb, with a strong cash balance and distributions to the company.

A fire at Indonesia’s Gunbuster Nickel Industry (GNI) smelter in Morowali killed one worker and injured six others. The statement says an investigation is underway. The plant is a major nickel smelting operation, and repeated incidents signal potential disruption risk for near-term nickel supply. | A fire at Indonesia’s Gunbuster Nickel Industry (GNI) nickel smelter in Central Sulawesi killed one worker and injured six. The incident at the 1.8 million tonnes/year facility owned by Jiangsu Delong Nickel Industry raises concerns about safety and could disrupt nickel output in the near term. | Copper prices fell as a firmer dollar after Fed comments made dollar-priced metals costlier and tepid China data dampened demand expectations. LME aluminium was slightly lower and SHFE nickel fell, reflecting broader risk- and demand-sensitive selling across industrial metals. | Copper slid as weak China data hurt the demand outlook and a stronger U.S. dollar made dollar-priced metals pricier. Reuters also notes LME aluminium fell and SHFE nickel dropped amid the same macro/fx pressures. Powell’s comments kept rate-hike expectations elevated, weighing on base metals sentiment. | Copper prices fell as weak China economic indicators hurt the demand outlook, while a stronger US dollar made dollar-priced metals costlier. Powell’s comments on possible additional rate hikes reinforced rate-hike expectations. LME aluminium also declined, while nickel was mixed, reflecting broader macro-driven pressure across base metals. | Copper hit a one-month low as hawkish Fed/rate-hike prospects strengthened the dollar and raised growth-demand fears. Weak China factory activity and very low LME exchange stocks (15-year lows) heighten volatility. Aluminium fell ~1% on lower LME inventories; nickel remained weak after an 11-month low. | The IMF, in its latest Indonesia country report, urges Jakarta to phase out mineral export restrictions and avoid extending them to other commodities. The stance targets Indonesia’s current nickel export ban policy and warns that unilateral export measures can destabilize trade, investment, and growth—potentially easing nickel supply expectations. | Copper prices fell to a one-month low as hawkish Fed messaging raised rate-hike expectations, strengthening the dollar and pressuring growth sentiment. Weak China demand signals and very low LME copper inventories reduced market buffering, increasing volatility. Aluminium also dropped on thinner LME stocks, while nickel weakened alongside broader base-metal risk. | Nickel prices soften as Indonesia increases output, supporting a growing surplus in the global nickel market. While prices remain elevated versus pre-pandemic levels due to EV battery demand, the article notes Indonesia’s stepped-up production and forecasting of sustained surplus that likely weighs on near-term prices. | The article says the UK regulator (FCA) was largely passive during the run-up to the LME’s 2022 nickel crisis, including March 7 when nickel surged after a missed margin call. It recounts regulatory questions sent later and references a legal dispute with traders.

Copper rose on Friday amid hopes of Chinese stimulus, but remained set for its biggest quarterly decline since Sept 2022 due to weak Chinese data and expectations of further U.S. rate hikes. Aluminium and nickel showed smaller day gains but were still down on the quarter/year, reflecting demand worries and firm dollar. | Stellantis and Kuniko signed a binding offtake term sheet securing 35% of future annual production of nickel sulphate and cobalt sulphate for nine years, alongside a €5m equity investment. Funds support Norwegian battery metals exploration including nickel and copper, potentially strengthening near-term demand expectations for nickel. | The IMF urged Indonesia to phase out mineral export bans and avoid extending restrictions to other commodities, warning that trade uncertainty and unilateral measures can weigh on investment. Indonesia is considering downstream value-addition; the prior nickel ore ban and bauxite ban are central. This guidance could increase expectations of more raw-material supply. | China’s nickel sulfate exports have risen sharply in April and May as traders exploit wider price gaps versus Japan and South Korea. The piece highlights cost-driven competition between China sulfate and ex-China MHP-based production and notes regulatory/geopolitical constraints. Premium nickel sulfate prices show mixed recovery, supporting nickel-positive sentiment. | Copper is set for its first quarterly decline in three quarters as gloomy China demand prospects and worsening factory activity weigh on prices. Inventories in Shanghai rose sharply. The piece also notes aluminium and nickel falling on the same day, pointing to broader bearish sentiment. | Stellantis signed an offtake term sheet with Kuniko to purchase battery-grade nickel and cobalt sulphate tied to Kuniko’s Norwegian exploration projects, committing to acquire 35% of output under a binding nine-year arrangement. Stellantis also plans a EUR 5m equity investment, supporting project advancement. | LG Chem has started mass production of single-crystal high-nickel cathodes for next-generation batteries, with initial shipments planned from July and production expansion at Gumi by 2027. Because these cathodes use high nickel content and are intended for upcoming EV battery formats, the news is supportive for nickel demand. | Stellantis has bought a nearly 20% stake in Norway’s Kuniko, a nickel and cobalt supplier, and agreed on nine years of future production offtake tied to its Norwegian exploration projects. Stellantis will invest about $5.5 million to fund further battery-metals development, reinforcing EV supply-chain demand signals.

Mallee Resources updated its resource estimate for the Avebury Nickel Mine near Zeehan and identified an exploration target within the mining envelope that could potentially double the current resource. The company says the target remains open along strike and at depth, implying longer-lived nickel production potential. | Copper rose on LME and SHFE as investor sentiment improved on hopes for China stimulus after weaker-than-expected factory contraction. However, the article flags increased supplies and subdued demand, with falling operation rates for copper users. Aluminium and nickel also edged up, reflecting the broader tone but not distinct catalysts. | Copper firmed as hopes for Chinese stimulus rose amid slowing industrial activity, while LME-registered inventories fell 30% since June 6 to 69,700 tons. Cancelled warrants at 50% and warrant-led tightness boosted cash premiums. Other industrial metals were weaker, with aluminium, zinc and nickel down. | The article provides daily updates on SHFE base metals and stainless steel warrants, including a July snapshot. It highlights that nickel is at critically low levels on SHFE, indicating tighter near-term availability. Such inventory constraints can raise market expectations and provide upward price pressure for nickel.

Copper prices eased as the U.S. dollar firmed and China’s June factory activity slowed, signaling weaker demand. Low copper inventories provided some support, but LME copper fell and LME aluminium and nickel also slipped, reflecting cautious near-term sentiment tied to macro data. | Midland Exploration, with Rio Tinto, resumes drilling at Quebec’s Tête Nord project to test new geophysical targets near the Santos nickel-copper discovery and the Palmeiras and Savane showings. The company reports prior drill intersections with Ni-Cu grades and schedules additional holes, subject to local forest-fire conditions. | Midland Exploration, partnering with Rio Tinto, resumed drilling on the Tete Nord project near the Santos nickel-copper discovery and identified additional gravity/geophysical targets. Drilling is planned in early July. | Midland Exploration, partnered with Rio Tinto, plans to resume drilling on the Santos nickel-copper zone at the Tete Nord project in Quebec, adding six holes totaling up to 1,900 metres. LME and SHFE copper stocks declined sharply, suggesting tighter supply, but the demand outlook dominated. Aluminium edged lower and nickel retreated as macro headwinds persisted. | Indonesia’s Corruption Eradication Commission is investigating alleged illegal nickel ore exports that violate the country’s nickel export ban, after a mismatch between Indonesian and Chinese shipment data. Indonesia’s anti-graft agency is investigating alleged illegal exports of nickel ore that bypass the country’s export ban, with China reportedly receiving large volumes despite restrictions. Experts cite a big domestic-versus-international price gap and argue the policy failed, suggesting supervision changes or replacing the ban with tariffs. | Wyloo Metals, led by Andrew Forrest, is buying Mincor Resources for $760m and focusing on “clean” nickel sulphides for EV battery supply. It highlights sustainability-driven avoidance of laterite deep-sea tailings and considers a Kwinana processing plant. The moves imply supportive expectations for battery-grade nickel demand and supply. | Wyloo has crossed 90% ownership in Mincor and expects to complete the acquisition soon, attracted by the Cassini nickel sulphide deposit in Western Australia’s Kambalda district. Wyloo says nickel is its main focus, expects to build on Mincor’s mine life, and links resources to planned battery-chemicals processing. | Ardea Resources agreed with Japanese firms (Sumitomo Metal Mining, Mitsubishi, Mitsui) to progress feasibility and final investment planning for the Kalgoorlie Nickel Project Goongarrie Hub. The company projects A$3.18bn capex and long-run output of ~30,000 tonnes nickel annually. It supports Japan’s push for battery-mineral supply. | Wyloo, controlled by Andrew and Nicola Forrest’s investment group, raised its stake in ASX-listed nickel miner Mincor to about 90.87% and will compulsorily acquire remaining shares at $1.40 each. Mincor’s Kambalda project produces nickel sulphides used in battery demand, but no near-term output changes are described. | Nordic Resources pitches its Pulju project in Finland as a major future supplier of battery-grade nickel, citing an updated JORC resource for the Hotinvaara prospect and proximity to other Cu-Ni sulphide deposits. The EU’s Critical Raw Materials Act names nickel as strategic, boosting near-term sentiment for nickel. | Copper fell on the LME alongside other base metals as global PMI data signaled weaker growth and services activity, dimming near-term demand for industrial metals. Citi warned metals consumption will remain under pressure. The stronger dollar added further pressure. Aluminium and nickel also declined. | Wyloo Metals agreed to buy 90.87% of Mincor Resources for A$760m, adding Mincor’s mainly producing nickel sulphide assets in Western Australia. Management frames the move as building an integrated nickel business, including plans for downstream processing for battery cathode materials in Kwinana. The acquisition is broadly supportive for nickel sentiment. | Mink Ventures announced it is issuing shares and warrants to commence an option on the Warren copper-nickel project in Ontario. The drill-ready property has historical Cu and Ni mineralized zones (A, B, C) and plans for a summer diamond program, but no production or definitive new supply is stated.

Mink Ventures announced it has issued shares and warrants to commence an option for the Warren copper-nickel project in Ontario. The company describes drill readiness, identified Cu-Ni mineralized zones, and plans for upcoming exploration expenditures. Historical assays and grades are cited, but no production or near-term supply shift is indicated. | SMM reports mixed overnight moves in copper: LME copper fell 0.44% while SHFE rose slightly; spot premiums dropped on low-priced importer shipments in east China and weak downstream demand, with inventories in south China falling due to lack of arrivals. Macro sentiment tied to US rates/ USD dominated short-term direction. Lead also fell on a stronger USD. | SMM reports nickel prices are under pressure after minutes from the US Fed’s June meeting signaled further tightening. In China, domestic nickel spot premiums declined and high prices curbed purchasing interest. SMM expects nickel to move sideways but remain under downward pressure. | The piece highlights heightened activity around Canadian nickel projects, citing EV/battery demand and reporting drilling and borehole electromagnetics results from Fathom Nickel’s Gochager Lake. While this supports expectations for future nickel supply, it is primarily exploration-focused and not a clear immediate market-wide supply or inventory shock. | Copper prices fell in London and Shanghai on a firmer U.S. dollar and hawkish Fed expectations for further rate hikes. LME aluminium eased marginally while nickel rose. Chinese smelters raised copper concentrate treatment charges, citing abundant supply and flat demand growth, but the macro factor pressured copper. | Copper is set for a third straight session of declines as a firmer U.S. dollar and weak global economic data weigh on demand expectations. Reuters reports LME copper down 0.3% and SHFE copper down 0.2%. Aluminium slips slightly, while nickel is mixed amid China-related policy and supply factors. | Avante Mining reports re-sampling results from historic core at its TL Nickel Project, with nickel assays up to 1.85% and copper assays up to ~1.84%. The company says the grades were verified and has received permits, beginning mobilization for an imminent drilling program. No current production volumes are mentioned. | Indonesia imposed a June 2023 ban on bauxite ore exports to force development of domestic processing/refining. Tightening bauxite-to-alumina logistics can support aluminium input costs. The piece also links Indonesia’s earlier nickel ore export ban to rapid downstream growth, affecting nickel supply expectations and supporting prices. | Pan Global reports visible copper and tin mineralization from initial holes at the Romana West target of Spain’s Escacena project, with IP geophysics expanding the chargeability anomaly westward. A 25-to-30 hole campaign is underway and assays are pending, supporting continued copper mineralization potential. | Pan Global Resources announced a 10,000-meter 2026 drill program at Spain’s Escacena and Cármenes projects. Escacena targets copper-tin-silver resource growth and new discoveries, while Cármenes targets copper-nickel-cobalt (with follow-up drilling after a 2025 gold-copper-nickel-cobalt discovery). No production change is announced. | Erdene reports expanded mineralization from a 12-hole program at its Zuun Mod molybdenum-copper porphyry project in Mongolia, including long Mo and Cu intervals and copper anomalism north of the deposit. While it may support longer-term copper supply expectations, there are no near-term production changes. | Horizonte Minerals received environmental approval from the Para State Secretariat to begin mining at its wholly owned Araguaia nickel project in Brazil. The permit allows initial work to establish ore stockpiles ahead of commissioning and plant start-up next year, reducing commissioning delays and supporting nickel supply expectations. | Horizonte Minerals received environmental approval from Brazil’s Pará state authority to begin mining at its Araguaia nickel project. The permit enables ore stockpiling and commissioning preparation before plant start-up next year. The project’s planned output of ferronickel and nickel adds to near-term supply expectations for nickel. | Norilsk Nickel said it has made “certain progress” selling accumulated metal reserves built up in 2022, according to Interfax (via Reuters). The report implies additional near-term supply reaching the market, which is most relevant for nickel given the company’s core output, though figures and delivery timing are not specified.

A report says China’s rapid expansion into Indonesia’s nickel operations has given it dominant control of nickel needed for EV batteries in the U.S. The Inflation Reduction Act’s mineral-sourcing requirements may exclude Indonesia and invite scrutiny of Chinese firms’ involvement, weakening U.S. supply prospects and near-term nickel demand outlook. | London copper rebounded 0.3% as the dollar eased, but gains were capped by uncertainty over Chinese demand and expectations for further U.S. Fed rate hikes. LME aluminium rose 0.6% and nickel edged up slightly, reflecting mixed sentiment across metals tied to broader risk assets. | BofA reduced its nickel price forecast for 2023 to $22,063/t from $23,222/t previously, while keeping the 2024 forecast unchanged at $21,250/t. The downgrade signals softer expected nickel market conditions, which can weigh on near-term nickel price expectations. | Copper edged up but remained in a tight range as investors weighed U.S. Fed rate expectations and a pending jobs report against China demand outlook. China’s property and new-energy activity supported demand, while expanding domestic supply from smelters and rising aluminium output capped gains. Aluminium and nickel also saw modest moves. | Indonesia’s ministry is probing allegations that nickel ore continued to be exported to China despite a ban since 2020. China imported 5.56 million tons between 2020 and May 2023, prompting investigations into misclassification and possible illegal shipments. Any crackdown could tighten nickel ore supply and support nickel prices. | Indonesia is investigating alleged illegal exports of nickel ore worth 14.5 trillion rupiah to China dating back to early 2020. Authorities say 5.3 million mt of ore were illegally shipped, causing estimated losses of royalties and export taxes. Possible enforcement tightening could reduce near-term nickel ore flows to China. | BofA Global Research lowered its commodity price forecasts, including copper and nickel. Copper forecasts for 2023 and 2024 were reduced versus prior estimates, and nickel’s 2023 forecast was also cut, while 2024 stayed unchanged. The revisions point to weaker expected market conditions for these metals. | BofA Global Research lowered its price forecasts for several commodities. Copper forecasts were reduced for 2023 and 2024, and nickel’s 2023 forecast was also cut (2024 unchanged). The changes suggest weaker expected prices, but the impact is mainly forecast-level. | A London court set a July 12 deadline for Prateek Gupta and related firms to file their defence in Trafigura’s nickel cargo fraud case. Trafigura alleged some 25,000 tonnes were not high-grade nickel, with early containers found to contain carbon steel, and booked a 590-million impairment.

The report describes nickel mining in Indonesia near Labengki Island, where residents say mine waste enters coastal waters during the rainy season, causing sediment damage and harming fishing. While officials argue licensed firms manage waste, they admit supervision gaps and ongoing illegal mining, raising risk of enforcement and supply disruptions. | London copper slipped after China inflation data missed expectations, deepening demand worries. The dollar firmed on expectations for further Fed rate hikes. While low inventories and potential supply disruptions supported prices, Chilean output fell and Peruvian miners face red tape, limiting losses. Nickel fell; aluminium rose slightly. | London copper fell 0.5% as disappointing China inflation data signaled slower recovery in the top metal consumer, weighing on demand expectations. Softer global economic sentiment added pressure. Despite supply issues and low inventories, copper inventories declined. Nickel also edged down, while aluminium was largely unchanged. | Palladium One announced receipt of a Class 1 exploration permit and started site work, including clearing access trails and planning an EM survey at its Canalask nickel-copper project in Yukon. Reuters reports LME aluminium rising 1.5% to about $2,179/ton, driven by a weaker U.S. dollar and hopes for stronger Chinese demand. Beijing’s extension of property-market support underpins base metals sentiment, while investors await U.S. inflation and Chinese trade data. | Canada Nickel announced drill assay results from its Midlothian property, reporting long mineralized intervals with ~0.26–0.29% nickel and dominant nickel-bearing minerals (awaruite/heazlewoodite). Mineralogy also shows high brucite content linked to its carbon storage process. The update is sentiment-positive for nickel supply expectations. | CanAlaska signed a LOI with Valterra to allow Valterra to earn up to an 80% interest in four North Thompson Nickel Belt projects in Manitoba. The deal includes staged cash, share issuance, and exploration expenditures near Vale’s Thompson nickel operations, with a joint venture after earn-in milestones. | Valterra signed a non-binding letter of intent to option mineral exploration licenses in Manitoba’s Thompson Nickel Belt, adjacent to the Thompson Nickel Mine. The company plans geophysics and diamond drilling and may earn up to 80% interest, with additional payments after a feasibility study. It targets nickel sulphide deposits.

Québec Nickel reports drill results from the Ducros Sill showing long intersections of ~0.19–0.21% nickel with associated cobalt, confirming a large ultramafic intrusion extent. It has invested in multiple Indonesian high-nickel matte projects and recently signed a JV for laterite nickel ore smelting/hydrometallurgy producing nickel raw materials. | Green River Gold reported assay results from two drill holes at its Quesnel Nickel Project, confirming consistent near-surface nickel mineralization along the Deep Purple anomaly. Copper prices fell to the lowest since early July and were set for the biggest weekly decline in three months as China’s post-pandemic recovery looked sluggish. Weak lending/TSF data and property worries hurt demand expectations. Inventories rose on SHFE and LME, with nickel and aluminium also hitting multi-week lows. | A report on Sudbury’s economy says high nickel demand—linked to electric-vehicle batteries and sustained by expanding North American supply constraints—should continue supporting the local minerals market. It highlights nickel’s strong price performance and Vale’s investment and production allocation toward EVs, suggesting bullish nickel sentiment.

TELF AG reports declining premiums for EU full-plate nickel cathodes, driven by weakened demand and sluggish market activity. Participants are adopting a cautious stance, resulting in fewer transactions and lower trade volumes, with additional impact from a summer lull reducing engagement and inventory management in the EU nickel market. | The piece focuses on the EU’s opposition to Indonesia’s ban on exporting raw nickel and the dispute that is moving into a new trade-war phase. It notes increased Indonesian nickel refining capacity and higher refined nickel exports, implying tighter raw-material flows and potential support for nickel prices. | SMM reports China refined nickel output at 21,500 mt in July, up MoM and YoY, supported by strong electrowinning margins as high nickel prices and lower nickel sulphate costs improved profitability. It forecasts August output around 21,650 mt, as some smelters ramp and electrowinning lines in South China expand. | SMM reports China’s nickel sulphate output rose strongly in July, contributing to oversupply and a price drop amid limited spot inquiries. August production is expected to increase further by about 5% MoM, driven by higher output at integrated ternary cathode precursor producers, likely weighing on nickel prices near term. | Aston Minerals says its recommenced drilling at the Boomerang nickel-cobalt deposit intersected sulphides in all three holes at the B2 zone, extending mineralisation targets at depth and along strike. The program aims to increase nickel resource size/grade and advance metallurgical flowsheet testing, potentially strengthening future supply expectations. | Reuters reports copper prices drifting lower as China property worries intensify, citing concerns over Country Garden’s ability to service debt and a stronger U.S. dollar pressuring dollar-priced metals. Three-month LME copper and September SHFE copper fell. LME aluminium also slipped slightly, while nickel rose modestly. | Philippines’ NCIP issued a cease and desist order against Ipilan Nickel Corporation to stop nickel ore mining at Brooke’s Point, Palawan, citing alleged encroachment on indigenous lands and harm to local livelihoods. The order gives the firm five days to halt operations, creating near-term supply risk for nickel ore. | Indonesia’s government suspended nickel mining in the Mandiodo Block in Southeast Sulawesi amid a graft probe involving permits. The Attorney General’s Office arrested a former minerals official linked to RKAB decree changes that expanded access to nickel ore. The halt reduces near-term nickel output and can pressure prices higher. | Copper prices sank to five-week lows as deteriorating China demand outlook intensified concerns tied to property-sector debt stress. Traders cited Country Garden’s bond issues and weaker Chinese credit data. Aluminium also fell on softer global demand and broader base-metal pressure from a stronger U.S. dollar.

Indonesia’s government defended its downstream nickel development policy, citing a surge in downstream nickel tax and export revenues after enforcement of a 2020 ore export ban. The official said ore exports would have been far lower without the policy, implying tighter, more processed nickel supply and potentially supporting nickel prices. | Copper edged higher from a six-week low after China cut short-term loan and reverse repo rates and the dollar weakened, making dollar-priced metals cheaper. Three-month LME copper rose slightly, while SHFE copper also gained. Gains were limited by ongoing declines in China property investment and home sales. Aluminium and nickel also rose modestly. | Mallee Resources, owner of the Avebury nickel mine, is seeking new funding and has extended a standstill agreement with Hartree Partners to allow refinancing documentation. It also arranged additional equity funding and notes potential concentrate-related payments, aiming to keep the mine operational and output flowing. | Copper prices rebounded after hitting a six-week low, rising on expectations that China’s unexpected second rate cut in three months will improve demand and on a softer dollar. LME/SHFE copper and LME aluminium and nickel also moved higher, though China’s continued property weakness capped gains. | The piece discusses Indonesia’s nickel industry chain, including forecasts for electro-deposited nickel delivery into LME-linked supply, analysis of declining ore grades and high-grade ore life cycles, and policy changes. It also reviews investment in MHP/high-nickel matte projects and follow-up activities that shape near-term nickel supply expectations. | Magna Mining reported additional 2023 drill assay results from its Crean Hill nickel project, including wide high-grade massive sulphide intervals with ~3.0–4.2% nickel and ~0.7–1.4% copper over ~28–32 metres. The company plans further advanced exploration/test mining in 2024, with no near-term production. | Copper, aluminium and nickel prices fell in London after weaker China data signaled worsening demand: slower industrial output growth and declining property investment. LME copper slid to a 6-week low, aluminium hit a recent low as near-term discounts widened, and nickel dropped toward its lowest since 2022 amid cautious growth outlook. | Copper, aluminium and nickel fell on Tuesday after weak China data signaled worsening demand. Slower industrial output growth and declining property investment pressured base-metal sentiment. Copper hit a 6-week low and aluminium touched its weakest in weeks as near-term discounts widened, while nickel slid to its lowest since mid-2022. | The United Steelworkers (USW) signed a memorandum of cooperation with NewRange Copper Nickel LLC, a joint venture between Polymet and Teck. The project holds copper and nickel deposits in Minnesota’s Iron Range. The agreement focuses on worker rights and responsible mining, supporting expectations for advancing domestic supply chains. | The United Steelworkers signed a memorandum of cooperation with NewRange Copper Nickel, a joint venture of Polymet Mining and Teck Resources, to support mining activities for critical minerals in northeastern Minnesota. The deposits include copper and nickel, but the article provides no timing or production change details.

Indonesia’s downstream policy includes a ban on nickel ore exports from Jan. 1, 2020, aimed at boosting value-added production. The article cites rising nickel export values and surpluses, arguing downstreaming has increased export earnings, though broader economic benefits remain limited. | Copper traded near a seven-week low as China’s weaker industrial output, investment and retail sales, plus a continuing property investment slump, raised concerns about demand for industrial metals. Easing supply worries were offset by rising LME copper inventories, while nickel fell and aluminium moved modestly higher amid soft sentiment. | Copper prices extended losses to a seven-week low as disappointing China data weakened demand expectations and metal stocks rose. LME copper fell and LME aluminium edged lower, with nickel mixed across contracts. Analysts cited easing global tightness, including a sharp LME stock build, and persistent property weakness. | Copper prices edged slightly above a seven-week low on Wednesday, supported by a weaker dollar but pressured by disappointing Chinese economic and housing data that raised concerns about demand. Zinc fell on inventory surge. Nickel gained and aluminium dipped modestly, reflecting mixed broader metal sentiment rather than a single dominant driver. | Premium Nickel Resources updates on underground drilling commencement and refurbishment at the Selebi nickel-copper-cobalt mines in Botswana and plans to expand the Selebi mining licence by acquiring adjacent deposits. The work aims to define resources and potentially a PEA, with historic intervals reporting both nickel and copper grades. | Murchison Minerals begins a 2023 summer exploration program at its HPM nickel-copper-cobalt project in Quebec, combining prospecting with a deep ground EM survey to refine targets for future drilling. The firm cites prior high-grade Ni and Cu intercepts, but no immediate production or supply changes are reported. | Murchison Minerals began a 2023 summer exploration program at its Quebec HPM nickel-copper-cobalt project, combining prospecting with a deep ground EM survey to identify sulphide targets at depth. The company cites prior high-grade Ni/Cu intercepts and aims to de-risk future drilling; it is not a near-term supply change.

Copper on the LME rebounded from an 11-week low after China’s central bank injected additional liquidity to support the economy. The dollar’s strength weighed, but the liquidity boost improved sentiment. Aluminium eased, while nickel rose slightly on both LME and SHFE, reflecting mixed but supportive macro conditions. | Copper snapped back from a 2-1/2 month low as China’s central bank injected liquidity and state banks sold dollars to buy yuan, supporting the renminbi. LME three-month copper rose 0.6%. A firmer dollar capped gains. Aluminium and nickel also advanced on the session’s risk/FX-driven move.

Indonesia’s export ban on raw nickel drew Chinese companies to build multiple nickel smelters in Sulawesi, including a major facility by PT Dragon Virtue Nickel Industry. The investment expands processing capacity to secure nickel for battery supply chains, likely boosting near-term nickel availability despite local pollution concerns. | A report on the March 2022 LME nickel blow-up says an LME margining “quirk” allowed some members to net variation-margin gains against initial margin, creating up to $915m in offsets for clients short nickel. It references possible future CCP intervention on VM deductions but provides no new nickel fundamentals. | Merdeka Copper Gold said it is ramping up production, including Wetar Copper Mine output of copper cathodes and expected higher copper volumes in 2H 2023 with added pyrite-ore deliveries for further processing. It also highlights increased nickel conversion/smelting activity at IMIP, supporting growing nickel matte and NPI production. | Japan-aligned consortium progress is highlighted for Ardea’s Goongarrie nickel laterite project in Western Australia. The pre-feasibility indicates production of at least 30,000 tonnes of nickel per year for decades, with a major processing and power plan. Investors are briefed as the project advances toward a definitive decision.

Copper rebounded after three weekly declines as China’s central bank pledged to resolve local government debt risks and cut the one-year lending rate, boosting sentiment. Low inventories also supported prices. LME and SHFE showed small gains in aluminium and nickel as well, reflecting broader risk-on demand expectations. | SMM says nickel prices were mainly driven by macro factors but also supported by concerns over Indonesia nickel ore mine investigations, which firmed nickel ore prices. Inventories of nickel plates rose on overseas arrivals, expected to add supply, while reduced procurement sentiment and higher input costs may limit demand, keeping volatility. | SMM reports Chinese nickel ore port inventories at 7.087 million wmt as of Aug 18, up 256,000 wmt week-on-week. Across seven major ports, inventories rose to 3.637 million wmt, up 266,000 wmt. The buildup occurs during peak shipment season and aligns with expectations, reducing perceived ore tightness. | Copper prices rebounded after a third weekly fall, supported by sharp SHFE stock declines and low inventories plus expectations of autumn demand. However, gains are capped by weak Chinese economic/property data and liquidity risks. China cut the one-year lending rate, adding support, while aluminium and nickel rose modestly. | Posco Future M and MC Group will form a joint venture in the Philippines to process MHP into high-purity nickel sulphate for EV battery cathodes, sourcing nickel ore from MC Group’s Palawan mine. The move expands refined nickel supply linked to cathode production and could bolster near-term nickel demand expectations. | Tsingshan Group began commercial production of refined nickel at its Morowali (Indonesia) plant, with ultimate capacity of 50,000 tonnes, according to three sources. The plant has started making nickel plate and Tsingshan may seek LME deliverable status. Additional Chinese brands are also expected after LME changes. | Wave Nickel says it is pursuing a $440 million Texas refinery to produce nickel sulfate for EV batteries, supported by U.S. incentives. It plans feedstock from a planned Brazilian microwave plant and cites a current nickel-sulfate glut tied to Indonesian supply, suggesting future tightening as projects develop slowly beyond China/Indonesia. | Talon Metals’ shares rose as drilling at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project in Minnesota returned high-grade results, including a long intercept with meaningful nickel and copper grades. The upbeat exploration update can support sentiment for nickel and copper by improving expectations for future supply.

Talon Metals announced drilling results from the Tamarack Nickel Project: 101.71 meters at 3.04% nickel-equivalent, including 1.94% nickel and 1.84% copper. The work, supported by a Columbia ARPA-E grant, also targets novel processing to lower CO2 footprint and increase nickel/copper recovery, improving future supply outlook. | Palladium One reports highly anomalous nickel (up to 785 ppm) and copper (up to 431 ppm) soil geochemistry between the West Pickle and RJ Zones at its Tyko Ni-Cu-Co project, supported by ultramafic mapping and geophysics. The news may modestly improve sentiment on future Ni/Cu supply, though no production change is indicated. | Palladium One reports highly anomalous nickel and copper (plus cobalt) soil results at its Tyko project, linking the West Pickle and RJ zones via interpreted chonolith/feeder dykes. It also states it earned an 80% interest in the Pezim II block. The news supports exploration potential but is not production-related. | Copper prices rose for a fourth straight session, supported by firmer Chinese refined copper demand (ICSG) and additional Chinese monetary support, alongside a weaker US dollar. Reuters also reported LME aluminium and nickel rising that day, indicating a broader, sentiment-driven lift across industrial metals.

Indonesia’s Jokowi and economist Faisal Basri spar over the government’s nickel downstream policy, with Faisal arguing it disproportionately benefits China and Jokowi citing export and tax gains. Neither side announces immediate changes to quotas, tariffs, or production, so near-term nickel price impact is uncertain despite the policy debate. | SHFE 2309 nickel closed higher on Aug 22, but intraday trading and volumes/open interest weakened, suggesting fund outflows. The report links the strong trend mainly to Indonesian nickel ore quota conditions and also cites China’s lower LPR supporting sentiment. Weak spot deals and higher upstream selling point to near-term rangebound trade. | Palladium One announced highly anomalous nickel and copper soil results between the West Pickle and RJ zones at its Tyko project, including values up to 785 ppm Ni and 431 ppm Cu. The company also earned an 80% interest in the Pezim II claim block. No production or supply impact is indicated. | Copper and most base metals rose in London as improved demand prospects from top consumer China—supported by upbeat Chinese copper consumption data and speculation of further government measures—boosted sentiment. A stronger yuan and weaker dollar supported dollar-priced metals. LME aluminium and nickel also increased alongside copper. | Moneta Gold announced it completed transactions to expand its Loveland Nickel property near Timmins, adding claims around the Cominco and Hollinger zones. The release cites historical intercepts and resource grades for nickel and some copper, but provides no development timeline or production affecting near-term metal supply. | Copper rose for a fifth straight session on improved Chinese demand prospects, upbeat consumption data and speculation of further Chinese support measures. Tight LME-monitored stockpiles near a 15-year low also helped. A stronger dollar eased, keeping base metals generally higher, with aluminium and nickel also moving up.

SMM comments on the nickel market: SHFE nickel opened 169,950 yuan/mt and closed 169,080 yuan/mt. Trading volume and open interest fell. The report links Indonesian nickel ore quota policy and China’s lower one-year LPR to upside support, but spot transactions remain depressed with weak downstream demand. | HKEX, which owns the LME, is expected to cover damages in LME nickel lawsuits tied to last year’s cancellation of billions of dollars of nickel trades. Lawyers say clearing members should not bear damage awards or settlements under LME rulebook treatment of non-default losses, per sources and confirmation. | Horizonte Minerals hired Fernando Marino as operations director for its Araguaia nickel project in Brazil, citing his experience commissioning and ramping up mining and nickel/ferronickel operations. The company says Araguaia construction is about 65% complete, signaling potential progress toward future nickel production. | London copper pulled back 0.7% after a five-session rally, driven by unease over high interest rates and weaker global growth expectations, which outweighed tentative demand optimism from China. Technical resistance also limited upside. LME aluminium and nickel fell alongside copper as investors awaited Jackson Hole. | Abaxx Commodities Exchange, getting final Singapore approvals, plans to launch nickel sulphate futures by year-end—aimed at EV battery nickel pricing. It says the contract will provide better price discovery and allow delivery between sellers and buyers. The piece contrasts with current LME/QME refined nickel contracts and recent nickel market volatility. | Lawsuits in London and Singapore allege that a trader purchased a cargo purported to contain nickel but received containers filled with rubble instead. The case follows an earlier large alleged fraud involving Trafigura-linked entities. While not a supply disruption, it raises concerns about physical nickel market integrity. | Nickel Creek Platinum reported a positive pre-feasibility study for its Nickel Shäw Ni-Cu-Co-PGM project in Yukon, with NPV and IRR calculated using nickel and copper price assumptions (US$11/lb and US$4/lb). The update supports development sentiment and potential future supply, but no immediate output changes were announced.

Reuters reports copper fell on Friday as a strong U.S. dollar increased costs for non-dollar buyers. Three-month LME copper dropped and SHFE October copper weakened. It also cites weaker China demand data and shows aluminium and nickel easing on SHFE, indicating broader near-term pressure from FX and macro sentiment. | Reuters reports London/LME copper is set for its biggest weekly gain in four, supported by Chinese policy measures stabilizing the yuan and hopes for stronger consumption in coming months. A firmer dollar caps gains. Aluminium is nearly flat, while nickel edges slightly higher, reflecting limited spillover. | Copper is set for its strongest week in a month after LME prices rise on expectations of a China housing-demand uplift, supportive seasonal consumption, and short-covering. China policy and “no lockdowns” help sentiment. Stocks elsewhere are low, raising volatility risk. Aluminium and nickel also tick higher. | Westwin Elements broke ground on the first U.S. refinery targeting cobalt and nickel in Lawton, Oklahoma, aiming to start construction by October and operations sometime in 2024. Backers cite national security and cite heavy global dependence on Congo and China. The announcement is supply-positive for nickel but largely future-dated.

SMM’s morning note says SHFE nickel fell as Powell indicated the Fed could raise rates again, strengthening the dollar and weighing on commodities. Spot transactions were depressed, and Norilsk nickel was sold at a discount for the first time. Downstream inventory remains high, limiting demand support, so nickel may fluctuate. | Copper prices rose as the dollar weakened ahead of major U.S. data, while China’s stock-market-support measures improved risk sentiment. LME three-month copper gained 0.5% and Shanghai October copper edged up. LME aluminium rose, but nickel fell, indicating mixed effects across metals tied to macro and sentiment. | Vale’s Indonesia unit plans to build HPAL facilities with Huayou and Huali in Malili, targeting 60,000 tonnes of nickel annually in MHP for further battery processing. The project follows related Vale–Huayou HPAL work at Pomalaa and other nickel developments, signaling continued Indonesian processing expansion. | Abaxx Commodities Exchange plans to launch the first physically settled nickel sulphate futures contract. Nickel sulphate is key to EV batteries, and the contract aims to improve price discovery and hedging for supply needed for rising demand. The initiative could bolster confidence in nickel’s battery-linked demand. | Copper prices rose 0.9% after China announced measures to support housing loans and stock trading, improving risk appetite and prompting funds/CTAs to cut short positions. Markets await China PMI data for demand signals. Aluminium also edged higher, while nickel was slightly down, reflecting mixed metal-specific flows. | Sibanye says it remains on the shortlist to buy Zambia’s Mopani Copper Mine assets, in a government process expected to select a bidder by end-July. The company is pursuing a copper portfolio expansion amid weaker recent profits. The article also notes prior nickel asset purchases, but without further near-term specifics. | US trader Kataman sues New Alloys Trading alleging a nickel cargo purchase was replaced with virtually worthless steel waste and that the shipment sat at port from August 2022 to March 2023. Kataman claims it paid $3.3m for LME-branded nickel to be resold. | Sibanye is still among potential buyers for Zambia’s state-owned Mopani Copper Mines, with Zambia aiming to choose a bidder by late July and moving into due diligence and exclusive negotiations. The company’s broader expansion includes nickel assets, but the near-term market focus is copper supply and ownership expectations. | The Philippine Nickel Industry Association urged the government to create a “one-stop shop” to fast-track mining permits, arguing this would reduce regulatory uncertainty and boost investor confidence. It linked timely exploration and extraction permits to the viability of long-lived nickel processing plants. No policy decision or immediate production change was announced.

London copper edged down 0.1% to $8,443/t as traders stayed cautious ahead of key China and U.S. economic data that could steer interest-rate expectations and demand outlook. LME aluminium rose 0.3% while nickel fell 0.2%, indicating mixed, low-magnitude pressure across industrial metals. | Philippine officials and the Nickel Industry Association urge streamlining mining permitting via a potential “one-stop shop,” cutting approval timelines from 5–10 years to 6 months to 1 year. They cite rising nickel ore demand for EV batteries and stable production, seeking regulatory predictability to accelerate nickel development. | London copper slipped as traders took a cautious stance ahead of key U.S. and China data. A softer dollar and China mortgage easing in Guangzhou limited declines. Copper premiums widened on higher London–Shanghai spreads. Nickel fell and aluminium edged up, reflecting mixed macro-driven sentiment rather than a specific metal supply shock. | Indonesia delayed mining quotas and suspended operations at a key Antam site, tightening local nickel ore availability. Smelters began importing low-grade Philippine limonite ore as a stopgap. Local prices rose 8% this week. Philippine ore prices in China jumped, reflecting tighter Indonesian supplies and boosting near-term nickel sentiment. | Copper prices slipped on concerns about weaker global growth and metals demand, with investors reassessing softer U.S. jobs data. Losses were moderated by signs of support for China’s property sector. Aluminium rose while nickel declined, reflecting mixed macro-demand signals across industrial metals. | Abaxx said it aims to launch nickel sulfate futures by year-end after MAS regulatory review. The contract targets EV-relevant nickel sulfate and seeks to bridge the physical market shift away from Class 1 nickel toward intermediates like nickel matte/MHP. Nickel sulfate and its premium were also assessed higher recently.

SMM’s morning comment reports SHFE nickel rising in the Aug 30 night session but with lower volume and falling open interest. Nickel expected to fluctuate. | London copper fell and was set for its first monthly drop since May as disappointing China manufacturing data extended weak growth signals. Tepid consumption outweighed hopes for policy support. A stronger U.S. dollar also weighed. Nickel and aluminium prices edged down/inched higher, but the overall macro tone was negative for metals demand. | SMM outlines nickel’s industry chain, arguing that demand for ternary precursor materials and intermediate products (nickel matte, nickel sulfate, MHP) is increasing, while Philippines nickel ore shipments are less stable seasonally. It also cites Indonesia’s export-ban related policies and expanding integrated capacity as affecting nickel supply and pricing expectations. | Si6 Metals plans a 10,000m drilling program next month at its Botswana Maibele nickel sulphide project, targeting extensions and depth to upgrade the Maibele North resource. Drilling also targets copper-silver mineralisation at Dibete and Airstrip. It may improve future supply expectations for nickel and some copper, but near-term price impact is limited. | Thiess (via contract partner HOCHTIEF) was awarded a A$240 million, 3.5-year nickel mining contract near Central Halmahera, Indonesia. Work includes mine development, haul roads, and ore loading/hauling. The deal expands Thiess’ Indonesian nickel operations and supports expectations of continued nickel output in the region. | Lifezone Metals announced completion of infill drilling at its Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, highlighting multiple high-grade nickel intercepts (e.g., 41 m at 2.07% Ni). The results support an upcoming Mineral Resource estimate and DFS, improving expectations for future nickel supply. Copper is also present in assays. | Copper fell 1.1% after China’s manufacturing PMI contracted for a fifth month, reinforcing demand distress. A stronger U.S. dollar added drag, and LME warehouse stocks rose sharply. Aluminium rose on expectations of robust Chinese demand and low inventories. Nickel was also down, reflecting weaker sentiment. | Lifezone Metals reported strong high-grade infill drilling results from its completed program at the Kabanga nickel project in northwest Tanzania, ahead of the project’s feasibility stage. The update strengthens confidence in the project’s resource potential, which can improve market sentiment for nickel supply prospects in the near term.

Copper prices rose 0.8% early Sept 1 as China issued further measures to revive its crisis-hit property sector, including easing borrowing rules and lowering mortgage rates. The improved demand outlook supported broader industrial metals, with LME aluminium and nickel also up around 0.8-1.1% despite a strong U.S. dollar. | Thiess won a AUS$240 million contract to deliver full-scale mining operations for a nickel project in Central Halmahera, Indonesia, for 3.5 years. The deal strengthens regional operating capacity and indicates ongoing nickel production investment, which can be mildly supportive for nickel price expectations despite limited immediate volume detail. | Copper hit a four-week high as China announced measures to support its crisis-hit property market and manufacturing activity expanded. Analysts attributed the rally to improving demand indicators such as new orders in PMIs and expectations of metals restocking as construction resumes. Aluminium and nickel also rose alongside copper. | Aurubis says it may face large losses after discovering discrepancies in its metals inventory tied to manipulated invoices for shipments that never reached its site. The affected inputs include copper and precious metals. The firm has downgraded forecasts and is investigating potential criminal activity, raising near-term risk perception in metals markets. | The piece argues nickel remains weak after the March LME squeeze, citing continued decline in the Stainless MMI, lower volumes versus pre-LME shutdown levels, reduced liquidity and hedging constraints, and risk aversion among market participants. It also highlights reputational damage and lawsuits, expecting fallout to last months or longer.

Reuters reports that Abaxx Commodities Exchange is proposing a physically settled nickel sulphate futures contract to create pricing for the expanding EV battery nickel sulphate market. It reviews LME stock depletion of Class I nickel and the lack of deliverable hedging for intermediate products. The piece focuses on a new pricing venue rather than immediate fundamentals. | Shanghai nickel jumped about 5% to near a four-month high as rising ore prices and tighter supply from Indonesia followed delays in issuing mining quotas. Strong September stainless steel production and a stainless steel futures rally boosted sentiment, pushing SHFE nickel contract gains despite a firming dollar headwind. | The Philippines’ Mines and Geosciences Bureau said H1 nickel ore output rose 40% to 16.87 million dmt. It is also expecting additional increases as four more mining projects are set to start between 2023 and 2025, improving nickel ore supply for stainless steel and EV battery feedstock. | Shanghai nickel jumped nearly 4% to a nearly four-month high on supply worries. Reuters attributes the move to higher ore prices and tighter Indonesian nickel ore availability after delayed mining quotas, alongside firmer demand from China’s stainless steel sector and improving sentiment. | Copper prices fell as markets worried about slower demand from China’s property sector and as LME-registered copper inventories rose sharply. A softer US dollar limited losses, but copper still declined. Aluminium also eased, while nickel dropped amid concerns over Indonesia delaying mining quota issuance for nickel supply. | Metals One said initial diamond drilling at its SRH Rana project in Norway confirmed high-grade nickel-copper-cobalt sulphide mineralisation at the Bruvann prospect. Reported intercepts include 1.0%+ nickel with copper grades around 0.14–0.18%. The mineralisation is open along strike, with further drilling planned by the farm-in partner. | Metals One said early diamond drilling at its SRH Rana project in Norway confirmed high-grade nickel-copper-cobalt sulphide mineralisation, including intercepts with up to 1–2% nickel and notable copper values. The company expects further drilling updates. Near-term fundamentals likely remain unchanged, limiting price impact on nickel and copper. | Copper prices retreated 0.9% to $8,425 as traders worried about China demand, particularly slower property activity, and about rising LME warehouse inventories. Losses were limited by a softer dollar. Aluminium also fell. Nickel edged lower, despite separate supply concerns tied to Indonesia mining quota delays. | The Philippines’ DTI said domestic downstream nickel processing is critical for clean-tech ambitions. Trade Secretary Alfredo Pascual urged private firms to invest or partner globally to upgrade nickel-related value chain activities, aiming to secure a stronger role in clean technology supply chains through sustainability and increased processing capacity.

Copper fell as LME exchange inventories rose and investors worried about demand amid soft economic data from China. Reuters also noted weaker Chinese services activity, while aluminium declined and nickel showed a slight/offset move. The net effect reflected near-term demand concerns and inventory pressure. | The IMF and WTO criticized Indonesia’s policy banning exports of unprocessed nickel, arguing it conflicts with WTO rules. The EU requested WTO mediation and the panel confirmed the EU’s claims. The article suggests potential policy reversal or regulatory pressure, which could increase expectations for nickel export supply. | Fastmarkets launched monthly European premium assessments for nickel sulfate on an in-warehouse Rotterdam basis to improve transparency in nickel-rich battery cathode markets.
