The move reflects regional nickel sulfate price divergence and rising EV demand, but the article does not cite a near-term supply shock. | SMM reports Indonesia’s NPI and high-grade nickel matte projects, noting 14 NPI projects in production and plans for roughly ten more within three years. It also highlights increased high-grade nickel matte production capacity, despite limited current output, with growing relevance to new-energy nickel demand for batteries. | Copper prices in London fell as weak China services data raised doubts about the post-pandemic recovery and whether Beijing’s property-support measures will translate into real metal demand. The article also notes broad LME softness, with aluminium and nickel down, alongside weaker global activity and a firmer U.S. dollar. | Minsur says it will invest at least $2 billion over five years to expand copper and tin activities. It plans about $543 million for underground work at Justa, plus $381 million for processing and site improvements. Copper output in 2022 was 125,488 mt, while tin and smelter upgrades are also planned. | Infinico Metals agreed to acquire full interest in the Nicobi Ni-Cu-Co project in Quebec, with plans to reprocess historic drilling and geophysical data and begin further drilling and downhole EM work in 2024. The announcement supports nickel sentiment and modestly relates to copper given reported grades. | CanAlaska approved a spinout of five nickel properties (36,174 hectares) plus $1 million cash into a new company, Core Nickel, to be held pro rata by current shareholders. Core Nickel will pursue exploration and development of the nickel assets. Uranium remains with CanAlaska.

Mallee Resources entered administration after a creditor refused further repayment extensions, but the company’s head says the Avebury nickel mine will not close and production will continue. He expects the process to take months and likely lead to new ownership. Payment to suppliers and employee entitlements are said to be current. | Kataman Metals sued several firms over alleged fraud involving London Metal Exchange-branded nickel cargoes, saying it paid for 167 metric tons that never reached Dubai and were missing at storage in Malaysia. The suit also covers unpaid interest on nickel and tin, plus related logistics disputes. | Copper eased 0.6% on the LME after China’s yuan slid to a 10-month low and German factory orders fell more than expected, reinforcing worries about weak global growth. Reuters notes disappointing Chinese stimulus and tepid demand into September. LME aluminium rose modestly, while nickel declined, reflecting mixed macro risk. | FPX Nickel says its Baptiste nickel project in central British Columbia would require at least US$2.2 billion in capex and, if developed, could remain a high-margin supplier of battery metals or nickel concentrate. The update strengthens the project’s perceived economic viability and may support near-term nickel sentiment. | The Watchlist releases interviews covering FPX Nickel’s preliminary feasibility study for the Baptiste nickel project and Nicola Mining’s letter of intent to form a joint venture to explore its New Craigmont copper project. Both are development-related updates but are early-stage with limited immediate impact on market fundamentals.

A miner died during a shift at the Avebury Nickel Mine near Zeehan. Mine management confirmed the death and said underground operations have ceased temporarily while employees are supported. Although the cause is stated as non work-related, the shutdown may reduce near-term nickel supply expectations. | Macron’s visit to New Caledonia emphasized nickel’s strategic role for France and Europe. Ministers met nickel firms to discuss sector challenges, including rising energy costs and industrial disputes. The energy crunch raises production risks and costs in one of the key nickel supply regions, creating near-term bearish pressure. | Copper prices fell to the lowest in over a week as a firm dollar and weaker yuan weighed on demand sentiment in China. LME three-month copper and SHFE October both dropped to multi-week lows. The piece also shows nickel weakness alongside modest aluminium gains, reflecting mixed but generally soft metal tone. | Indonesia investigated illegal nickel ore practices, including exports without proper IUP qualifications and ore exports after quota usage. New quota approvals were stopped and expected approvals may be delayed, tightening short-term Indonesian nickel ore supply. With large portions of supply estimated as illegal, the policy raises near-term nickel price expectations. | SMM says battery-grade nickel sulfate prices rose on September 6 to about 31,309 yuan/ton, with the nickel sulfate coefficient around 97–98. It attributes gains to tighter current supply, wider nickel sulfate–electro-nickel spreads improving production profit, Indonesia-related supply worries, and logistics constraints that tighten MHP supply. | Copper prices fell to their lowest in more than a week as a firm U.S. dollar made dollar-priced metals more expensive and the market awaited additional economic stimulus. China’s yuan weakened and trade data remained soft, weighing sentiment. LME copper dropped 0.4%, with nickel also down while aluminium edged up. | Copper Lake Resources announced an updated NI 43-101 mineral resource for its Norton Lake Ni-Cu-Co-PGE deposit in Ontario, totaling 1.96 Mt Measured & Indicated at 0.67% Ni and 0.66% Cu (plus PGE). The release is a resource estimate, not reserves, implying limited short-term supply impact. | Copper fell to a two-week low as LME inventories jumped 21% to 133,850 tonnes, signalling potential surplus concerns, while China’s August imports were 5% lower year-on-year. A stronger U.S. dollar and easing Chinese premiums added pressure. Aluminium and nickel also declined. | Copper Lake Resources reported an updated NI 43-101 mineral resource for the Norton Lake nickel-copper-cobalt-PGE deposit, including measured and indicated copper and nickel contained pounds. The release is primarily technical and does not announce production, permits, or operational changes, so near-term price impact on copper/nickel is likely limited. | Russian nickel producer Nornickel did not pay dividends on 2022 results, citing “negative geopolitics” and the need for steadier free cash flow. While free cash flow rose in H1, management said risks remain. Dividend decisions rest with key shareholders; buybacks are not considered, though a share split is. | A federal judge dealt another setback to a proposed copper-nickel mine in Minnesota’s wilderness. The ruling likely delays or undermines future mine development, reducing anticipated supply additions and supporting tighter near-term balances. The impact is most direct for copper and nickel, with no clear linkage to aluminium. | A federal judge dismissed Twin Metals’ lawsuit seeking permission to mine land where the Biden administration imposed a 20-year mining ban. Twin Metals’ proposed copper-nickel underground project near Ely, Minnesota, faces further delays. Reduced near-term supply expectations for copper and nickel could pressure prices. | A federal judge dismissed Twin Metals’ lawsuit seeking permission to mine land covered by a 20-year federal moratorium. The project targets copper and nickel near Ely, Minnesota. The decision upholds agency actions and extends uncertainty over timelines, reducing expected incremental supply in the near term and weighing on both metals. | A federal judge dismissed Twin Metals’ lawsuit seeking to restore mineral rights for its proposed Minnesota copper-nickel mine, after the Biden administration canceled leases and imposed a long moratorium. The decision keeps the $1.7 billion project stalled amid environmental opposition, adding near-term supply uncertainty for copper and nickel. | A federal judge dismissed Twin Metals’ lawsuit to regain mineral rights for a proposed copper-nickel mine in Minnesota. The decision follows Biden-era cancellations and a mining moratorium, further stalling the $1.7 billion project. Backers cite critical-mineral needs, but opponents say it protects Boundary Waters, limiting supply expectations.

SMM reports that 8–12% high nickel pig iron (ferronickel) prices were unchanged at 1,175 yuan/nickel point, with tight spot conditions. It cites a downward August production trend driven by costs, limited downside from Indonesia’s strengthening local pig iron costs, and bullish domestic sentiment despite weak stainless demand. | Reuters says copper is on track for its worst week in four weeks as a firmer US dollar and rising LME inventories weigh on prices. Risk sentiment is dampened by weak Chinese data and property woes. LME aluminium and nickel also fall on the day, reflecting broad bearish metal pressure. | A federal judge dismissed Twin Metals’ lawsuit aimed at regaining mineral rights for the proposed Twin Metals copper-nickel mine in northeastern Minnesota. The decision adds to setbacks and keeps the Biden-era lease cancellations and mining moratorium in place, lowering prospects for incremental copper and nickel supply. | A federal judge dismissed Twin Metals’ lawsuit seeking to overturn the Biden administration’s cancellation of mineral rights leases for the proposed Twin Metals copper-nickel project in northeastern Minnesota. The ruling upholds agency decisions citing environmental risks. The mine remains stalled, limiting near-term incremental supply prospects. | SMM reports the September 7 average price of 8–12% nickel pig iron (ferronickel) held steady, while production fell in August due to cost pressures and spot supplies remain tight. Domestic bullish sentiment, strengthening Indonesia ferronickel costs, and rising domestic premium suggest limited downside and near-term nickel support. | SMM reports the battery-grade nickel sulfate index at 31,427 yuan/ton on Sept 7, up from the prior session. Prices are supported by still-active procurement demand across precursor and electro-nickel ends and by production weakness near cost levels, while cost remains relatively high. | The piece compares MHP and high-nickel matte supply outlooks, noting expected growth in nickel intermediate production and MHP’s role as a main feedstock for nickel sulfate in China. It also mentions nickel briquettes/powder pricing mechanisms tied to LME. Overall, it signals evolving supply structure rather than an immediate nickel market disruption. | Copper extended losses toward its biggest weekly drop in four weeks as a strong dollar and mixed/patchy macro data, including weaker China demand expectations, weighed on sentiment. Commerzbank cut its copper target and SHFE-registered stocks rose 18%. LME aluminium and nickel also fell on the same pressure. | Canada Nickel’s investor presentation outlines progress toward feasibility and permitting for its Crawford nickel sulphide project, describing resource growth and ongoing impact assessment and financing discussions. Such updates can modestly improve expectations for future nickel supply outside the Indonesia/China concentration, supporting nickel sentiment near term.

Indonesia is urged to provide further incentives and financing support to nickel miners and banks to expand nickel exploration and increase domestic supply. Despite being the top nickel producer, Indonesia imported more nickel ore in 1H2023 than 2022. Observers link import changes to processing capacity and highlight financing hurdles. | London copper rose 0.4% after a weekly loss, supported by signs of China stabilisation: easing deflation, slower factory price declines, and property-market support measures that lift demand hopes. A weaker yuan and higher SHFE copper inventories weighed sentiment. LME aluminium and nickel also gained modestly. | SMM reports SHFE nickel broke its upward trend as a stronger US PMI boosted the dollar and yields, pressuring non-ferrous prices. Overseas nickel stayed at discounts and Jinchuan premiums fell on higher arrivals, while demand remained weak from stainless and civilian alloys. Despite supply-tight logic and some support, SHFE nickel is expected to fluctuate this week. | SMM says battery-grade nickel sulfate prices rose to about 31,427 yuan/ton on Sept. 7, with supply described as tighter after prior sales, ongoing demand for rigid needs, and more active purchasing by electrolytic nickel producers. Cost support from high raw-material coefficients and improving sentiment lifted quotations. | Copper prices rose on Monday as China’s yuan strengthened, the U.S. dollar weakened, and August loan growth beat expectations, encouraging algorithmic trading buy signals. Reuters notes copper had hit a three-week low Friday. Aluminium and nickel were also higher in the same market move amid improving China data. | XCana Petroleum says it completed a 248-acre expansion to acquire full rights over the Treasure Box Hill copper prospect on its Table Mountain project, and notes the prospect sits above western portions of its Nevada nickel lode claims. The release outlines historic grades and mining/smelting history but no new production timeline. | LME data show Russian-origin share in LME-registered warehouses steady at 81% for aluminium, with Russian aluminium on-warrant tonnage falling in August. The article also notes Russian-origin copper share declined while copper tonnage rose, and Russian nickel share slipped. It implies mixed inventory tightness signals for metals.

Copper rose after China’s Country Garden won creditor approval to extend repayment terms on onshore bonds, easing stress in the property sector that drives large metal demand. Reuters notes supportive macro factors like weaker dollar and stronger yuan, and aluminium rose as well, while nickel was slightly lower. | London copper declined as the U.S. dollar rebounded, making dollar-priced metals more expensive. Relief in China’s property sector after Country Garden won bond-repayment extensions provided some support, but the market saw short-covering. The article also notes LME aluminium easing and nickel falling. | SMM reports that on Sept 11 the battery-grade nickel sulfate index rose to 31,481 yuan/ton (+52). Spot ranges increased as well. Downstream procurement demand continues, while some nickel sulfate producers have sold out September spot inventories, strengthening factory price support. Costs and fundamentals remain supportive, lifting prices slightly. | SMM and Indonesia’s nickel miners association (APNI) agreed to cooperate on data systems and to launch an Indonesian nickel industry price index. The article links index development to reducing nickel futures volatility’s impact and supporting policy tools such as export tariffs and ore pricing formulas. | Copper prices fell in London as the dollar strengthened and LME-registered copper inventories rose to their highest levels since October 2022. The dollar reaction ahead of U.S. inflation data pressured metals, while China’s property-sector news capped losses. Aluminium and nickel were also down on the day. | EV Nickel upsized a non-brokered private placement, raising CAD$2.08M to fund continued exploration and advancement of its Shaw Dome nickel project in Ontario, plus general corporate purposes. The offering is subject to TSX Venture approval and is expected to close shortly. | Talon Metals said the US Department of Defense will provide about $20.6 million in cost-share funding via DPA Title III to accelerate drilling and geophysics for high-grade nickel deposits in Minnesota and Michigan. The program targets domestic nickel for defense and US battery supply chains, potentially improving short-term nickel sentiment. | The U.S. Department of Defense agreed with Talon Metals to fund (via DPA Title III) accelerated exploration for high-grade domestic nickel in Michigan and Minnesota. With matching cost-share funding, the program aims to discover and delineate more nickel supply for U.S. batteries and defense supply chains. | Québec Nickel reports drilling results from the Ducros Ni-Co-Cu-PGE project, including multiple long intervals grading around 0.20–0.22% nickel with associated cobalt. The company frames this as a second large Ni-Co ultramafic intrusion discovery this year. No production, permitting, or near-term supply changes are announced. | Amnesty International alleges forced evictions, intimidation, and village destruction tied to expansion of copper and cobalt mining projects in DR Congo’s Kolwezi region. While the DRC supplies most of the world’s cobalt and large copper volumes, the article provides no immediate operational or regulatory disruption, limiting near-term price impact. | The US Defense Department is providing $110 million in support to lithium and nickel miners. While lithium is not tracked here, the funding for nickel producers can improve expectations for near-term nickel supply or production continuity, which tends to be mildly bullish for nickel prices over the coming weeks. | The U.S. Department of Defense signed a deal with Talon Nickel to advance the Tamarack nickel project in Minnesota. The move is intended to strengthen critical-material supply chains and reduce dependence on China and Russia. With limited U.S. operating nickel supply, the announcement is supportive for nickel prices. | A report says nickel mines in Michigan and Minnesota received funding. The financing is intended to support operations and/or development at the mines, linking the update to nickel supply prospects. Because new capacity usually takes time to translate into production, the immediate effect on nickel prices is likely modest. | The U.S. Department of Defense awarded $20.6 million to developers of the proposed Talon nickel mine in Michigan and Minnesota, supporting prospecting work, following earlier DOE funding for ore processing. The article frames nickel as a national security priority amid efforts to bolster domestic critical mineral supplies. | The U.S. Department of Defense awarded $20.6 million to support prospecting for the proposed Talon nickel mine in Minnesota and Michigan. The funding complements prior Department of Energy support for ore processing in North Dakota and aims to identify more high-grade nickel deposits in Upper Michigan. | Indonesia, the world’s largest nickel producer, is importing nickel ore as smelter capacity expands, with shipments rising sharply in 1H 2023. The piece argues the country may have “overplayed its hand” via heavy reliance on export bans and lingering environmental/investment hurdles, potentially weighing on nickel demand and investment sentiment.

Copper prices were largely unchanged in a tight range ahead of U.S. CPI, with traders weighing potential interest-rate implications and China’s demand outlook despite property-support measures. Aluminium and nickel showed only modest moves (aluminium slightly up, nickel listless/falling), implying no strong near-term directional commodity pressure. | SMM says the battery-grade nickel sulfate index in Shanghai rose slightly to 31,511 yuan/ton on Sept. 12. Downstream procurement demand persists, but some manufacturers sold out September spot stocks, tightening supply. With high raw-material costs and a widening upstream-downstream price gap, nickel sulfate prices remain supported. | The U.S. Department of Defense awarded $20.6 million to Talon Metals for prospecting work tied to its proposed nickel mine in Minnesota and Michigan. The DoD cited critical-mineral supply-chain resilience, building on a prior DOE grant for ore processing. Support contrasts with attempts to block other Minnesota copper-nickel projects. | The U.S. Defense Department awarded $20.6 million to Talon for prospecting supporting the proposed Talon nickel mine in Minnesota. The funding follows an earlier DOE grant for ore processing and positions domestic nickel as a national-security priority, potentially improving near-term sentiment for nickel supply versus imports. | The U.S. Department of Defense awarded $20.6 million to Talon Metals to support a proposed nickel mine and prospecting work in Minnesota and Michigan, following earlier DoE funding for processing plans. The support underscores nickel’s defense importance and aims to expand domestic supply, likely boosting near-term nickel sentiment. | The U.S. Department of Defense awarded Talon Nickel (USA) $20.6 million under Defense Production Act Title III to advance nickel exploration and mineral resource definition at the Tamarack Intrusive Complex. DoD said the goal is stronger nickel supply-chain resilience, but the work is mainly exploratory rather than immediate production. | Flash floods from heavy rains struck Indonesia’s Weda Bay, damaging parts of the IWIP nickel industrial park in North Maluku. The floods started Wednesday morning; water levels were receding and there was no immediate casualty report. A source said operations later returned to normal, but disruption risk remains. | Premium Nickel Resources reported new SGS metallurgical testing for its Selebi and Selkirk Botswana Ni-Cu-Co/PGE projects. Results show recoveries into bulk nickel-copper concentrate and excellent extraction in Platsol leaching, enabling Phase 2 downstream tests. Outcomes in coming weeks could support bullish expectations for nickel and copper feasibility. | Reuters reports flash floods in North Maluku, Indonesia, hitting the Weda Bay Industrial Park (IWIP), a major nickel processing hub. Flooding followed heavy rains since Tuesday, with water levels receding and no immediate casualties reported. IWIP operations were said by a source to be back to normal, though disruption risk remains. | Heavy rains and flash floods hit Indonesia’s Weda Bay Industrial Park, a major nickel processing hub in North Maluku. Floodwater began Wednesday morning but reportedly receded, with operations said to be back to normal and no immediate casualties reported. The event may briefly affect processing/logistics, but disruption seems limited. | Flash floods from heavy rains hit Indonesia’s Weda Bay Industrial Park, a major nickel processing hub. Floodwater receded and there were no immediate reports of casualties. IWIP did not comment, but a local source said operations had returned to normal, suggesting only a short disruption risk for nickel supply flows. | The U.S. Defense Department awarded $20.6 million to support prospecting for the proposed Talon nickel mine in Minnesota and Michigan, following prior DOE grants for related processing plans. The news frames nickel as a national-security supply-chain priority, reinforcing expectations for improved domestic nickel supply and supporting sentiment for nickel prices. | ShFE is exploring launching nickel futures for international use via its INE platform, studying market supply/demand and potential deliverable forms like nickel briquette. The effort follows disruption to LME nickel benchmarks after the March 2022 trading debacle, but timing and contract terms remain undecided. | Shanghai Futures Exchange is exploring launching internationally deliverable nickel futures on its INE to give global users an alternative to LME nickel. Officials are consulting industry and studying nickel supply-demand and contract structure, including possible nickel briquette delivery. CME and others are also planning nickel-related contracts. | The U.S. Department of Defense awarded $20.6 million to support prospecting for Talon’s proposed nickel mine in Minnesota, with work also in Michigan. The funding follows prior DOE support for ore processing and highlights nickel’s role in defense and batteries, reinforcing expectations for domestic nickel supply.

London copper fell 0.5% as LME warehouse stocks jumped toward a two-year peak, signaling weaker demand. China’s post-summer pick-up failed to materialize. Aluminium eased and nickel was slightly higher/volatile. A softer dollar capped some losses, but demand concerns dominated. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate at 31,528 yuan/ton on Sept 13, up slightly day-on-day but broadly stable. Downstream buyers continue procurement, low-priced goods are largely sold out, and salt plants are generally out of stock. Tight supplies and high raw-material costs support prices, keeping the market steady. | Gama Explorations reported processed airborne EM/geophysical results identifying new near-surface drilling targets on its Tyee nickel-copper-cobalt project in Quebec, including permits for a temporary camp and submitted drilling applications for priority conductors. The company plans ground validation and potential drilling, but provides no assay or resource update. | The roundup highlights a Defense Department award of $20.6M to support prospecting for nickel mine projects in Michigan and Minnesota, alongside prior DOE backing for ore processing in North Dakota. While this may improve longer-run nickel supply outlooks, it is mainly early-stage and likely limits near-term price impact.

SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate price at 31,529 yuan/ton on Sept. 14, up 1 yuan but essentially unchanged. Procurement of precursor companies is weaker and electrolytic nickel end purchasing has slowed, with less transaction activity. Costs remain strong and with stable downstream demand, nickel sulfate stays flat. | Widgie Nickel reports a major nickel sulphide drilling result, described as a “needle in a haystack” hit with notable width and grade, including an exceptionally high 20% nickel figure. Management discusses the find on an ASX media segment, which may improve near-term sentiment for nickel supply prospects. | Copper rose on Friday after China’s August industrial data signaled stabilization, easing fears of slowing demand from the world’s largest metals consumer. A stronger yuan and softer dollar also supported dollar-priced buying. Aluminium fell on record Chinese production; nickel declined alongside broader base-metal moves and rising inventories.

Copper and other base metals were largely flat in Asia as markets awaited the U.S. Federal Reserve’s Sept. 20 rate decision and guidance. Analysts said the Fed outcome would steer metals and energy. Reported moves were minimal: copper near-flat, aluminium slightly higher on LME, and nickel marginally up. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate around 31,500–32,000 yuan/ton, up modestly and then stabilized. Manufacturers are mostly sold out September spot stocks and expect production cuts, but precursor transactions are weaker and electro-nickel purchasing has slowed. With demand steady and costs high, prices are expected to remain stable next week. | Indonesia’s energy minister Arifin Tasrif warned that, at current nickel ore consumption, Indonesia’s reserves could be depleted in about 15 years. The forecast is tied to high production and growing demand from smelting and EV battery supply chains. Recycling and further exploration are cited as potential ways to extend life. | Canada Nickel closed a US$12 million secured loan facility with Auramet to fund working capital and continue permitting and detailed engineering for its nickel-cobalt sulphide project. The financing supports project progress and potential future nickel supply, but the transaction is not a direct near-term supply or demand shock.

SMM reports SHFE nickel rose modestly, with the most-traded contract closing higher and volume up while open interest fell. It notes pure nickel spot transactions improved alongside a downward trend in futures. Ahead of the Fed decision on Sept 21, SMM expects a slight nickel price rebound. | Copper prices edged lower as markets awaited central bank decisions, with the Fed meeting raising expectations that rates may stay high. Demand worries from China’s slowing rebound and higher exchange inventories also pressured prices. Nickel slipped slightly, while aluminium was marginally up, reflecting mixed sentiment across metals. | SMM reports that on Sept 18 the battery-grade nickel sulfate index rose to 31,707 yuan/ton (+75). The spot range for battery-grade nickel sulfate increased to 31,800–32,100 yuan/ton (+200). Spot availability is low, procurement demand persists, and strong cost support keeps fundamentals tight. | Philex’s CEO said the company is looking into entering the nickel mining space as the global transition to green energy continues. The statement is exploratory and lacks details on timing or scale, but it suggests future nickel supply participation, modestly improving sentiment for nickel. | Copper prices fell in Asian trade as markets awaited the Fed’s two-day meeting and worried about a faltering China rebound, with expectations for weaker demand and higher exchange inventories. Traders also monitored yuan depreciation affecting dollar-priced metals demand. LME aluminium and nickel edged lower alongside copper. | Copper prices fell ahead of a U.S. Fed meeting as traders expected higher-for-longer rates to dampen industrial metals demand and as LME inventories continued building. Reuters also notes declines in aluminium and nickel, reflecting broad risk-off sentiment tied to demand uncertainty, China weakness, and tighter financial conditions. | SMM publishes and updates its methodology for setting an electro-deposited nickel spot benchmark, including data collection rules, cut-off times, and calculation as the SHFE Shanghai nickel main contract plus premium/discount ranges. It explains price range publication and correction/force-majeure handling, aiming to provide a transparent reference for settlements. | SMM publishes a methodology announcement for its electro-deposited nickel (Ni9996) spot price benchmark, detailing data inputs, premium/discount calculation versus SHFE nickel, and daily release timing/cut-offs. The benchmark is an indicative reference for physical spot settlement, not a direct signal of nickel fundamentals or an external shock. | Copper prices extended losses ahead of the Fed meeting as investors expect higher rates to persist, weighing on industrial demand. LME copper inventories continue building, rising sharply since mid-July. China growth concerns, weaker yuan pressure, and weak demand contribute to the broader downside, with aluminium and nickel also falling. | SMM reports nickel sulphate prices rallied on tight spot supply, constrained arrivals, and expectations of high-grade nickel matte shortages linked to Indonesian ore quota issues. Profit inversion at salt producers may trigger production cutbacks, while dip buying and rigid replenishing demand from cathode precursor makers keeps inventories tight. | Copper prices fell as LME inventories built and traders grew cautious before the Fed meeting, fearing “higher rates for longer” could damp metals demand. China’s lacklustre growth and yuan depreciation pressures also weighed. LME aluminium edged down, while nickel was flat after briefly hitting a 14-month low.

SMM’s Sep 20 morning comment reports the most-traded SHFE nickel contract fell to 161,670 yuan/mt and open interest declined, while trading volume dropped. Spot activity weakened after advance purchasing ended. Macro attention is on the Fed decision, but SMM expects nickel prices to keep fluctuating downward. | Copper in London edged up on a softer dollar, ahead of an expected steady Fed decision, but LME-registered copper inventories are building and reaching the highest since May 2022, limiting upside. Nickel rose and aluminium edged higher in LME/SHFE, reflecting day-to-day macro and flows rather than a major supply disruption. | Nordic Nickel’s drilling at the Pulju project in Finland expanded the Hotinvaara prospect, intersecting disseminated sulphides 500m south of the current mineral resource boundary. The company says the mineralised footprint is larger and drilling continues, with assays pending and an updated resource estimate expected by year-end. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate rising to 31,803 yuan/ton on Sept. 19, up 96 yuan from the previous working day. Prices for 31,900–32,400 yuan/ton increased as spot availability remained low, while strong cost support and continued replenishment demand supported further gains. | Copper prices moved in tight ranges ahead of a U.S. Fed decision as rising LME inventories to the highest since May 2022 and higher-for-longer rate expectations/dollar strength limited upside. Aluminium edged up slightly. Nickel rose but faced a large surplus backdrop alongside concerns over Indonesia’s refusal to approve new output quotas. | The Pentagon awarded Talon Metals a $20.6M Defense Production Act grant to accelerate nickel exploration and development in Michigan and Minnesota, including the Tamarack project supplying Tesla. Officials cite national-security and battery demand needs amid reduced reliance on foreign nickel. The funding supports equipment and hiring, improving near-term supply expectations. | Philex Mining plans to expand into nickel after studies and sampling in Zambales, aiming to position itself as a nickel supplier for electric-vehicle demand. The company is also progressing its Silangan copper-gold project. Philex notes its mining scale is small, but the EV-driven nickel focus is supportive for nickel sentiment.

Philippine nickel miners Nickel Asia and another top producer plan about US$1 billion each to build new processing plants. Nickel Asia is pursuing a third HPAL plant near its southern Davao project and is in final talks to secure operating rights. The investments support the government’s push for downstream capacity. | Baowu has delayed a planned transaction to acquire Tsingshan’s Indonesian Morowali nickel-related assets, citing management changes and shrinking profits amid weaker nickel and steel results. The hold raises uncertainty around the sale of ferro-nickel lines and coincides with Tsingshan ramping refined nickel output in Indonesia. | SMM says battery-grade nickel sulfate prices rose on September 20, supported by still-healthy demand from the electro-nickel and upstream segments. Spot nickel sulfate supply is tight and hard to source, pushing prices higher. Indonesian policy to stop issuing 2023 nickel ore quotas may further strengthen cost support, leaving room for more gains. | Harita Nickel says its Obi island facilities have started Indonesia’s first nickel sulfate production operation and process MHP for EV battery cathode precursors. The company expects 240,000 tonnes of nickel sulfate annually and has expanded HPAL-linked production. This boosts near-term nickel supply-demand expectations tied to EV demand. | Copper prices slid to a five-week low as the Fed signalled restrictive rates for longer, boosting the dollar and pressuring risk appetite. LME inventories rose to the highest since May 2022, widening the near-term discount. Nickel and aluminium also fell on the same macro and demand/sentiment backdrop. | Magna Mining released additional assay results from the Crean Hill diamond drill program, reporting high-grade nickel mineralization in the 105 Footwall Zone, including 4.2% Ni and 0.9% Cu over 7.0 metres. The company highlights nickel tenor and additional precious-metal enrichment as exploration continues.

Reuters reports copper prices in London rose slightly on Friday but were set for a weekly decline as LME inventories swelled and the U.S. dollar firmed on higher-for-longer Fed expectations. Analysts cite uneven China growth and tighter financial conditions. Nickel is near multi-month lows on surplus data; aluminium is up. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate at 31,949 yuan/ton on Sept 21, up 54 yuan/ton, with spot scarcity and strong purchasing activity supporting prices. The high precursor price is only partly transmitted downstream, but electro-nickel margins remain attractive. Cost support may strengthen after Indonesia ends nickel ore quotas starting in 23 years. | Reuters reports copper is on track for a weekly loss as the US dollar strengthens following Fed signals, while LME-registered copper inventories remain at their highest since May 2022. Reduced risk appetite and a wider near-term discount point to ample supply and an oversupplied market. | Giga Metals announced a positive NI 43-101 pre-feasibility study for the Turnagain nickel-cobalt project in British Columbia. The study outlines large, long-life production of high-grade nickel concentrate and favorable economics under nickel price assumptions, which can support bullish sentiment for nickel development prospects. | Giga Metals announced a positive pre-feasibility study for the Turnagain nickel-cobalt project in Canada, projecting long-life output of nickel/cobalt concentrate with low impurities and favorable costs. Mitsubishi highlighted the project’s low-carbon profile and potential role in growing nickel demand for batteries. The update improves development outlook for nickel supply. | Giga Metals announced a positive pre-feasibility study for the Turnagain nickel-cobalt project in British Columbia, showing production of nickel-cobalt concentrate with positive post-tax economics. The company highlights demand growth for battery-related nickel products and positions the project to attract strategic investors, supporting nickel sentiment.

London copper fell 0.3% as risk sentiment weakened on expectations of higher-for-longer U.S. interest rates and a stronger dollar, while China’s Sept. 29–Oct. 6 holiday reduced near-term buying ahead of the shutdown. LME aluminium and nickel also declined, reflecting broad macro-driven pressure. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate increasing to around 32,000-32,500 yuan/ton, citing tight August–September spot stocks, greater-than-expected September production reductions, and active precursor demand. Electro-nickel buyers are strengthening purchases despite limited inventory. High raw-material costs and narrowing sulfate-to-nickel spreads provide further support, with expectations for continued gains this week. | SMM says Fed policy expectations turned bearish for nickel: after the Fed held rates, Powell’s remarks kept alive the possibility of further hikes, pressuring prices. Fundamentals show slightly weaker spot transactions and softer stainless demand with possible production cuts. Electroplating demand is stable, while nickel sulphate cost support limits downside. | KoBold Metals will spend about $150 million to accelerate studies for the Mingomba copper project on Zambia’s Copper Belt, aiming for production within a decade and completing additional studies in 2024. The firm uses AI to search for several critical metals, including nickel, but the article only quantifies copper resources. | Copper prices fell on Monday as central banks reiterated a “higher-for-longer” stance, dampening risk sentiment, growth and likely metal demand, and a stronger dollar. Higher inventories added pressure. LME aluminium and nickel also declined, reflecting broader negative metal market sentiment. | Copper prices fell toward four-month lows as concerns grew over China’s demand, helped by rising LME-registered stocks and expectations of further inflows that widened cash discounts. A stronger US dollar weighed on dollar-priced metals. Aluminium and nickel also declined on the broader industrial metals selloff. | ValOre Metals reported initial drill assays from the Pedra Branca project in Brazil, including an interval with 0.37% nickel and 0.11% copper alongside high-grade PGE+Au. The company highlights potential resource expansion from step-out drilling, but the information remains exploration-scale and unlikely to quickly shift nickel or copper prices. | ValOre announced assay results from drilling at Brazil’s Pedra Branca project, including a high-sulphide PGE-Ni-Cu interval with 0.37% nickel and 0.11% copper over 71.8 m. The company highlights resource expansion potential, but no production or logistics changes, implying only modest short-term price effects. | ValOre reported initial assays from 2023 core drilling at its Pedra Branca PGE (plus Ni-Cu) project in Brazil. Results include intervals of high-grade 2PGE+Au with associated nickel (0.37%) and copper (0.11%). The release suggests resource expansion potential but does not imply near-term production changes. | Velocity Minerals reports initial drilling results from the Iglika copper-gold project in Bulgaria under a funding arrangement with Dundee Precious Metals. All five holes intersect anomalous copper/gold mineralization in skarn targets, with cited copper-rich intercepts. The article is exploration-focused and does not affect near-term production supplies. | DMCI Mining expects record nickel ore shipments in 2023-24, citing robust first-half production at Zambales Diversified Metals Corp. and shipment progress toward annual targets. An amended ECC doubling permitted output supports higher volumes, which could increase nickel supply expectations even as DMCI notes higher prices from Surigao closures. | Flying Nickel Mining Corp. announced a non-brokered private placement of up to 5,000,000 shares at $0.09 each to raise as much as $450,000. Proceeds will fund exploration and general working capital for its Minago nickel project in Manitoba, subject to TSX Venture approval. | Flying Nickel Mining Corp. announced a non-brokered private placement raising up to $450,000 to fund exploration and general working capital for its 100% owned Minago nickel project in Manitoba. SMM reports nickel sulfate prices rising on September 25, with battery-grade indices increasing versus the prior working day. Downstream procurement strengthened ahead of the National Day holiday, while high costs and tight supply expectations (including potential production cuts) supported price sentiment. The widened spreads indicate firm support and possible further upside. | Copper prices fell on concerns about China’s demand, especially the property sector, alongside high LME warehouse stocks and a strong U.S. dollar with higher-for-longer rate expectations. The piece notes broader base-metals weakness, including lower nickel. | Transforma Resources provided an update on its MAC Nickel Property, with initial sampling results pending, and announced a small flow-through share financing to fund further exploration. The company targets battery-metal quality nickel/awaruite deposits in British Columbia. No production, shipping, or definitive resource changes were disclosed.

Nickel 28 reported results for its quarter ended July 31, 2023, highlighting Ramu’s performance. It disclosed nickel production and sales volumes in MHP, profitability from the nickel mine, and cash costs of US$3.92/lb net of by-product sales. Reduced production was tied to maintenance and upgrades. | Copper prices fell as the US dollar held near a 10-month high, making dollar-priced metals more expensive. China Evergrande/re-sale concerns and broader property worries weighed on sentiment, though improving industrial profit declines offered limited support. LME aluminium and nickel also dipped, reflecting softer demand expectations. | Avante Mining reported initial assays from a maiden drill program at its Voisey’s West nickel project, including intercepts up to 1.9% Ni and nickel-equivalent grades. Results confirm a magmatic sulfide system and suggest potential expansion at depth, with remaining drill-hole assays pending. | EV Nickel reports optimization of its tank bioleaching on the W4 sulphide zone, achieving >90% Ni extraction and successful precipitation of nickel-rich mixed hydroxide precipitate for battery-plant use. Results progress to a pilot plant phase, potentially enabling localized nickel processing and supply-chain changes. | Glencore announced it will fund Koniambo Nickel SAS only until Feb. 29, 2024 due to continued financial losses and unfavorable cost and market conditions. The company expects site activities to continue but will seek alternative funding and operational solutions, creating near-term uncertainty for nickel supply. | Glencore plans to stop funding its struggling Koniambo nickel mine. The move raises concerns about reduced nickel output and potential delays in production recovery, tightening near-term supply expectations. That typically supports nickel prices as traders price a higher risk of supply shortfalls over coming weeks.

Talon Metals says Department of Defense funding will accelerate its Tamarack nickel project, with $20 million in government investment and $21 million in matching funds. The company is expanding drilling and planning construction in 2026, with processing in North Dakota. The news is supportive for nickel supply expectations. | Copper fell on the LME, pressured by a firm dollar and rising LME warehouse inventories. The Reuters report also notes LME nickel easing and aluminium edging lower, alongside mixed moves in Shanghai. Overall, the cited macro and inventory factors point to near-term bearish sentiment for industrial metals. | Copper prices rebounded as traders squared positions ahead of China’s Sept. 29-Oct. 8 holiday, after earlier losses in the session. LME three-month copper rose, though a firm U.S. dollar limited gains. The report also notes rising LME copper stockpiles and mixed moves in nickel and aluminium. | Atlas Materials raised $27 million to develop technology that processes low-grade saprolite nickel ore into mixed hydroxide precipitate (MHP) for EV batteries. It plans a North America pilot plant later, with production targeted for 2027. Low-emissions processing and byproduct sales are highlighted, implying longer-run supply optionality. | The article argues that Indonesia’s downstream “nickel nationalism” policy—especially the 2014 ban on nickel ore exports—boosted export revenues by forcing processing into higher value products like nickel matte, stainless steel and battery materials. It credits Indonesia’s market power and financing that expanded local production, potentially tightening near-term nickel supply expectations. | Palladium One reported seven drill-ready VTEM anomalies at its Tyko II nickel-copper project in Ontario and said drill testing is planned for mid-October. The anomalies relate to historical Cu-Ni-PGE showings and possible extensions, but no production or immediate supply changes were announced, limiting short-term metal price impact. | Fathom Nickel reports completion of a 2,000+ meter drilling program at its Gochager Lake Ni-Cu-Co property, with multiple sulphide intersections and BHEM conductivity anomalies indicating potential mineralization at depth. Assay results are expected within 4–5 weeks, and no immediate production or confirmed grade changes are provided. | Flying Nickel Mining engages Mercator to prepare an independent NI 43-101 mineral resource estimate for its Minago project in Manitoba. The updated MRE will include nickel and platinum group metals, building on reported assays with strong nickel grades and correlated PGM values. No production or supply changes are announced. | Flying Nickel Mining engaged Mercator Geological Services to prepare an independent NI 43-101 mineral resource estimate for its Minago nickel project in Manitoba, including PGM. The release cites successful June 2023 assay results showing nickel mineralization correlated with PGM, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel in the near term. | Canada Nickel says its IPT Carbonation carbon-storage pilot plant successfully processed over 7 tonnes of tailings and confirmed design parameters for inclusion in an October 12 feasibility study. The company also estimates potential CO2 storage fees and discusses CCUS credits while planning large-scale critical mineral production, including nickel. | Gungnir Resources reported 2023 drilling results from the Lappvattnet high-grade nickel deposit in Sweden, including a new intercept of 6.10 metres at 2.47% Ni (with a 0.55-metre peak at 7.36% Ni) and additional Footwall Zone mineralization. Results suggest potential extension beyond the current resource. | Atlas Materials raised $27m to develop technology that processes low-grade saprolite nickel ore into MHP suitable for EV batteries. The company plans a North America pilot plant after selecting sites in Canada or the US, with initial output expected by 2027. No high-temperature waste claims were cited.

Atlas Materials raised $27 million to develop a waste-free process for converting low-grade saprolite nickel ores into battery-grade MHP, with a planned North America plant and battery-focused output starting in 2027. It cites lower waste and emissions and plans to source ore from New Caledonia, potentially expanding future nickel supply. | Reuters reports copper prices rose on Friday but were on track for a second consecutive quarterly decline, pressured by rising LME inventories and a sharp rally in the dollar. It also notes LME nickel is on track for a third straight quarter of decline and remains the worst base metal year-to-date. | Reuters reports aluminium on the LME rose about 1.9% to around $2,320/t as traders squared positions ahead of quarter-end. Exchange stocks fell on warrant cancellations in South Korea, while the weaker dollar supported dollar-priced metals. Copper also gained, while nickel edged lower. | Talon Metals reported new drilling results from Minnesota’s Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project, including a thicker massive nickel-copper intercept in the Raptor Zone versus a prior hole, with assays pending. It also cites additional increased thickness in a nearby hole and highlights ongoing drilling supported by US government grants. | Talon Metals reports drill results from Minnesota’s Raptor Zone, including a thicker 2.2 m intercept of massive nickel-copper mineralization versus a prior hole, with assays pending. The company says expanded high-grade thickness and ongoing drilling increase prospects for future resource growth, supported by recent federal/DoD grants. | Talon Metals announced new drill results from its Tamarack Raptor Zone in Minnesota, reporting a 139% increase in the thickness of high-grade massive nickel-copper mineralization (2.2 meters) versus a prior hole. Assays are pending, but the thicker zone raises expectations for a larger future resource and output. | Talon Metals reported drill results from the Raptor Zone at its Tamarack nickel-copper project, highlighting a 2.2m intercept of massive nickel and copper mineralization (assays pending) and a 139% increase in thickness versus a prior hole. The company is encouraged about expanding potential resources. | Talon Metals reported new drilling at its Tamarack project in Minnesota, finding thicker high-grade massive nickel-copper mineralization in the Raptor Zone and a significant increase in intercept thickness versus a prior hole. The company also received US$20.6m defense exploration support, but assays are pending and impact is mainly exploratory.

Copper prices eased at the start of Q4 as a firm dollar and higher LME inventories weighed on demand expectations, with China markets closed until Oct. 8. Copper output in Chile rose. Nickel prices edged up, with nickel inventories still climbing. Aluminium was flat, with no clear near-term catalyst. | Copper rallied 0.7% and nickel 1.3% on Monday after the U.S. passed a stopgap bill to avoid a government shutdown, boosting risk-on sentiment. Aluminium also rose 0.8%. The weaker dollar supported dollar-priced metals, while higher LME copper inventories and increased Chile supply limited further upside. | Horizonte Minerals updated construction progress for its Araguaia nickel project in Brazil, but final engineering changes and supplier delivery delays increase planned capex by at least 35% and push first production to Q3-2024. The company continues site work and expects Line 2 feasibility results in Q4 2023. | Gunsynd PLC said its investee, Pacific Nickel Mines, began mining saprolite nickel ore at the Kolosori nickel project on Isabel Island in the Solomon Islands. The project targets direct shipping ore and Pacific Nickel expects its first ore shipment in November, supporting prospective nickel supply outlooks.

Reuters reports London copper slid to its lowest in more than four months, down as much as 0.6% to around $8,005/t, driven by a firmer U.S. dollar after strong U.S. data supporting higher-for-longer rates. Aluminium dipped slightly, nickel rose, and COMEX showed managers increasing net short positions. | Copper prices fell to a four-month low as the dollar strengthened and LME exchange inventories rose sharply. Aluminium retreated and nickel slipped slightly as broader metal sentiment weakened. | Indonesia says its WTO appeal over the EU dispute (DS592) challenging Indonesia’s nickel ore export ban remains pending because the WTO appellate body has been dysfunctional since 2019. The nickel ore ban is still in effect, keeping restrictions on unprocessed nickel exports as the dispute is revisited. | Vale has partnered with BluestOne under a commercial off-take and non-binding agreement to repurpose refinery waste and extract materials from its Onça Puma nickel mine in Brazil. Copper traded below $8,000/t after falling on Wednesday as a firmer U.S. dollar and rising LME exchange inventories weighed on sentiment. Three-month copper slipped about 0.4% to $7,976.5. LME aluminium and nickel also declined, reflecting the same macro and inventory-driven pressure. | The article links nickel’s recent fall to weakening demand and a supply surplus. It cites H1 2023 data showing a 140,000-tonne contained nickel surplus versus a prior deficit, driven by smelter expansions in Indonesia outpacing slower demand growth. Higher U.S. interest rate expectations further pressured prices near-term. | Copper prices fell to a more than four-month low as a firm U.S. dollar and rising LME warehouse inventories weighed on sentiment. Higher-for-longer interest rates and yields increased contango/cash-and-carry financing costs. LME aluminium edged lower and nickel slightly higher amid the same macro backdrop. | Analysts say Korean EV battery cathode material firms (POSCO Future M, LG Chem, EcoPro BM) will see weaker Q3 profits as global EV demand softens and lithium and nickel prices plunge. The lower cathode-material price and negative price-lag effect from higher-priced mineral purchases are expected to hurt margins. | Copper prices slumped to a four-month low as investors worried about global growth and higher rates, with U.S. Treasury yields surging. A weaker dollar offered some support, but technical breakdown signals further losses. Rising LME cash-to-three-month spreads and higher inventories reinforced the bearish near-term tone for base metals. | QC Copper will acquire a 10% interest in Pickle Lake Minerals’ Thierry Copper Project in Ontario, described as the province’s largest primary copper resource. The release highlights supportive drilling and a historical PEA producing copper (and nickel). The deal may bolster copper sentiment via improved resource outlook, with modest nickel relevance. | Orecap Invest Corp. agreed to acquire a controlling interest in the Thierry copper mine in Ontario. The release highlights drilling results and prior production, plus a PEA estimating future output and economics. The deal and exploration upside could support copper sentiment, but no immediate supply change is indicated. | Orecap Invest Corp. agreed to acquire a controlling interest in the Thierry Copper Mine, Ontario’s largest primary copper resource. The release cites recent drill intersections and infrastructure that supports development, aiming to expand resources via a bulk tonnage scenario. Historical production included copper and some nickel. | Wyloo’s CEO says Ring of Fire nickel could supply up to 500,000 EVs to North America, highlighting strong nickel demand for batteries and Canada’s lower-carbon nickel processing. The project’s ambitious 2030 timeline reduces immediate supply influence, keeping near-term nickel price impact limited.

Copper is set for a fourth straight daily fall as rising LME inventories weigh on sentiment, while trading volumes remain thin because China is on holiday. Reuters reports three-month copper down 0.2%. Aluminium and nickel also declined modestly, reinforcing a cautious near-term tone despite earlier intraday gains. | BHP says EV makers are not paying a “green premium” for sustainably mined Australian nickel while Indonesian laterite nickel volumes are unexpectedly large. This additional Indonesian supply has pushed nickel to a two-year low. BHP plans to invest in sulphide geology but anticipates further Indonesian nickel growth. | Copper is poised for a fourth straight session of declines as LME inventories rise and the dollar stays firm, pressuring dollar-priced metals. Aluminium also fell. Nickel slipped and the piece cites a deeper supply surplus from production ramp-ups in Indonesia and China, reinforcing bearish sentiment across base metals. | Sumitomo Metal Mining said the global nickel market should remain in surplus in 2024, with the surplus widening to 36,000 metric tons from 24,000 this year. The view is based on Indonesia’s growing NPI output and rising supply outpacing demand, despite expected increases for stainless steel and batteries. | Australia’s Nickel Industries plans to enter final investment decision within weeks on an Indonesia Morowali HPAL smelter producing MHP, nickel sulfate and nickel cathode. With ~72,000 tonnes annual MHP capacity and large investment, the project strengthens expectations for future nickel output, supporting nickel market sentiment. | Québec Nickel Corp. reports AI-driven exploration results at its Ducros property, generating multiple new nickel-sulphide (and Ni-Cu-PGE) targets and updating geological interpretations to guide further work. The company also continues deep drilling at the Fortin Sill target, aiming to extend mineralization at depth. | Copper fell for a fourth straight session as investors sold on worries over China’s demand momentum, uncertainty about interest rates and growth, and rising LME stocks. The piece also cites CTA-driven selling that pressured aluminium and nickel, while nickel/lead saw losses and nickel’s surplus outlook worsened sentiment.

INSG forecasts nickel production exceeding demand for the third straight year, with the surplus rising to 239,000 tonnes next year and growing larger each year. Despite rising EV-related demand, output growth is faster, especially from Indonesia and China. Bears are heavily short, and surplus may increasingly reach LME/ShFE deliverable nickel. | Copper prices eased and were set for their steepest weekly decline in nearly 11 months as a firm dollar and high LME inventories weighed on sentiment. Aluminium rebounded slightly but remained on track for its biggest weekly drop since February. Nickel also extended losses for a fourth straight session. | Reuters reports that LME nickel contract volumes are improving after the 2022 trading crisis, with August volumes up year-on-year. However, liquidity remains below early-2022 levels and brokers stay wary due to low LME warehouse stocks and market fragmentation. Nickel prices are down for the year amid surpluses from Indonesia. | Reuters reports copper rose Friday but is set for its biggest weekly drop in five months due to sustained high interest rates, a firm dollar, and elevated LME inventories. It links the fall to weaker-than-expected demand growth and CTA fund selling, while aluminium and nickel prices track the broader base-metal mood.

Copper opened higher after a weekly decline as China’s demand remained resilient, supporting both copper and aluminium despite property weakness. However, expectations of further U.S. Fed rate hikes weighed on overall sentiment. The piece reports nickel falling while other base metals rose slightly. | Abaxx Commodities Exchange in Singapore plans to launch a physically settled futures contract for nickel sulphate used in EV batteries and aims to add additional battery-metal contracts after nickel. Executives say delivery mechanics differ from the LME, and regulatory approvals are needed for timing. Nickel is the direct linkage; other metals are unspecified.

London copper is set for a third straight daily gain as a weaker dollar and bullish sentiment lift LME prices, with LME Seminar showing copper as the top upside bet for 2024. China demand is cited as robust despite property stress. Aluminium edges up; nickel is also higher in SHFE trading. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate trading around 32,100–32,800 yuan/ton, with slight week-on-week increases but weaker demand transmission from high prices. Post-holiday, nickel sulfate is expected to stay stable. | SMM reports that China’s battery-grade nickel sulfate index rose to 32,182 yuan/ton on Oct 9. Spot activity remains weak after holidays, but inquiries persist. Tight raw-material supply and low shipping willingness support costs, while downstream demand continues, leaving potential upside for nickel sulfate prices. | Indonesia’s government says a European company is negotiating a large nickel smelter investment to supply batteries and electric vehicles. Since Indonesia’s 2020 ban on raw nickel ore exports, the policy has driven downstream investments despite an EU WTO dispute. New smelter plans are likely to support nickel supply expectations. | Copper prices fell as renewed jitters over China’s property demand intensified after Country Garden warned of significant challenges meeting offshore debt. The move was reinforced by sharply higher LME warehouse inventories, widening a surplus outlook and weakening cash premiums. Aluminium and nickel also declined amid broader industrial-metals risk sentiment. | Russian-origin aluminium on LME warrants fell to 76% in September from 81% as  cancellations occurred in Gwangyang, with Russian aluminium tonnage dropping to 132,475 from 183,650. Russian copper share fell to 23%, while nickel rose to 22.5%. Aluminium was up on the day, Reuters said. | Pacific Ridge reported additional drilling at its Kliyul copper-gold project, including 103.5 m of 0.63% CuEq (0.18% copper) in the Kliyul North zone and expanded known extents of the porphyry system. The release is exploration-focused with no near-term production or infrastructure impact. | Advance Metals announced “outstanding” rock chip assay results from its Augustus polymetallic project in Arizona, including up to 11.97% copper and economically viable copper grades in ~40% of samples, plus gold and zinc. The update is exploration-related and unlikely to change near-term physical metal balances.

Copper prices rebounded as the U.S. dollar weakened and investors hoped China could restart demand via potential infrastructure-focused stimulus, including possible sovereign debt issuance. The piece also notes mixed moves across other metals: LME aluminium rose while nickel edged lower, reflecting uneven market impact. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate spot prices rising to around 32,203 yuan/ton, with expectations of further daily increases. Demand inquiries are active but trades remain weak. | Indonesia’s Merdeka Battery Materials (MBMA), linked to Merdeka Copper Gold, is expanding nickel processing via HPAL and other routes, including an agreement with GEM to build an HPAL plant at IMIP. The strategy targets EV battery supply chains, potentially boosting expectations for nickel demand and downstream utilization. | Copper prices edged higher as U.S. bond yields fell from recent peaks and markets looked for China to announce more stimulus. The housing-demand outlook remains a concern and LME inventories rose toward a 17-month high, reflecting a likely surplus. Aluminium rose slightly, while nickel eased. | Canada Nickel Company will host a webcast and conference call to discuss its Definitive Feasibility Study for the Crawford nickel sulphide project. The update could affect market expectations for future nickel supply from the Timmins-Cochrane camp, providing modest near-term support to nickel pricing sentiment. | Copper edged higher on the London Metal Exchange as U.S. bond yields fell from recent highs and investors looked for more China stimulus. However, concerns persist about China’s housing slowdown, while evidence of rising copper inventories and mine ramp-ups points to a potential surplus.

Copper prices edged lower as London inventories rose to the highest since May 2022, with LME copper down and the copper premium signaling resilient Chinese imports. The drop was partly offset by a soft U.S. dollar and hopes for further China stimulus. Nickel and aluminium moved modestly. | Copper rose on Thursday as a weaker U.S. dollar made dollar-priced commodities cheaper and as investors looked for further Chinese stimulus. Chinese infrastructure-debt expectations lifted demand signals, including a higher Yangshan premium. However, gains were capped by sharply higher LME copper inventories, while aluminium stayed flat and nickel rose. | Canada Nickel reported a positive bankable feasibility study for its Crawford nickel sulphide project, highlighting higher after-tax NPV/IRR and production of about 48 ktpa nickel. The update strengthens expectations for future long-life development (permits/financing) and improves sentiment for nickel demand-supply balance over coming months. | Midland Exploration released new assay results from drilling at its Santos nickel-copper discovery on the Tete Nord property. The latest hole intersected mineralized zones with grades including 0.33% Ni and 0.12% Cu over 39.73 metres. Additional drilling is planned after permits in November. | Grizzly Discoveries reports first sample results from work at the Greenwood District targets (Midway, Imperial, Copper Mountain). Results highlight gold and silver, with some base-metal grades including copper (up to 0.475%) alongside lead/zinc. The article is exploration-focused and contains no immediate supply or market-impact details. | The Market Herald releases interview content covering multiple companies. The only clear industrial-metals link is Canada Nickel’s positive bankable feasibility study for its Crawford nickel sulphide project, reporting improved economics versus the preliminary analysis. Better project economics can support expectations for future nickel supply and sentiment.

SMM reports that on Oct 12 the Shanghai battery-grade nickel sulfate index was unchanged day-on-day at 32,202 yuan/ton, with the average also stable. Supply remains tight due to unresolved raw material issues, but demand is soft with weak inquiries and transactions, leaving overall nickel sulfate market conditions weak. | London copper edged up as a softer dollar supported USD-priced metals, but it was still set for a weekly decline amid weaker industrial demand sentiment tied to “higher-for-longer” rates. Copper was pressured by rising LME warehouse inventories. The article also notes minor moves in aluminium and nickel on the day. | LME Week highlights that last year’s nickel crisis still lingers: lawsuits over canceled nickel trades remain pending, and the exchange is still rebuilding confidence in its nickel contract. Nickel volumes and participation lag, while rivals expand nickel trading and other venues gain EV-battery-related traction, pressuring sentiment.

Reuters reports LME Week with lingering fallout from last year’s nickel crisis, including pending lawsuits over cancelled nickel trades and continued efforts to strengthen trading/clearing systems. It says nickel trading volumes and participation remain weaker than other metals and nickel lags the broader LME recovery, implying cautious near-term market sentiment. | Copper prices opened higher on resilient Chinese demand and signs of stabilizing growth, with imports at an intra-year high. However, gains were capped by rising LME inventories and a firmer dollar ahead of a Powell speech, limiting the net near-term upside. Aluminium was steady; nickel rose modestly. | SMM said China’s battery-grade nickel sulfate index rose to 32,202 yuan/ton and has stabilized under upward pressure after the holiday. Tight raw-material supply persists with low spot circulation and reluctant selling. Demand weakened on slower electro-nickel and precursor purchasing, but cost-side support remains strong, supporting nickel. | Papua New Guinea’s PM Marape urged MCC, developer of the Ramu nickel/cobalt project, to expand into downstream processing, and MCC said it would seriously consider such expansion in its PNG investment plans. The project is MCC’s biggest investment outside China, supporting nickel-focused sentiment. | Copper edged higher on Monday as the dollar weakened, reversing two sessions of losses, supported by stronger-than-expected Chinese physical demand and infrastructure spending. However, nerves over the Middle East and weaker global growth sentiment weighed on metals, with traders still net short. Aluminium dipped, while nickel rose. | Flying Nickel Mining closed a non-brokered private placement raising about $600,705 at $0.079/share, with Norway House Cree Nation as sole investor and strategic partner. Proceeds fund exploration and working capital for the Minago nickel project in Manitoba. The announcement may modestly support near-term nickel sentiment via development progress expectations. | Alaska Energy Metals reported assay results from initial holes at its Nikolai Nickel Project, intersecting long, persistent nickel sulphide mineralization (about 0.23% Ni over ~341m and ~296m) with accompanying copper. The company says results validate historical drilling and may accelerate a maiden resource.

Copper prices slipped on Tuesday as the U.S. dollar strengthened ahead of Powell’s speech and as elevated LME inventories weighed on sentiment. Reuters notes weaker demand indicators, including falling Chinese copper imports. LME aluminium also fell slightly and nickel eased, reflecting broader industrial-metals softness. | SMM reports the October 16 battery-grade nickel sulfate index at 32,236 yuan/ton, up 36 yuan/ton from the prior day, with the market broadly flat. Battery-grade nickel sulfate is quoted at 32,100–33,000 yuan/ton. Supply and demand remain weak and quotations unchanged, indicating muted near-term pressure. | Copper prices in London fell as traders worried about weaker global demand, focusing on China’s property sector and upcoming monthly economic data. The article cites strong supply in copper. LME aluminium and nickel also fell, reflecting softer sentiment and a stronger dollar, which can dampen metal buying. | Bell Copper says the Arizona Geological Survey, with USGS and state funding, is starting a three-year geochemical study of critical minerals at Arizona porphyry copper deposits, using core samples from its Big Sandy project. The effort targets critical co-minerals, but no immediate production or supply changes occur for copper or aluminium/nickel. | Canadian North Resources commissioned an updated NI 43-101 mineral resource estimate for its Ferguson Lake project in Nunavut, adding 39,270 metres of drilling and expanding nickel- and copper-bearing mineralized zones. The company notes an independent technical review, but no production or shipment changes were announced.

Glencore said its Mount Isa underground copper mines (Enterprise, X41, Black Rock) and the Mount Isa copper concentrator will close in 2025 due to end-of-mine life, low ore grades, and technical constraints. Copper smelter/refinery and some other assets continue, limiting but supporting copper supply outlook. | Copper prices rose ahead of China’s upcoming GDP data, driven by a brighter demand outlook and concerns about longer-term supply amid energy-transition demand. Reuters cites robust consumption from renewables and infrastructure and Glencore planning to close Mount Isa copper operations by 2025. LME aluminium and nickel also edged higher. | SMM reports the battery-grade nickel sulfate index at 32,235 yuan/ton on Oct 17, down 1 yuan/ton from the prior day. Trading activity remains weak and participants take a wait-and-see stance. Supply and demand for nickel sulfate are described as still weak, with no change in quotations. | Funds have returned to the LME nickel market, building $4.6 billion in short positions. Such positioning generally signals bearish expectations and can add incremental selling pressure in the near term, weighing on nickel prices over the coming days to weeks. | Copper and other base metals rose in London after China posted better-than-expected GDP growth, supporting expectations for higher demand from the world’s top consumer. The piece cites improving consumption/industrial activity and robust non-ferrous output alongside relatively low Chinese stockpiles, driving gains in copper, aluminium and nickel. | Canada Nickel’s October 2023 investor presentation highlights the Crawford nickel sulphide project, presenting economic returns and progress on permitting. It argues global nickel supply is concentrated in Indonesia/China while demand growth from EVs is accelerating. Overall, the content supports a tighter nickel market outlook versus existing supply.

Copper prices edged up as China’s better-than-expected GDP and signs of improving consumption/industrial activity supported demand expectations. Gains were modest due to a firmer U.S. dollar and higher Treasury yields as markets expected rates higher for longer. LME zinc, tin, lead fell while aluminium rose. Nickel was down on SHFE. | SMM reports the SHFE most-traded nickel contract fell overnight to close slightly lower, with lower volume and open interest. Macro uncertainty about potential US rate hikes pressured commodities broadly. Fundamentally, electrowinning demand was stable, but Norilsk spot transactions were sluggish, leading SMM to expect nickel prices to edge down further. | Copper rose 0.6% on LME support from stronger-than-expected Chinese growth, consumption and industrial activity, plus hopes for additional stimulus. Gains were capped by a firmer dollar as U.S. yields approached 5%. Copper prices edged lower as the U.S. dollar strengthened following a surge in the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield. Three-month LME copper fell 0.1%. It targets copper output from 15 million tonnes of tailings and contaminated groundwater in Suhar starting from late 2023, with further ramp and gold production in 2024. Invested in a copper tailings remediation plant.

Copper prices opened lower on Monday as Middle East conflict concerns intensified after fears Israel-Hamas could broaden, while LME/SHFE copper stocks rose. Three-month LME copper fell 0.4% and Shanghai December copper dropped 0.8%. Aluminium also dipped slightly; nickel was slightly higher. | The SMM morning note cites stronger-than-expected US retail sales but a higher December Fed hike probability, with a USD spike then retreat and commodity pressure. For nickel, new electrowinning capacity is ramping, imports of nickel plates keep declining, and demand weakens amid reduced 300-series output and lower downstream orders. Prices seen weak but stable this week. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate prices stabilizing around 32,000–33,000 yuan/ton, with wider price ranges. Raw materials remain tight and high, providing strong cost support tied to the LME nickel coefficient, but electro-nickel buyers face losses and procurement stagnates, limiting demand and constraining downside. | Russia’s Nornickel said Q3 nickel output rose 21% quarter-on-quarter to 53,945 tonnes, boosting near-term supply expectations for a major nickel producer. Full-year guidance remains intact. Although Western suppliers avoiding Russia could create longer-term operational constraints, the reported production increase is supportive for nickel. | Norilsk Nickel reported consolidated 9M 2023 production results. Nickel output fell 9% y/y to 145 kt due to scheduled furnace repairs and concentration grinding mill maintenance. Copper output fell 4% y/y to 304 kt mainly from cathode-quality adjustments. Q3 showed q-o-q increases for both metals. | Canada Nickel’s investor presentation emphasizes the Crawford nickel sulphide project’s economics, permitting progress, and potential production scale, including carbon capture-related incentives. SMM reports that on Oct 23, the battery-grade nickel sulfate index was 32109 yuan/ton, up 1 yuan from the prior working day. The battery-grade range stayed at 32000–33000 yuan/ton. Even with weaker nickel prices, a high raw-material coefficient and cautious downstream buying kept quotations stable. | Anglo American said Q3 copper-equivalent production fell 1% year on year but rose 4% sequentially. Overall, it implies supportive near-term nickel price sentiment. | Anglo American said Q3 copper production rose 42% overall, including higher output from Peru’s Quellaveco and gains at Collahuasi and El Soldado, partially offset by declines in Chile due to ore hardness and an electrical substation fire. Nickel production fell 7% on lower grades and maintenance. Guidance was revised lower for copper. | Anglo American reported a 42% jump in third-quarter copper output, but lowered its full-year copper production guidance to 830,000-870,000 tons after copper fell in Chile due to unfavorable ore and mine incidents. No production or supply impact is described. | Nickelex Resource Corp. announced a non-brokered unit private placement to raise about $1.05 million to fund working capital and the initial phase of exploration on its Strong nickel project in Manitoba. Such exploration updates can raise expectations for future supply from the project, generally providing mild near-term support to nickel and copper price sentiment, while aluminium is not implicated.

SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate at 32,109 yuan/ton on Oct. 24, unchanged from the prior working day. Despite declines in nickel prices, a high raw-material coefficient supports salt-factory pricing. Downstream precursor buyers remain in wait-and-see mode with heavy offers, so market activity is weak and quotations are stable. | Nickel 28 says the Ramu Mine’s Basumuk HPAL plant has resumed full production after a 6.7 earthquake on Oct. 7.
