The update also references a curtailed sulphur programme at its copper plant, while aiming for reductions by 2027. | Nornickel began sulphur dioxide capture at its Nadezhda smelter near Norilsk, aiming to cut emissions 45% by 2025 via converting SO2 into sulphuric acid and gypsum. It cites equipment supply challenges after foreign vendors withdrew post-Ukraine and mentions a curtailed similar programme at its Copper Plant. | Nornickel began operating at its Nadezhda Smelter in Norilsk, launching a project to cut sulphur dioxide emissions by 45% by 2025 via SO2 capture and conversion to sulphuric acid. The programme was reshaped after foreign suppliers pulled out, but it signals environmental compliance rather than near-term nickel supply disruption. | Nornickel began operations at its Nadezhda smelter in Norilsk, starting a sulphur dioxide capture scheme to cut emissions by 45% by 2025. The project required reconfiguring equipment after foreign suppliers pulled out, supporting ongoing nickel (and related copper) smelting activity despite sanctions-related procurement challenges. | Zambia, facing large external debt, is pursuing major copper expansion with investments such as KoBold’s $150m Mingomba copper-cobalt project and renewed mining/refining activity. The government aims to triple production by 2032, supported by debt restructuring and improved operational control, which is generally supportive for near-term copper market expectations. | Nornickel began operations on a multi-billion rouble sulphur dioxide capture project at its Norilsk smelter, converting SO2 to sulphuric acid and gypsum via limestone to cut emissions 45% by 2025. Equipment sourcing was reshaped after Ukraine-related vendor pullouts, with similar work at the Copper Plant affected by sanctions. | Nornickel began sulphur dioxide capture at its Nadezhda Smelter near Norilsk to cut emissions by 45% by 2025, using equipment sourced largely from Russia due to sanctions-related supplier pullouts. The move links directly to nickel smelting operations, while a similar copper-plant programme was previously curtailed by sanctions. | Nornickel began sulphur dioxide capture at its Nadezhda Smelter near Norilsk, aiming to cut emissions by 45% by 2025 versus 2015. The company cites supply disruptions from sanctioned foreign vendors but says production has started. It also notes its copper-plant emissions project was curtailed, with further reductions targeted by 2027. | Leading Edge Materials reports high-grade cobalt-nickel assay results from Gallery 7 (including 3.5% cobalt and 29.7% nickel) and copper-zinc-lead-silver results from Gallery 4 (including 11.7% copper). The release is based on chip sampling from galleries with follow-up drilling planned, not production volumes.

SMM’s morning comment notes SHFE nickel opening at 146,500 yuan/mt and closing at 147,900 yuan/mt, up 340 yuan/mt. Volume fell and open interest decreased. With dovish Fed messaging reducing rate-hike expectations and softer spot demand, SMM expects nickel to rebound slightly. | Fastmarkets launched free-on-board (FOB) Indonesia price assessments for nickel pig iron (NPI) and mixed hydroxide precipitate (MHP), key intermediates for stainless steel and nickel-rich lithium-ion battery materials. No immediate market disruption was cited. | Fastmarkets launched free-on-board Indonesia price assessments for nickel pig iron (NPI) and mixed hydroxide precipitate (MHP), key nickel battery raw materials. The release highlights potential for continued high-grade mineralization near an existing world-class nickel deposit, but provides no production impact. | Indonesia’s investment and foreign affairs ministries say negotiations are underway with French and British firms to build a nickel smelter and downstream facilities in Indonesia, alongside Indonesian partners. Nickel prices fell on Friday and were set for their worst weekly performance in about a month. The move was attributed to expectations of a growing global nickel surplus in 2023. Analysts also cited possible short-covering rallies, but the near-term market tone remains bearish. | Nickel prices reversed earlier losses but remained on track for their worst weekly decline in a month, pressured by forecasts of a widening global nickel surplus in 2023. While some analysts see potential rallies from fund short-covering and supply risks in Indonesia, the immediate outlook is dominated by surplus concerns. | The LME said Guangxi CNGR New Energy Science & Technology has applied to list its primary nickel brand CNGR. Glencore maintained most 2023 guidance but lowered nickel output expectations by 9% to around 102,000 tons due to Sudbury smelter maintenance outages, a longer recovery from 2022 strike action, and a revised lower outlook for Koniambo. Its nickel output year-to-date is down 16%, tightening supply expectations. | Glencore said it expects full-year copper and zinc despite lower first-nine-month output, citing Cobar mine sale and weaker by-product production. These updates are likely to affect copper and nickel market expectations near term; aluminium is not addressed. | Glencore reported lower production across most operations and trimmed 2023 guidance for nickel and ferrochrome. Nickel output fell 16% in the first nine months, and annual nickel forecasts were reduced to ~102,000 tonnes. Copper production also declined and guidance points to slightly lower output for 2023, adding selling pressure. | The piece announces Argus’ launch of a new suite of nickel prices with Indonesia’s PT IKI. The move concerns price assessment/benchmarking rather than physical market fundamentals. With no details on supply, demand, inventories, or sanctions, the immediate effect on nickel prices is likely minimal. | Glencore lowered its 2023 nickel production guidance by 9% to about 102,000 metric tons, citing Sudbury smelter maintenance outages and a slower-than-expected recovery after 2022 strike action, alongside a weaker Koniambo outlook. This implies tighter nickel supply expectations, likely weighing on nickel prices. Copper guidance was maintained. | Indonesia’s high-grade nickel ore, used mainly for nickel pig iron (NPI) production, may deplete in about six years. With Indonesia holding ~23% of global nickel reserves and having expanded after the 2020 ban on ore exports, reserve outlook can influence nickel supply expectations. | Class 1 Nickel reported assay results from 2022–2023 diamond drilling at its Somanike Ni-Cu sulphide project near the former Marbridge mine in Quebec, including multiple nickel intersections above cut-off grades. The article examines the 2022 nickel trading disruption on the LME, when nickel prices surged and large nickel trades were cancelled. It focuses on regulatory accountability rather than new immediate drivers for nickel fundamentals. | QC Copper closed a deal acquiring a 10% interest in Cuprum, which owns the Thierry copper mine in Ontario. The transaction is financing/exposure related, not immediate supply. | Orecap Invest Corp. closed a controlling stake acquisition in Ontario’s Thierry Copper Mine, described as the province’s largest primary copper resource. The release highlights recent underground drilling intercepts and a prior PEA with projected copper and some nickel production, but no near-term output change. | Copper fell Tuesday as weak October China manufacturing data renewed demand concerns, prompting selling despite support from a softer dollar and falling LME/cross-exchange stocks. The asset is described as large and infrastructure-ready, with drilling intersections reported as copper (plus minor nickel). The news is corporate/development oriented and does not indicate immediate production or supply changes.

Orecap Invest acquired a 45% stake in Cuprum, which owns the past-producing Thierry copper mine in Ontario. CCMI will receive warrants and milestone payments tied to NI 43-101 resource expansions. CCMI also uses proceeds to fund the Bull River Mine loan. Thierry includes copper and nickel. | Mosaic Minerals begins a minimum 2,000-meter drilling program at its Gaboury nickel project in Quebec to assess nickel potential in the Pike Nickel zones. The release also notes lithium exploration work. | Visionary Metals announced progress at its King Solomon nickel project in Wyoming, completing over 3,000 m of RC drilling with reported nickel-cobalt intersections (e.g., 0.23% Ni over 44 m) and battery-grade nickel sulfides. The update is exploratory and does not indicate production or near-term supply changes. | Visionary Metals reports it has completed over 3,000 m of reverse circulation drilling and found nickel mineralization at its King Solomon project in Wyoming, including a 44 m interval of 0.23% nickel and 17 m of 0.43% nickel, plus battery-grade nickel sulfides. The International Nickel Study Group forecasts rising supply surpluses due to Indonesia’s rapid output growth. Sibanye-Stillwater confirmed it will exercise an option to buy Tasmania’s Mt Lyell copper mine and is conducting a feasibility study for reopening. The article highlights copper as key for the clean-energy transition and notes restart cost and potential annual output, supporting expectations of improved copper supply prospects. | Reuters discusses LME nickel bears increasing net shorts to the biggest since 2018, alongside an INSG outlook for large and widening global nickel oversupply driven by Indonesian mined production growth. Yet it notes Indonesia’s shift toward higher-purity Class I output and LME brand approvals that may close the product gap, making timing uncertain. | A new video shows an uncontacted Indigenous tribe in Indonesia warning outsiders to stay away as bulldozers destroy nearby rainforest on Halmahera. Campaigners say mining operations are penetrating deep into the tribe’s territory and are calling it evidence of severe harm, potentially increasing scrutiny and disruption risks for nickel mining. | Ero Copper reported Q3 2023 operating results, including 10,766 tonnes of copper production and reaffirmed full-year copper guidance, with cost updates reflecting a stronger BRL. The company also cites continued regional nickel exploration. While relevant for copper sentiment, the update is not a clear market-wide supply or policy shock.

The article argues Indonesia’s nickel downstreaming is overly dependent on China’s investments, smelter partnerships, and market demand. It says China’s slowdown has reduced imports of some Indonesian commodities and that China is increasingly favoring LFP EV batteries that require less nickel, potentially lowering nickel demand. | Copper gained for a second straight week as a weaker U.S. dollar supported commodities amid subdued sentiment after poor Chinese economic data. LME aluminium rose modestly and LME nickel edged higher alongside broader risk-driven moves, suggesting macro/FX factors dominated rather than fresh metal-specific fundamentals. | Members of the Hongana Manyawa tribe in North Maluku blocked heavy equipment entering the Aki Sangaji River to stop logging tied to nickel mining at Mount Topu Blewen. Copper prices held in a tight range as investors balanced higher supply and uncertain demand against support from a weaker U.S. dollar. China’s slower winter consumption and uneven recovery added demand uncertainty. The report also shows mild easing in LME aluminium and a decline in LME nickel, alongside small overall moves. | London copper rose 0.4% as a weaker U.S. dollar supported commodity prices and China stimulus improved risk appetite. LX International said it will invest 133 billion won to acquire a 60% stake in Indonesia’s AKP nickel mine and secure management rights and the entire offtake. It plans phased production increases and aims to more than double annual output over time, shifting toward battery minerals like nickel. | Copper fell 1% from a one-month high as a stronger U.S. dollar pressured dollar-priced commodities and China’s trade data pointed to uneven recovery. This is tightening supply for high-nickel materials, pushing nickel spot prices higher in Europe and the US and raising near-term pricing risks despite lower raw-material costs.

Reuters/Interfax reports that some customers who previously rejected buying from Russia’s Nornickel are now in discussions about purchasing metals in 2024. The shift suggests renewed willingness to source Nornickel output, potentially improving expectations for nickel demand and pricing over the near term.

The U.S. Department of Energy allocated nearly $115 million for a proposed nickel processing facility in Mercer County, North Dakota. Talon Metals would ship nickel ore by rail from Minnesota for processing, with supply linked to Tesla. The project uses fly ash from coal processing to extract more nickel.

Global Ferronickel Holdings reported higher revenues, driven by increased volume of high-grade nickel ore from its Palawan mine and an improved sales mix. The average realized nickel ore price rose to $31.93/WMT. Costs increased, but improved pricing and output are likely supportive for nickel market sentiment. | Nickel Shaw is a planned open-pit mine in Yukon producing nickel and copper concentrates (along with PGMs). The pre-feasibility study was completed in August 2023, with 19.1 years of mine life and $1.68bn pre-production capex. The article lacks any new short-term operational or supply-shift catalyst.

London copper edged higher to about $8,052/t, supported by firmer equities, but stayed near a two-week low as a stronger U.S. dollar and China demand uncertainty weighed. LME aluminium dipped and nickel rose, reflecting mixed sentiment. Chile’s Codelco production guidance added minor context. | Copper hovered near a two-week low as a firm U.S. dollar and uncertainty about China’s and Europe’s recovery weighed on demand expectations. Investors awaited U.S. inflation for rate outlook clues. China stimulus needs also raised concerns. Aluminium was steady and nickel rose modestly, but copper sentiment remained bearish. | Eramet earmarked about $2 billion for expansion focused on nickel (and lithium). While full production ramp-ups may take longer, the announcement can support market sentiment by improving prospects for future nickel supply, influencing expectations over the next several weeks. | Copper prices rose on Monday after bearish traders covered shorts, lifting three-month LME copper about 1% to $8,115/t. Gains were limited by uncertainty over economic growth, high interest rates, and weaker China demand signals. Aluminium rose slightly; nickel fell, reflecting broader mixed base-metal sentiment ahead of data. | The U.S. and Indonesia plan discussions aimed at a nickel-focused mineral trade agreement tied to Biden’s Inflation Reduction Act EV tax credits. Work includes desktop target refinement, confirmatory drilling on a historical nickel/copper resource, exploratory drilling on a TDEM anomaly, and metallurgical testing. Drilling has commenced.

Copper prices edged down as the US dollar firmed, while traders awaited key US inflation data to gauge whether the Fed may raise rates further. Powell’s recent comments have tempered hopes the hiking cycle is ending. LME aluminium and nickel showed only slight moves, suggesting mainly macro-driven effects. | Si6 Metals announced jagged high-grade copper-silver drill intersections at its Dibete project in Botswana, including 2m at 4.2% copper and 268g/t silver, tied to geophysical anomalies. The results strengthen the perceived copper sulphide potential in early drilling, supporting bullish sentiment for copper. | Canadian Copper reports trenching and exploration at its Murray Brook West project and updates on court-supervised efforts to restart the former Caribou Mine. The company notes existing toll-milling infrastructure and argues Murray Brook could extend mine life, but no clear near-term production or shipment figures are provided. | Eramet plans to invest €1.9bn from 2024-2026 to expand lithium and nickel capacity. It aims to double nickel output at Indonesia’s Weda Bay mine, rising from ~30 million wet metric tonnes this year to 60 million by 2026. The supply outlook is negative for nickel prices. | EU Raw Materials Week coverage highlights policy measures to secure strategic raw materials for net-zero technologies. Copper traded near a five-week high as the dollar weakened following softer U.S. inflation data, boosting dovish Fed rate-cut expectations. LME copper was steady-to-higher and Shanghai copper rose. The report also shows LME aluminium gaining slightly, while nickel fell on the day.

Copper prices eased from near six-week highs as a stronger U.S. dollar—supported by retail sales and cooling inflation data—reduced demand for dollar-priced commodities and reinforced expectations that the Fed will cut rates later. In the same report, LME aluminium and nickel also fell modestly. | Talon Metals reported assay results from 116 new drill holes at the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project, including 14 exploration holes with high-grade nickel-copper massive sulphide intercepts outside the current resource. The company cites potential for resource growth in the CGO East and CGO West areas, supporting longer-term supply expectations. | Talon Metals reported results from 116 drill holes at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt Project in Minnesota, including 14 exploration holes intersecting high-grade nickel-copper mineralization outside the current resource. Intercepts include several thick massive sulphide zones with elevated Ni and Cu assays, indicating potential resource growth. | Talon Metals reports that 14 of 20 newly drilled exploration holes at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project intersect high-grade massive sulphide mineralization outside the current resource. The company highlights multiple strong nickel and copper (NiEq) assays and suggests continued potential for resource expansion, improving near-term sentiment for Ni/Cu. | Talon Metals reported high-grade nickel-copper massive sulphide intercepts in 14 of 20 new exploration holes at its Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project, including thick, elevated NiEq intervals outside the current resource. Management highlighted continued potential to expand resources, supporting bullish market sentiment toward nickel and copper. | Talon Metals announced results from 116 drill holes at its Tamarack Nickel Project, with 14 exploration holes intersecting high-grade nickel-copper massive sulphides outside the current resource. Copper prices inched higher as the weaker U.S. dollar made dollar-priced metals cheaper, but gains were capped by renewed concerns over China demand, with home prices falling again and commentary on rising Chinese refined copper output. LME aluminium also edged up, while nickel rose slightly, indicating mixed near-term pressure. | Indonesia’s state holding Mind Id is negotiating to increase control of Vale Indonesia, Indonesia’s major nickel producer, amid rising resource-nationalism and elections. Savannah is a nickel-copper-cobalt mine with prior suspensions. | Reuters reports that under a contract, Trafigura will supply nickel in the range of about 20,000 to roughly 40,000 tonnes per annum. It also notes widespread complaints about working conditions, but does not indicate immediate operational stoppages. Net near-term impact on nickel prices appears limited.

London three-month copper rose to an eight-week high on a softer U.S. dollar and hopes that Chinese property support will improve demand. Reuters cites pledges for property financing and debt-risk resolution. LME aluminium also advanced and nickel increased, reflecting broader bullish sentiment across industrial metals. | Korea Zinc and Trafigura signed a KRW185bn (USD140m) deal to build an all-in-one nickel refinery in Ulsan via KEMCO, using an integrated pyro-hydro process for nickel matte and MHP. A long-term contract secures nickel feedstock supply of 20,000–40,000 tonnes per year, supporting nickel supply outlook. | Trafigura will invest $140m for a 12.9% stake in Korea Zinc’s KEMCO to build an all-in-one nickel refinery in Ulsan. The plant will process nickel matte and mixed hydroxide precipitate, supported by Trafigura’s long-term feedstock supply of 20,000–40,000 tpa and offtake rights. | Gama Explorations completed a SkyTEM time-domain EM survey over its 625 km2 Tyee nickel-copper project in Quebec, adding 1,187 line km and generating 66 drill targets. Results from pending analyses are expected to refine targeting for sulphide-related nickel-copper occurrences, but there is no production or supply update. | Gama Explorations says it completed a SkyTEM geophysical survey over its Tyee nickel-copper-cobalt project in Quebec. The EM data is expected to improve drill targeting and highlight additional targets after previously discovered nickel-copper and titanium-vanadium showings, with samples pending analysis.

Copper prices climbed to two-month highs as supply risks intensified. An indefinite strike at Peru’s Las Bambas mine and maintenance at First Quantum’s Cobre Panama facility—both tied to protests blocking coal—supported LME and Shanghai copper gains. A weaker U.S. dollar further boosted the base-metals complex, lifting aluminium and nickel too. | Ranchero Gold announced it can earn a 100% interest in the Pinchi Lake nickel project in British Columbia under an option agreement with Recharge Resources. The project is a prospective awaruite deposit and includes exploration funding and cash/share payments. No production or near-term supply changes are stated. | Korea Zinc and Trafigura plan to expand an all-in-one nickel refinery in Ulsan using an integrated hydro-pyro process to produce nickel sulfate via MHP feedstock supplied by Trafigura. The project targets battery-grade supply in South Korea, where MHP conversion capacity is limited, supporting nickel expectations despite price softness.

Copper prices pulled back from two-month highs as a firmer U.S. dollar and renewed growth concerns weighed on sentiment. However, declines were limited by a supportive supply-demand backdrop, including low inventories, supply disruptions, and strong Chinese demand. Aluminium and nickel also fell modestly amid the macro/dollar influence. | The article reviews Indonesia’s dominant nickel role and its raw nickel export ban since 2020. It also highlights EV-battery supply chain shifts and policy moves in the US and EU, including an anti-subsidy investigation targeting Chinese automakers sourcing Indonesian nickel. The implications are trade- and policy-driven, but timing/magnitude are uncertain.

Nickel prices plunged on the LME, with the contract hitting a 2.5-year low amid oversupply. Reuters cites a rising global nickel surplus (155,000 tons in the first nine months), driven by an Indonesia supply surge outpacing demand. Other base metals moved slightly, but nickel was worst hit. | London nickel prices fell to a more-than-2.5-year low as oversupply concerns weighed on sentiment. Reuters cites a widening global nickel surplus (155,000 tonnes in the first nine months) driven by Indonesian supply, plus inventories near the highest since March 2021, pressuring nearby and deferred nickel contracts. | SMM announced it will introduce two new benchmark price points for Indonesian domestic laterite nickel ore with 1.2% and 1.6% nickel (delivery to factory) from Dec 1, 2023. The update formalizes data collection and calculation under its methodology and is published weekly, aiming to improve pricing transparency for settlement. | Copper prices rose on the LME as a weaker U.S. dollar and a stronger yuan supported Chinese purchasing demand. The Yangshan premium hit a yearly high and front-running for potential China property stimulus boosted buying. LME nickel and aluminium were also slightly mixed in the session. | Great Southern Copper raised GBP905,000 via a placing to fund further exploration in Chile, including drilling at its Especularita copper-gold project and work at other targets, plus initial activity at its Monti Lithium project in Salar de Atacama. The funding is modest, with limited immediate implications for copper prices. | Copper rose on the LME as a firmer yuan and weaker U.S. dollar boosted Chinese buyers’ purchasing power for the dollar-priced metal. Front-running around potential China property stimulus further supported demand. Nickel was also higher and aluminium edged up, reflecting a broadly supportive base-metal tone.

Zangezur Copper and Molybdenum Combine said a sanctioned beneficial owner (Gleb Trotsenko) no longer holds a stake after selling it in 2023, and that the new large shareholder (Svetlana Ershova) acquired shares legally and avoids sanctions. Media speculate this could be sanctions circumvention, raising currency-market concerns for Armenia’s exports. | Reuters reports London copper on track for a second straight weekly gain, supported by renewed China support for the property sector boosting demand signals (higher Yangshan premium), a weaker U.S. dollar, and improved risk appetite after softer US CPI. Copper holds around $8,409/t, while aluminium and nickel drift lower. | Nornickel will pay $18 million to drop its nickel JV stake in South Africa. The Nkomati mine has been on maintenance after stopping mining amid low nickel prices, and the buyer ARM will assume environmental liabilities. Nickel prices are falling sharply, but the transaction closes in 2024, reducing near-term impact. | Canada Nickel filed a Bankable Feasibility Study for its Crawford nickel sulphide project in Ontario. The study highlights large reserves/resources, long-life average nickel output, and low cash costs, alongside strong economics. While development is still future-facing, the BFS can support bullish sentiment for nickel prices over coming weeks.

Shanghai nickel slid to a 14-month low as weakening stainless steel demand and rising supply pressured prices. SHFE December nickel fell 3.8% and LME three-month nickel extended losses. Analysts pointed to broader stainless production cuts, softer EV consumption, and stock builds with capacity ramp-up. | Nickel Industries is seeking investors for its Indonesian Morowali plant, which will produce EV-battery nickel products such as nickel sulphate, matte and MHP. The company is inviting firms like Tesla and Panasonic to its operations, expects customer deals in 2024, and targets sizeable output once completed in 2025. | Nornickel’s South African unit agreed to transfer its 50% stake in the Nkomati nickel JV to African Rainbow Minerals, subject to competition approval, with closing due next year. Nkomati has been on care and maintenance since 2021, and the deal mainly shifts ownership and liabilities rather than immediate production. | France is seeking an agreement by January to stabilize New Caledonia’s nickel industry after political tensions and high energy costs drove producers toward collapse. It aims to secure €1.5bn short-term financing, improve energy and logistics, and shift exports toward battery-grade nickel, supporting near-term supply outlook. | Premium Nickel Resources reported additional assay results from underground drilling at the past-producing Selebi North Ni-Cu-Co mine in Botswana. Intercepts show meaningful nickel and copper grades (NiEq highlights), supporting continued resource definition efforts. The release improves project outlook but does not signal immediate supply changes, limiting near-term price impact. | Premium Nickel Resources reports additional underground drilling assays from the Selebi North mine in Botswana, a Ni–Cu–Co sulphide system. Results include thick intercepts with grades such as 1.74% NiEq over 22 metres and higher-grade Ni and Cu sections, supporting optimism for defining mineralization down plunge and potential future resource growth.

Reuters reports copper edged higher as China’s central bank signaled accommodative policy and as a strike began at Peru’s Las Bambas copper mine, boosting supply-risk sentiment. Nickel rebounded from three-year lows after an industry report raised the prospect of changes to Indonesia’s nickel pricing. Aluminium slipped modestly. | Copper edged higher on hopes China’s central bank keeps policy accommodative and on a reported indefinite strike at Peru’s Las Bambas mine. Nickel rebounded from three-year lows after an industry report suggested changes to Indonesia’s nickel pricing. Aluminium slipped modestly as broader metal prices were mixed. | Benjamin Hill Mining announced two inaugural diamond drill holes at its Alotta copper-gold-molybdenum project in Yukon, confirming multiphase porphyry-style mineralization with copper and molybdenite indicators. The program is proposed to continue in May 2024, with more drilling targeting geophysical and soil geochemical anomalies. Assays are pending. | London’s High Court will rule on whether the LME unlawfully cancelled billions of dollars of nickel trades in March 2022. Elliott and Jane Street seek $472 million over voided trades after a one-week suspension during a chaotic nickel price eruption. The outcome could affect confidence and LME crisis-response expectations. | Canadian North Resources reported assay results extending sulphide mineralized zones at its Ferguson Lake nickel-copper project in Nunavut, including wide intersections grading both Ni and Cu. The release indicates targets and potential resource upgrades, but provides no production or shipment data that would immediately alter near-term supply expectations for copper or nickel.

SMM’s conference focused on developing an independent Indonesian nickel pricing mechanism. Indonesia’s ore and NPI values are said to trade at higher CIF/transaction levels than HPM benchmarks, and the gap is expected to widen as supply-demand imbalances grow. SMM plans trial Indonesian nickel ore and NPI price benchmarks from Dec 1. | BHP will trial Arca’s “air-to-rock” carbon mineralisation at the Mount Keith nickel mine in Western Australia, capturing carbon from mine waste and converting it into rock. The 18-month pilot has Canadian grant support and aims to reduce emissions, but it does not signal immediate changes in nickel supply or pricing fundamentals. | UBS’s base metals outlook for 2024 projects copper deficits widening to a larger shortage than previously expected, supportive for copper prices. It also forecasts stronger nickel oversupply and sustained downward pressure. Aluminium is expected to be broadly balanced but with a tendency to shortages later in 2024. | UBS forecasts base metals broadly rangebound short term. Copper is expected to shift to a slightly larger supply deficit (108k mt in 2023, 73k mt in 2024), which supports higher prices. Nickel faces intensifying oversupply and downside pressure. Aluminum is balanced overall but projected to tighten later in 2024. | The piece headline says the LME won a case tied to the “nickel crisis,” with a UK ruling going against Elliott. If related to nickel contract or delivery/settlement mechanics, the decision likely reduces uncertainty and supports nickel sentiment near term, though details are not provided in the text. | Orecap Invest Corp. completed an additional seed-round private placement into Cuprum, which owns the Thierry Copper Mine in Ontario. Proceeds fund working capital, going-public preparations, and further assays/drilling. Thierry is described as a primary copper resource with nickel byproduct, but no near-term production change is announced. | The London High Court upheld the LME’s decision to cancel about $12 billion in nickel trades in March 2022 after a sharp price spike. Firms including Elliott Associates and Jane Street had sought $472 million in compensation. The judgment affirms LME crisis powers, with limited direct implications for near-term nickel fundamentals. | An administrative law judge recommended Minnesota regulators not reissue a crucial “permit to mine” for the long-delayed NewRange/PolyMet copper-nickel project due to concerns that its waste-basin design won’t prevent water pollution. The permit decision is now with the DNR, keeping supply timelines uncertain. | An administrative law judge recommended Minnesota regulators not reissue a key permit for the long-delayed NewRange/PolyMet copper-nickel mine, citing insufficient waste-basin design to prevent water pollution. Further regulatory and court obstacles remain, increasing the risk of additional delays and reduced near-term supply expectations for copper and nickel. | A UK court upheld the LME’s decision to cancel $12 billion of nickel trades during the March 2022 turmoil, rejecting investors’ claims it exceeded its powers. The ruling likely affects legal/regulatory sentiment around the LME, but it does not introduce new near-term nickel supply or demand shocks. | A UK High Court upheld the LME’s decision to cancel nickel trades on March 8, 2022, rejecting investor lawsuits. The ruling highlights LME rules, clearing-house risk from margin calls, and references a dramatic prior nickel price surge. Impact is mainly about confidence and liquidity in LME nickel contracts, not fundamentals. | The LME won a £372m legal battle over its cancellation of about £11bn in nickel trades after the nickel price surged past $100,000/t in March 2024. The High Court ruled LME can void trades in exceptional circumstances. The decision should ease clearing and legal uncertainty for nickel markets.

The Advocate reports China-linked buyer interest in the Avebury nickel mine sale. The mine has resumed production and continues exporting nickel concentrate, despite Mallee Resources’ financial distress and receivership. The article cites a nickel oversupply narrative from Indonesia affecting prices, but offers no clear near-term supply cut. | London copper rose 0.6% as China’s manufacturing slowdown boosted expectations of additional government stimulus, supporting near-term demand. Prices were also supported by supply worries after Panama ordered the First Quantum Cobre Panama mine shut down following a Supreme Court contract ruling. LME aluminium edged up; nickel was slightly lower. | An administrative law judge recommended Minnesota regulators not reissue a crucial permit for the long-delayed NewRange (PolyMet) copper-nickel mine, citing concerns that the planned waste-basin design won’t adequately prevent water pollution. The ruling extends uncertainty around permitting, potentially delaying or derailing future copper and nickel supply. | An administrative law judge recommended Minnesota regulators not reissue a crucial permit for the long-delayed NewRange/PolyMet copper-nickel mine, citing inadequate waste-basin design to prevent water pollution. Further legal steps remain, but the setback adds supply uncertainty and undermines the project’s timeline, pressuring sentiment for copper and nickel. | Archer Exploration resumed drilling at its 100% owned Grasset nickel sulphide project in Quebec. Fall 2023 will test a newly identified nickel-rich massive sulphide zone from prior hole GR23-03, with follow-up EM surveys. Assays include high-grade nickel and minor copper, with no immediate production impacts. | Archer Exploration resumed drilling at its Grasset nickel project in Quebec to test shallow extensions of a newly discovered nickel-rich massive sulphide zone. Earlier results included 1.55% Ni over 5.80 metres and intervals of higher-grade nickel, with minor copper. Fall 2023 drilling and DHEM targeting continue. | Copper Road Resources announced drill results from the JR Zone, reporting long intervals of copper and molybdenum mineralization in the Jogran Porphyry and extending the vertical extent of Richards Breccia. The company emphasizes continuity and potential for additional discoveries, but the update is exploration-only and doesn’t affect near-term supply. | EnerVenue, a US-based manufacturer of metal-hydrogen batteries, is launching pre-assembled EnerVenue Energy Racks with integrated energy storage vessels (150 kWh and 102 kWh) and nickel-hydrogen battery systems. The company promotes simplified installation and modular scaling, but provides no volume figures that would clearly move nickel demand or supply soon. | A U.S. city approved Westwin Elements to build a first-of-its-kind nickel and cobalt refinery in Lawton, but residents are concerned about the company’s lack of mining/refining experience and potential environmental and health impacts. The debate and possible petition to halt progress increases near-term delay risk for nickel supply.

The US blocked Indonesia from becoming a free-trade partner for IRA battery sourcing benefits, after failed talks between Jokowi and Biden. As Indonesia is not on the US list, Indonesian materials are less likely to qualify for EV tax credits. Australian nickel miners welcomed the move, supporting nickel demand expectations. | Copper prices rose after the dollar weakened and China’s Caixin factory activity unexpectedly expanded in November, supporting demand expectations for industrial metals. The article also shows aluminium up modestly and nickel roughly unchanged, suggesting a broader positive metals tone driven mainly by macro and China data. | SMM says it has launched Indonesia’s domestic nickel ore price from Dec 1, publishing weekly assessments for Ni 1.2% and Ni 1.6% laterite delivered to factories. The average prices were $22.9/wmt and $36.7/wmt, each down $0.9 and $1.6 from the prior week. | Copper rose to a three-month high as Caixin showed stronger-than-expected China factory expansion, while exchange inventories declined and tighter supply signals emerged (Panama issues, less concentrate availability). A softer U.S. dollar and expectations of earlier Fed easing provided additional tailwinds. Nickel and aluminium were also modestly higher. | Talon Metals agreed with the U.S. Department of Energy on how to spend nearly $115 million to develop a North Dakota nickel ore processing plant supplying Tesla, with ore from a proposed Minnesota mine. The project has to pass further Minnesota permitting and faces environmental opposition, but DOE funding signals support for critical-minerals supply. | Developers of a Talon Metals nickel ore processing plant backed by the U.S. Department of Energy have reached an agreement on how to spend nearly $115 million. Funding would be released as permits are obtained, improving the project’s execution timeline and supporting domestic nickel supply prospects.

Premium Nickel Resources Ltd. announced a proposed $20 million equity and debt financing, including a brokered private placement and an amended term loan, to fund exploration and development of its mineral assets in Botswana. The article does not cite any imminent production or supply disruption, so near-term nickel pricing impact is likely limited. | Copper prices retreated Monday as the dollar strengthened, LME warehouse stocks increased, and doubts re-emerged over China’s demand prospects. Copper was down 1.3%. The report also mentions nickel pressured by surplus expectations and aluminium lower, reflecting weaker industrial-metals sentiment. | CaNickel signed a 12-month option agreement to sell its Bucko Lake Mine in Manitoba, a nickel project, for up to C$80 million, subject to approvals. The option includes an upfront payment and additional tranches tied to permitting and commercial production, implying potential future nickel supply. | Premium Nickel Resources announced C$20m ($14.7m) equity and debt financing, including funds to advance exploration and development at its Selkirk mine in Botswana. Selkirk produces nickel and copper (among other metals), but the company describes use of proceeds for development and working capital, implying limited immediate supply impact. | Producers in China and South Korea are shifting NCM cathode production toward mid- to low-nickel chemistries to reduce battery costs as EV demand weakens and auto-makers intensify price discounts. High-nickel NCM output is reported cut by 20–30%, implying softer near-term nickel demand despite longer-term plans to return to high-nickel. | Marvel says it is mobilizing drill crews to the Duhamel nickel project. The move suggests progress in exploration/development toward potential future nickel output, which can modestly lift sentiment for nickel supply expectations. The press-release excerpt provides limited operational detail, constraining how strongly it may move prices soon. | Marvel Discovery Corp. says it has retained a contractor to run a 2023 drilling campaign at its Duhamel Ni-Cu-Co (and Fe-Ti-V) property, targeting massive sulphide zones with airborne and TDEM geophysical trends. Historical results include nickel and copper grades, but no production or supply-change details are given. | The piece argues nickel’s underperformance will persist as Indonesia’s output and smelting expand after the 2020 ore export ban, adding to global surplus. It forecasts surpluses widening in 2024, notes weaker stainless-steel demand, and highlights very bearish LME positioning, leaving nickel prices under pressure near term.

Premium Nickel Resources Ltd. upsizes a previously announced private placement and amends its term loan, raising total financing to about $22 million. The company expects closing around Dec. 14, subject to approvals. The news relates to potential project funding but does not cite production volumes or nickel supply changes. | Si6 Metals in Botswana says recent drilling at Maibele North intersected strong visible nickel-copper sulphides (pentlandite, chalcopyrite) and expects assays in 6–8 weeks. The company plans a mineral resource review and open-pit mining study. While relevant for nickel and copper sentiment, no immediate supply impact is indicated. | The US Treasury’s FEOC definition will disqualify nickel projects with more than 25% foreign-government control—especially many Indonesian operations with Chinese stakes—from IRA battery-related tax credits. This likely reduces near-term expectations for Indonesian nickel entering the US EV supply chain, tightening demand prospects. | Pacific Ridge reported diamond drilling results at its Kliyul copper-gold project, including an interval of 111.0 m grading 0.45% CuEq. The company says drilling expands the known KMZ mineralized footprint and that mineralization remains open. The release is copper-focused and provides no near-term nickel or aluminium drivers. | Kutcho Copper Corp. reports target generation for its Kutcho copper-zinc project, highlighting high-priority drill areas such as Esso West, with historical intercepts including 7.2m at 2.0% Cu and plans for ground and drill testing in 2024. The release is exploration-focused with no clear near-term market supply impacts. | Cobalt One (Blackstone Minerals) agreed to acquire CaNickel’s Bucko Lake nickel mine in Manitoba for about C$80m. The past-producing asset is outside Wabowden and requires tranches, permitting and eventual commercial production. The buyer is described as well-resourced to advance the project, supporting nickel supply sentiment. | Trafigura seeks to keep a freezing order against businessman Prateek Gupta, arguing he orchestrated a $600 million fraud involving nickel trades. The dispute alleges nickel was substituted with lower-value materials, leading to a major impairment. The ruling could affect Trafigura’s finances, but it does not indicate new near-term nickel market disruptions. | Arya Resources Ltd. said drilling has begun on its Dunlop nickel-copper project in Saskatchewan, after permits were obtained and crews were mobilized to test identified targets. No resource estimates or production changes were provided, implying limited near-term implications for nickel and copper supply expectations and prices. | Arya Resources Ltd. announced the start of drilling on its Dunlop Nickel-Copper Project in Saskatchewan, targeting identified nickel-copper targets. The company says it has permits and is mobilizing crews to begin around Dec. 6, 2023. The news concerns exploration activity, not production or shipments. | Arya Resources announced mobilization and planned commencement of a drill program starting around Dec. 6, 2023 on its Dunlop Nickel-Copper project in Saskatchewan, targeting nickel-copper targets. Permits have been received; however, no drilling results or production changes are reported, limiting near-term price impact. | A London court document alleges that Trafigura used shipping receipts (bills of lading) from nickel trades to raise financing by lending them to uninvolved firms and potentially substituting low-value metals for pure nickel. The dispute focuses on trade documentation/arbitrage, with no stated immediate effect on nickel supply or official stocks.

Blackstone Minerals executed a 12-month option to acquire 100% of the Wabowden nickel sulphide project in Manitoba, which includes the Bucko mine and processing facility. The firm says the asset could supply its Ta Khoa refinery, reducing reliance on third-party feed, with low-cost, renewable power and supportive policy alignment. | The LME is researching a possible nickel sulphate contract amid rising EV battery demand. Nickel sulphate is a lower-purity nickel input for batteries, and Indonesia’s dominance in nickel supply increases focus on this product. A new Singapore exchange also plans a nickel sulphate futures contract. | Blackstone executed a 12-month option to acquire 100% of the Wabowden nickel sulphide project in Manitoba, including the Bucko mine and processing facility. The company says Wabowden could supply Ta Khoa refinery, improve nickel feed security, and benefit from low-cost hydropower and IRA compliance as battery demand grows. | Indonesia expects 2023 nickel product output at 1.65-1.75 million tonnes and highlights 2.2 million tonnes of current capacity, including NPI/matte and MHP. The government is not issuing new NPI permits but supports HPAL/MHP expansion. Commentary on smelters facing tighter ore supply adds near-term supply-market tension. | Green River Gold reported assay results from three drill holes at its Quesnel Nickel Project, confirming consistent nickel mineralization starting at bedrock surface and continuing across 50 consecutive holes. The company also plans further drilling (about 6,000 meters) to test depth and expand the known zones, supporting project upside but not near-term supply. | Green River Gold reported assay results from three holes at its Quesnel Nickel/Magnesium/Talc Project, with average nickel grade of 0.18% across eight received holes and evidence of nickel-bearing minerals starting at bedrock. The company is continuing drilling and awaiting permits for additional 6,000 m of NQ drilling. | The report highlights two early-stage proposed nickel projects in B.C. (FPX Baptiste and Giga Metals Turnagain), with pre-feasibility studies and major investor backing. It notes potential output, processing approaches, and permitting hurdles, but emphasizes early development and long timelines, limiting immediate nickel price impact.

London copper prices were flat as traders waited for catalysts. A nearly 1% weekly gain in the U.S. dollar index weighed on dollar-priced copper. LME aluminium slipped slightly, while nickel rose sharply; however, overall moves suggest limited new directional impetus. China export growth helped cushion sentiment. | Lifezone Metals announced an updated Mineral Resource estimate for its Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, increasing measured/indicated resources and contained nickel (and also copper) and adding ultramafic material via a revised processing flowsheet. The release is based on resource modeling, not reserves or near-term production. | Lifezone Metals reported a significant Mineral Resource update for its 69.7%-owned Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, increasing Measured & Indicated resources and contained nickel (and also copper) versus the prior estimate. It strengthens the technical basis for a Definitive Feasibility Study, improving sentiment on future battery-metal supply. | Lifezone Metals reported a 69% increase in measured and indicated resources at the Kabanga Nickel Project in Tanzania, with updated grades including nickel and copper. The company cites ongoing work toward a definitive feasibility study, but no production change, making near-term effects on nickel and copper prices likely limited. | Nickelex Resource Corporation requested a 30-day extension to close a non-brokered private placement to fund working capital and early exploration on its Strong Project in Manitoba’s Thompson Nickel Belt. Net proceeds target detailed geophysics and drill planning, with the offering subject to TSX Venture Exchange approval. | Nornickel (Norilsk Nickel) announced shareholder approval of an interim dividend for nine months of 2023 (RUB 915.33 per share) and a 100-to-1 ordinary share split. The announcement does not cite any operational changes affecting nickel or copper supply, demand, or inventories in the near term. | Ford said it will take a direct stake in Indonesia’s Pomalaa battery-nickel chemicals project with PT Vale and Huayou Cobalt. The venture targets 120,000 tons of nickel chemicals annually, with production starting in 2026. Ford also agreed to procure battery materials from Pomalaa to secure EV supply.

Pampa Metals contracted Eco Minera to conduct a ~2,500m diamond drilling program at its Piuquenes porphyry copper-gold project in Argentina, aimed at testing depth and lateral extension of known mineralization. Drilling starts in January 2024, with assays expected progressively from March. Historical intervals reported included high copper grades. | Anglo American said it will cut near-term production and capital spending to lower costs amid weak Chinese demand and macro uncertainty. The firm will reduce output at its Los Bronces copper operation and trim iron ore at Kumba due to logistics constraints. Shares fell sharply following the guidance. | Core Nickel Corp. announced a non-brokered private placement of up to $350,000 via flow-through units to fund exploration work on its Halfway and Resting Lake nickel properties in Manitoba. It does not indicate any production changes, disruptions, or immediate supply impact; any effect on nickel prices is indirect and likely limited. | Singapore authorities approved Abaxx exchange and clearing house, enabling launch of the world’s first nickel sulphate futures contract, expected to begin trading in coming months and targeted for Q1 2024. The contract is physically settled via direct delivery. It is based on nickel used in EV batteries. | ArcWest and Interra Copper signed an earn-in agreement to explore ArcWest’s Rip porphyry Cu-Mo project in British Columbia, including staged funding, payments, and planned 3D induced polarization surveys. It outlines geology and past drilling but no near-term production, logistics, or inventory impacts for metals.

Indonesia believes its ban on exporting unprocessed nickel is boosting domestic value-added. The country has invested heavily in nickel smelting capacity, with downstream provinces showing double-digit growth. The ban has increased nickel-related export value substantially, suggesting improved supply concentration and near-term supportive conditions for nickel prices. | Nornickel’s 2023 has been difficult, though not described as a disaster. The article notes trimmed medium-to-long-term estimates and highlights rapid redirection of sales to Asia. By focusing on the company’s nickel revenue and market access rather than a specific disruption, near-term price pressure appears limited. | Aluminium prices fell to their weakest in over three months as speculative funds increased selling, driven by expectations of rising LME inventories ahead of the Third Wednesday prompt. Weak China data and a strong dollar pressured the wider metals complex, with copper and nickel also declining. | ValOre reported assays showing nickel-copper sulfide mineralization in all four 2023 drill holes at its Pedra Branca Salvador target, with grades including ~0.26–0.29% Ni and ~0.10–0.13% Cu over tens of metres. The company says further discovery could add Ni-Cu credits in future estimates. | ValOre Metals reported assay results from four drill holes at its Pedra Branca project, confirming nickel-copper sulfide mineralization with cited Ni grades up to 0.50% and Cu up to 0.18%. The company frames it as exploration upside for future resource estimates; no production or supply disruption changes are mentioned.

Copper prices rose in London as a weaker dollar improved currency buying power and tighter supply prospects next year were reinforced by recent mine closures/disruptions. The piece also cites China stimulus hopes supporting metals demand. Aluminium also gained, while nickel edged lower, suggesting mixed cross-metal performance. | Copper prices reversed earlier gains after U.S. CPI reinforced expectations that the Fed will keep interest rates higher for longer. A weaker outlook for early rate cuts supported the dollar, weighing on growth-sensitive industrial metals. The piece also notes nickel and aluminium price movements alongside copper, while supply disruptions remain supportive.

Copper prices fell after China’s Central Economic Work Conference readout disappointed investors by omitting large fiscal stimulus measures, worsening expectations for demand from the world’s top consumer. LME three-month copper and Shanghai January copper both dipped, while nickel also declined and aluminium rose modestly. | Nickel North Exploration announced amended and restated option agreement terms for selling interests in its Hawk Ridge nickel-copper project in Quebec to 1844 Resources. The amendment adjusts cash/deposit timing and share payments for staged option exercises up to 100% ownership. It is an exploration-deal update with no stated production changes. | IGO’s Ivan Vella-led IGO warned investors to expect another write-down on its Western Areas nickel assets after job losses began at the Cosmos nickel project. The company says the $1.3 billion acquisition value may be impaired again, indicating weakening nickel project outlook and potential supply uncertainty. | Aluminium prices fell to their lowest in nearly 15 months as oversupply expectations outweighed demand hopes. LME data showed funds’ net short positions at the highest since May 2020. The firmer U.S. dollar also pressured base metals, with aluminium weaker and copper and nickel also down. | Quebec Nickel Corp. says it has completed 2023 exploration at its Ducros Ni-Cu-PGE project in Quebec, drilling 17,815 metres in 50 holes and advancing a scoping-level metallurgical study. It reports discovery of large nickel-cobalt-bearing ultramafic intrusions and new targets from AI, with environmental baseline work started. | South32 has invoked force majeure at its Colombia ferro-nickel operation, signaling an interruption to production. Such disruptions can lower near-term ferro-nickel availability and tighten nickel supply expectations, which usually weighs on nickel prices over the first days of the event.

Panoramic Resources was placed into administration and its Savannah nickel mine is expected to be sold or recapitalised, with Trafigura positioned to take control. The article links the company’s trouble to nickel prices halving this year, equipment failure, and bad weather disrupting transport routes. | Panoramic Resources, operator of the Savannah nickel mine, has entered voluntary administration as nickel prices have fallen 44% over the past year. The firm says it will keep mining on a trade-on basis short-term. Analysts cite recession fears and increased Chinese-linked Indonesian nickel supply weighing on prices. | Panoramic Resources, an Australian nickel producer, has called in administrators as nickel prices hit three-year lows. The company cites repeated setbacks at its ageing Savannah project and years of poor acquisitions, reflecting continued financial stress in the nickel market. | Canada One Mining reported SGS lab results from an MMI soil survey at its Abitibi East project, identifying anomalous nickel and copper ion zones and planning follow-up surveys and electromagnetic work. The findings suggest potential nickel and copper mineralization, but no production or supply impact is stated. | Canada One Mining reported MMI soil survey results at its Abitibi East project, identifying anomalous nickel and copper ion responses in multiple target areas and planning follow-up surveys and drilling. While positive for potential future discovery, the release provides no production or supply-change details. | Copper rallied sharply, gaining 2.8% to around $8,565/t on Thursday, the biggest daily jump in nearly a year, driven by a weaker dollar and expectations of Fed rate cuts. The move was reinforced by mine closures and production cuts. Aluminium and nickel also rebounded on the macro sentiment shift. | Leading Edge Materials reports further high-grade Co-Ni-Au assay results from Gallery 7 at the Bihor Sud project in Romania, extending the mineralized zone by about 250 m to roughly 400 m. Samples show elevated cobalt and nickel grades (including 13% Ni), reinforcing exploration upside and supporting nickel sentiment. | Stillwater Critical Minerals announced drilling intercepted new “N series” sulfide-rich mineralization at its Stillwater West property in Montana, including copper and nickel sulfide. The company reports high-grade NiEq from a drill hole and plans expanded drilling in 2024, improving prospects for future copper and nickel supply. | Stillwater Critical Minerals says drilling at its Stillwater West (Montana) intercepted new “N series” sulfide mineralization, including 2.89% nickel-equivalent over 400.8m, and copper-nickel sulfides. While the discovery is supportive for future supply expectations, it offers no near-term production or inventory implications for copper or nickel prices.

London copper was steady after a sharp prior-session jump as dovish Fed expectations pressured the dollar near four-month lows. Three-month LME copper was almost flat, while January SHFE copper rose. In the same session, aluminium fell and nickel declined, though the week trend was mixed and copper ended stronger weekly. | The UK announced new sanctions restricting imports of certain Russian-made metals and banning exports of dual-use goods. Panoramic Resources also entered administration this week. | Indonesia’s leading political candidates are expected to maintain Jokowi’s nickel ore export restrictions. With Indonesia producing a large share of global nickel, continued export limits could tighten supply dynamics and support nickel prices near term.

Reuters reports copper eased on worries about weak Chinese demand, but sentiment was supported by mine closures/disruptions suggesting tighter supplies and supply concerns around Panama’s Cobre mine. It also notes aluminium fell after LME warehouse stock jumps, then rebounded. Nickel dropped as other base metals moved with risk appetite. | Canada Nickel said it extended the repayment date of a US$12 million loan facility with Auramet to January 18, 2024, paying an extension fee and issuing warrants. The update is intended to advance permitting, engineering, and financing/offtake initiatives during the extension period, supporting near-term project continuity. | The article title says a long-delayed Minnesota copper-nickel mining project won a court round after several setbacks. That outcome improves the project’s prospects to proceed, which can modestly raise near-term expectations for future supply and support copper and nickel prices. | Minnesota’s Court of Appeals upheld the MPCA air permit for NewRange Copper Nickel’s proposed copper-nickel mine and processing at the former LTV site in Hoyt Lakes. Environmental groups had challenged the permit, but the decision reduces regulatory uncertainty for development, with no immediate production timing stated. | Minnesota’s Court of Appeals upheld the state’s air permit for NewRange Copper Nickel’s planned copper-nickel mine at the former LTV site in Hoyt Lakes. The ruling removes a regulatory hurdle after environmental groups challenged the permit, but it doesn’t signal near-term production, limiting immediate price effects. | A court upheld the air permit for a proposed copper-nickel mine, lowering regulatory uncertainty. By improving the prospects that the project can proceed toward development and eventual production, the decision is likely to support market expectations for increased future supply. This can be bullish for copper and nickel prices. | A court upheld the air permit for the proposed NewRange copper-nickel mine, removing a permitting obstacle and lowering the risk of further development delays. The decision improves the project’s outlook and can affect near-term sentiment by increasing expectations of future supply for both copper and nickel.

Copper prices were largely flat as traders stayed cautious ahead of a Bank of Japan decision and assessed US rate-cut prospects. Strong Chinese refined copper production and rising imports weighed on the Yangshan premium. Aluminium weakened on rising LME inventories and nickel also declined. | Si6 Metals completed its first-phase drilling in Botswana, reporting high-grade copper-silver intercepts at Dibete and strong visual nickel-copper sulphide mineralisation at Maibele North, with assays pending in three to four weeks. The results are positive for resource potential, but no near-term supply impact is indicated. | Indonesia’s Adhi Kartiko Pratama (AKP) begins an IPO on Jan. 9 and expects proceeds from selling shares held by major investors. LX International will acquire a controlling stake and plans to expand nickel production capacity, signaling continued nickel supply efforts linked to EV battery demand. | Minnesota’s Court of Appeals affirmed an air emissions permit for the proposed NewRange copper-nickel mine near Babbitt, concluding regulators adequately considered possible expansion plans. The project remains delayed with other permits still contested, but the ruling reduces one near-term regulatory hurdle for copper and nickel supply expectations. | Minnesota’s Court of Appeals upheld a state agency decision granting an air emissions permit for the proposed NewRange/PolyMet copper-nickel mine. Environmental groups had argued “sham permitting” and possible future expansions. The permit win is partial, but it lowers near-term regulatory risk for potential copper and nickel output, subject to remaining court and regulator steps. | Silver One Resources announced additional staking at its Phoenix Silver Project in Arizona to cover areas tied to magnetic anomalies along a 30+ km porphyry copper belt. The company cites select rock samples returning >1% copper and plans further geophysical work and 2024 drilling. It’s an exploration expansion without immediate production changes. | Copper prices climbed as supply worries intensified: mine closures, falling LME-approved inventories, and a high share of cancelled copper warrants signaled more copper leaving the exchange. Traders also cited uncertainty around First Quantum’s Cobre Panama and production guidance cuts from Anglo American. | An Inter-American Court ruled Guatemala violated Indigenous Q’eqchi’ property and consultation rights for allowing a large nickel mine on tribal land. The court ordered an immediate halt to mining, required new legislation and a land title process within six months, and barred additional mining without community approval. | Premium Nickel Resources reports additional assays from underground drilling at the Selebi North Ni-Cu-Co sulphide mine in Botswana, including intervals with Ni and Cu contributions to NiEq. The company says intersections confirm down-plunge extension of mineralization beyond the historic resource, supporting future resource estimation, but no immediate supply changes. | Indonesia unveiled revised EV investment tax incentives allowing duty- and VAT-free imports of EVs (CBU/CKD) and extending local content timelines. The policy aims to accelerate EV market development before domestic production ramps up, which could raise expectations for battery metal demand, supporting nickel sentiment over the near term. | A Minnesota appeals court upheld a key air emissions permit for the proposed NewRange copper-nickel mine near Babbitt, after regulators reconsidered potential expansion scenarios. The decision is a win for Glencore/Teck, but other major permits remain unresolved, keeping near-term output uncertain for copper and nickel markets. | A Minnesota appeals court affirmed regulators’ decision granting an air emissions permit for the proposed NewRange copper-nickel mine near Babbitt, overturning claims of “sham permitting.” The project is still stalled by other court and regulatory issues, but the ruling lowers uncertainty for potential future copper and nickel supply.

Nickel 28 reported Q3 results for its 8.56% stake in the Ramu nickel-cobalt operation in Papua New Guinea. Ramu produced 8,979 tonnes of contained nickel in MHP, with cash costs net of by-product credits falling 29% to $2.77/lb and distribution proceeds supporting strong quarter cash generation.

Minnesota’s Court of Appeals upheld NewRange Copper’s air emissions permit, rejecting claims the company overstated the scale of its proposed copper-nickel mining operation. But other permits remain suspended or withdrawn, including wetlands approval, so the mine still can’t be built without further changes, limiting immediate supply impact. | Nickel prices fell on subdued consumption alongside increasing production and higher inventories in exchange warehouses. LME and SHFE contracts declined, reflecting continued surplus conditions in the global nickel market. The report notes expanding supply from downstream investments and cathode output while demand has not improved. | Reuters reports copper prices pulled back as bullish investors reduced positions ahead of the holidays after copper failed to hold above recent highs. Nickel was little changed, weighed by growing supply and weak consumption, while aluminium fell modestly. Overall, the moves reflect short-term positioning and demand/supply expectations.

Shanghai aluminium (Feb) and zinc rose to November highs as markets priced potential further Chinese rate cuts and expected Red Sea shipping delays. Traders cited delayed refined aluminium shipments via Suez. The same session saw copper at multiweek highs and nickel higher, reflecting broad metal risk-on and logistics supply friction. | Reuters reports aluminium prices in London rose to the highest since Nov 6 on Friday, driven by supply concerns after a fuel depot blast in Guinea (key bauxite supplier) raised fears of bauxite shortages for Chinese alumina smelters. Chinese alumina futures hit record highs, alongside a weaker dollar, technical buying and logistics disruption risk via the Red Sea/Suez. | A DOE-led study presents a cost-effective solid-state method to produce smooth single-crystal, nickel-rich NMC811 cathodes, scaling to kilogram levels with industry collaboration. Copper prices were steady to slightly higher as a weaker U.S. dollar supported demand and tight global raw-material supply concerns persisted after recent mine closures. LME aluminium fell, while nickel rose on the same day’s market tone. Shanghai prices were mixed, with copper little changed and aluminium slightly up. | Hundreds of Indonesian nickel workers rallied to demand stronger occupational safety, threatening a strike. A Sunday fire at a nickel smelter furnace killed workers and led to an operation suspension while police investigate. Other plants in the Morowali park run normally, but safety scrutiny is increasing. | Hundreds of workers protested outside a Chinese-funded nickel-processing plant in Indonesia after an explosion killed at least 18 and injured dozens. Unrest centers on safety, furnace maintenance, and working conditions at the Morowali Industrial Park, a key nickel hub. | Hundreds of Indonesian nickel workers rallied demanding stronger workplace safety standards after a fire killed 18 people. Labour groups threatened strikes if demands aren’t met. A spokesperson said operations at the Morowali industrial park, the world’s biggest nickel ore producer, have not been affected and talks are ongoing. | Workers in Indonesia protested after an explosion killed 18 at a Chinese-funded nickel processing plant in Morowali. Smelter operations were suspended during investigations, while unions demanded stronger health and safety compliance and threatened a strike. The manpower ministry pledged tighter safety laws amid ongoing hospital treatment. | Hundreds of nickel workers in Indonesia rallied for improved safety after a fire killed 19 employees. Police investigations suspended operations at one smelter at the Morowali industrial park, though other plants reportedly run normally. Authorities also pledged stronger safety enforcement, and unions threatened further strikes if demands aren’t met. | Indonesia’s police are investigating a deadly explosion at the Chinese-owned Indonesia Tsingshan Stainless Steel nickel plant in Sulawesi. Operations at the facility were temporarily halted while investigators probe the incident. The fire killed 18 workers and injured 40+, adding disruption risk for near-term nickel output in a key producing region. | Base metals rose in London on improved Chinese industrial profits. Aluminium hit an eight-month high on technical buying by CTAs and supply concerns after a Dec. 18 blast at Guinea’s main fuel terminal handling bauxite-related inputs. Copper and nickel also increased as the dollar eased and Indonesia-related supply focus strengthened. | An explosion at a Chinese nickel smelter in Indonesia killed at least 19 workers and injured dozens, with fire and hot steam disrupting operations during routine maintenance. The incident highlights safety concerns in Indonesia’s rapidly expanding nickel industry, raising risks of near-term supply disruption for nickel used in stainless steel and EV batteries. | A fire at Indonesia Tsingshan’s nickel smelter in Morowali has killed 18 people and prompted authorities to investigate, with smelter operations paused. Indonesia is a major nickel producer and export ore bans push feed into smelting. The shutdown threatens near-term nickel supply, pressuring prices lower. | A furnace explosion at a Chinese-owned nickel plant (PT Indonesia Tsingshan Stainless Steel within PT IMIP) in Indonesia’s Sulawesi killed 18 workers. Police ordered the plant to halt operations until an investigation is completed, indicating near-term disruption to nickel smelting supply while authorities examine negligence and causes. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. closed a $1.35 million flow-through financing at $0.17 per share to fund exploration, development, and advancement of its Kenbridge Nickel Project in Ontario, plus it issued shares for debt. The announcement is related to nickel activity but does not indicate immediate production or supply changes.

Copper prices in London were steady near a five-month high as a softer U.S. dollar—driven by expectations for potential Fed interest rate cuts—supported dollar-priced metals. Three-month LME copper was almost unchanged while SHFE copper edged higher. LME aluminium was flat and nickel slightly lower, reflecting mixed but mostly mild macro effects. | An explosion at a Chinese-owned nickel smelter in Indonesia’s Sulawesi killed at least 18 workers and prompted police to order the plant to stop operating pending investigation. The incident is part of Indonesia-China nickel processing expansion, potentially disrupting near-term nickel output and supporting prices. | Copper retreated from a five-month high as investors took profits ahead of year-end, with concerns about Chinese growth/property. Aluminium fell after LME inventories built to six-month highs. Nickel was the largest decliner as LME nickel inventories rose to levels not seen since July 2022.

A fire at an Indonesian nickel smelter in the Morowali industrial park killed at least 19 workers and injured others. The incident occurred during furnace repairs, and unions threatened potential strikes over safety demands. Production risk and operational disruption at a major nickel processing hub are likely near-term bearish for nickel prices. | Avebury Nickel Mine avoided an abrupt shutdown and continued operating safely and productively after Mallee Resources entered administration and receivership. Management added drilling equipment and trucks while a sale process for the going concern is reportedly close, suggesting steadier near-term nickel supply. | London copper rose as the dollar weakened, while expectations of tighter copper concentrate supply and thin global inventories supported sentiment. The piece cites firm Chinese demand despite property weakness. LME aluminium and nickel were mostly flat or slightly lower, suggesting the strongest near-term upside is for copper rather than aluminium or nickel. | Avebury nickel mine continues operations under a new owner as Mallee Resources moved into administration and receivership this year. Management says the mine remained safe and productive, added drilling and trucks, and expects sale completion soon. Restarted production last year after years in care and maintenance. | The IMF warns that after the Russia-Ukraine conflict pushed metals higher, nickel and copper (along with other EV battery minerals) could rise further. It echoes IEA demand forecasts and notes supply-chain concentration risks, such as cobalt dependence on Congo, which may tighten expectations for critical metals. | The IMF warned that following Russia-Ukraine-related increases, prices of metals used in EV batteries—especially nickel and copper—could rise further. It cited IEA forecasts of sharply higher long-run demand and highlighted concentration risk in key producing countries, implying potential supply-chain fragility. | Reuters reports copper dipped on a firmer dollar but expects a modest yearly rise supported by healthy Chinese demand and hopes for U.S. rate cuts, alongside tighter concentrate supply. Nickel fell after LME inventories hit 18-month highs, signaling excess supply. Aluminium edged higher with smaller market impact than copper. | Core Nickel Corp. has closed an oversubscribed flow-through private placement, issuing 4.35 million units for about C$392k to fund exploration work on its Halfway and Resting Lake projects and other programs. The company is a junior nickel explorer, so the news is only indirectly connected to near-term nickel pricing. | Agnico Eagle Mines acquired 19.6 million units of Canada Nickel Company at $1.18 per unit, including common shares and warrants. London copper edged higher as China manufacturing improved and investors looked for fresh stimulus, while SHFE copper benefited from low warehouse inventories. Aluminium and nickel were weaker on the firm dollar. Overall, the piece signals supportive demand expectations for copper but mixed impacts for other industrial metals. | Canada Nickel completed a C$34.7m flow-through private placement at C$1.77 per unit, with Agnico Eagle becoming about a 12% shareholder. Management says proceeds will fund continued advancement of its Timmins Nickel District and related initiatives. The deal may mildly improve sentiment on near-term nickel supply prospects. | Canada Nickel’s investor presentation highlights the Crawford nickel sulphide project with detailed economics (NPV/IRR), expected nickel production, and a mid-2025 permitting target. The findings support prospects for continued expansion, but they do not imply near-term changes to metal supply. | Canadian North Resources reported borehole geophysical survey results indicating the West Zone at its Ferguson Lake project extends further down-dip by more than 200 metres at depths around 650 to >850 metres. The mineralization is described as Cu-Ni massive sulphides plus PGE zones, but no near-term production or supply changes were announced. | GFNI said it has secured a new agreement with Baosteel for up to 1.5 million WMT of nickel ore for 2024, priced monthly based on prevailing market values. The ore will come from GFNI subsidiaries operating nickel mines in the Philippines, reinforcing nickel ore demand for Asia-Pacific buyers. | Agnico Eagle acquired a 12% minority stake in Canada Nickel via a flow-through offering, supporting the development of the Crawford nickel-cobalt project in Ontario. The deal is an investment in critical minerals, but it does not directly change near-term nickel supply or demand conditions. | Agnico Eagle Mines bought a minority stake in Canada Nickel through a flow-through offering, purchasing 19.6 million units for about $23.1 million. Canada Nickel says proceeds will fund work to advance its Timmins Nickel District and the Crawford Nickel Project, supporting development of future nickel supply. | Global Ferronickel said it will sell 1.5 million wet metric tons of nickel ore to Baosteel under renewed purchasing arrangements. The ore is sourced from its mines in Surigao del Norte and Palawan, with monthly pricing tied to prevailing market nickel prices, supporting nickel-related supply expectations in Asia.

Copper prices eased as the U.S. dollar firmed on reduced expectations for early, aggressive rate cuts and as Chinese buying slowed with seasonal weak demand and strong domestic output. LME three-month copper and SHFE February fell, alongside a sharp drop in the Yangshan premium. Aluminium also slipped; nickel rose. | Copper fell to a two-week low as the U.S. dollar rose and China’s industrial demand worries intensified after weak December manufacturing PMI data. The article also shows softer LME prices for aluminium and nickel on the same risk-off, demand- and FX-driven backdrop. | An explosion at a Chinese-owned nickel smelter in Morowali, Sulawesi killed at least 19 workers and injured dozens. Operations were halted by police while investigations continue after a furnace accident involving molten metal residue and a subsequent fire. Indonesian nickel miner AKP launches an IPO and after listing will sell 60% to South Korean buyer LX International via Energi Battery Indonesia. Copper rebounded on Friday, supported by a weaker dollar and hopes tied to the Fed’s potential rate cuts, though gains were limited as December minutes suggested rates may stay high longer. Supply tension from low stocks in China supported prices. LME aluminium and nickel also rose modestly. | Copper prices edged up as supply concerns and thin Chinese stocks supported spot premiums, offsetting changes in investor expectations for U.S. Fed rate cuts. LME aluminium was stable and nickel slightly higher, but the main focus was copper’s supply-tightening backdrop and macro-driven risk appetite. | Reuters reports copper at a three-week low as a stronger U.S. dollar and reduced odds of early Fed rate cuts pressured LME prices. Copper fell on the LME as the firm U.S. dollar pressured dollar-priced metals and traders awaited Chinese data for demand signals.
