Drilling will resume, supporting positive sentiment for future copper supply potential. | Nickel 28 reports Ramu’s 2023 nickel production of 33,604 tonnes, above design capacity, despite earthquake-related downtime. For 2024, MCC plans a US$33 million upgrade requiring a 30-day shutdown in September, with guidance for about 30,000 tonnes of contained nickel in MHP.  | Administrators for Panoramic Resources in Western Australia have stopped mining at the Savannah nickel mine, with operations halting within days and about 140 company roles plus contractor jobs at risk. The decision is linked to further weakening in the nickel price, but the shutdown reduces near-term nickel output. | The article says Chinese nickel investments in Indonesia are slowing as falling LME nickel prices, shrinking project economics, and FEOC/IRA rules threaten profitability and eligibility for US tax credits. Panoramic Resources has shut its Savannah nickel mine in Western Australia, placing 340 jobs at risk. Administrators say the decision follows falling nickel prices that made the operation unviable. The closure is expected to tighten or curtail nickel supply in the near term, though the cited cause reflects bearish pricing pressure. | Copper rose as a softer U.S. dollar reduced the cost of dollar-priced metals, following bets on imminent Fed rate cuts. Reuters also reported LME aluminium and nickel edging higher in the same session. Investors awaited upcoming U.S. inflation data for further base-metals volatility. | Tsinghsan founder Xiang Guangda is credited with expanding nickel processing in Indonesia, boosting ferro-nickel alloy exports. Indonesian ferro-nickel exports rose sharply in the first five months, driven by increased processing capacity that helps lower-cost producers compete against higher-cost Australian nickel supply. | Aluminium prices rebounded on Tuesday after recent lows as LME-registered stocks fell from a seven-month high, suggesting reduced nearby supply and some consumer buying. The piece also notes wide copper contango and rising nickel stocks, reflecting mixed short-term balance across metals. | Australia’s nickel miners are preparing for more job losses after Panoramic ends production. The article says nickel faces a massive oversupply forecast this year and points to worsening Canberra–Indonesia tensions. Panoramic Resources says it will make 140 on-site roles redundant at its Savannah nickel mine in Western Australia after entering voluntary administration, citing a low prospect of near-term turnaround. The World Bank’s forecast of slower growth adds demand concerns. Aluminium edged up modestly, while nickel slipped slightly, reflecting mixed risk sentiment beyond copper. | Australia’s Resources Minister Madeleine King supports a pricing structure that pays a premium for nickel meeting higher ESG standards in Australia versus “dirty nickel” from Indonesia. The move is aimed at rewarding compliant producers amid industry turmoil and job losses, potentially lifting expectations for cleaner nickel pricing. | Copper prices fell as a steady U.S. dollar and worsening demand sentiment, reinforced by World Bank warnings on global and China growth, pressured risk appetite. NickelX Limited says it will begin drilling at the Dalwallinu nickel-copper-PGE project in late January 2024. The update is primarily operational and relates to exploration activity and deal mechanics for an option arrangement. No immediate production, reserve, or supply disruption figures are provided. | Nickel Industries warns Australian nickel miners will not receive a “green premium” as the sector becomes uncompetitive. The CAD$1.8m fully funded program targets historic Ni-Cu mineralization and uses BHEM alongside drilling to identify new conductive zones. Assays are expected in late Q1 2024. | Infinico Metals started a ~1,000 m diamond drilling program at its Nicobi Ni-Cu-Co project in Quebec, targeting historic mineralization and new down-dip/along-strike zones using borehole EM. Assays are expected in late Q1 2024. The release does not indicate production, only exploration testing. | Alaska Energy Metals told residents it has positive results from prospecting its Nikolai Project near Paxson and plans to spend at least $15 million on further nickel sampling and surveys in 2024, with exploration resuming in June. Samsung SDI will invest US$18.5m in Canada Nickel and gain rights tied to the Crawford nickel-cobalt project, including an option for a 10% equity interest and offtake of nickel-cobalt production upon a construction decision. The deal highlights battery end-user demand and supports sentiment for future nickel supply from a long-life mine. | Canada Nickel shares rose after Samsung SDI agreed to invest $18.5 million for nearly a 9% stake and to subscribe for 15.6 million shares. Samsung SDI also gains the right to purchase a 10% equity interest in nickel production from the Crawford project in Timmins, Ontario, supporting sentiment for nickel supply. | Samsung SDI agreed to invest US$18.5 million for an 8.7% stake in Canada Nickel and will also have rights to buy an additional 10% stake in the Crawford project in Ontario. The deal includes access to nickel-cobalt production for battery/EV supply chains, supporting nickel demand expectations. | Samsung SDI agreed to invest US$18.5 million for an 8.7% stake in Canada Nickel and received options to buy additional stake rights tied to the Crawford nickel-cobalt project in Ontario. It will also gain rights to future nickel-cobalt production, reinforcing perceived nickel supply support for EV supply chains. | Canada Nickel announced a subscription agreement with Samsung SDI to invest US$18.5 million for shares at C$1.57, giving Samsung SDI about 8.7% ownership. The firms also position the investment as a long-term partnership to advance the Crawford nickel sulphide project for EV battery supply chains, supporting nickel sentiment.

London copper held steady as a weaker U.S. dollar supported prices, while investors awaited China data for demand outlook. China’s unchanged policy rate and softer pre-holiday replenishment weighed, but mine-side disruption could reduce refined output. LME aluminium edged up, while nickel fell slightly amid mixed moves across metals. | First Quantum Minerals will suspend mining at the Ravensthorpe nickel operation in Western Australia for up to three years and cut 30% of staff, while continuing to process ore stockpiles. The decision follows weaker nickel prices and is intended to preserve viability until nickel prices recover. | Samsung SDI invested $18.8m for an 8.9% stake in Canada Nickel, a Toronto-based junior developing Ontario’s Timmins-Cochrane camp/Crawford Nickel sulphide project. The move is intended to secure critical nickel inputs for EV battery production, boosting confidence in future nickel supply demand. | Copper prices fell after the dollar strengthened and Chinese demand worries resurfaced when the central bank left key rates unchanged.
