Copper rallied above $10,000/ton on LME as traders revived expectations of U.S. Fed rate cuts after softer labor-market data. The move coincided with gains in LME aluminium and nickel, supported by the view that lower rates improve growth and weaken the dollar, boosting metals demand. | SMM reported Jinchuan nickel premium at 1300–1500 yuan/ton and higher nickel bean prices (140,700–141,500 yuan/ton), up 800 yuan/ton versus the prior day. The nickel bean versus nickel sulfate spread widened to about 1,327 yuan/ton, with purchases still restrained but prices staying elevated.

Indonesia says its nickel reserves are sufficient to expand processing and keep high-grade supply available for years, while industry groups urge a moratorium on new smelters to counter a glut. With nickel already hit by oversupply and producers squeezed, expansion expectations are bearish for nickel prices. | Indonesia’s investment ministry says nickel reserves are sufficient for expanded processing through both stainless-steel (high-grade NPI) and EV battery feedstocks, with smelters able to be built for different products. Miners have urged a moratorium amid oversupply that previously drove nickel prices down, but no decision is made yet. | Ivanhoe Electric and BHP signed a three-year U.S. exploration alliance, funded initially by $15 million from BHP. The program will use the Typhoon geophysical system and inversion software across Arizona, New Mexico, and Utah to identify potential projects for joint ventures targeting copper and other critical minerals including nickel. No near-term production impact is stated. | The piece outlines global nickel balances shifting from shortage (2016–2021) to surplus (from 2022 onward), with faster supply growth via refined nickel, NPI and nickel sulfate than demand. It highlights Indonesia’s expanding nickel-intermediate output and stainless-steel exports, mainly for NPI-based production, suggesting capacity increases but with no clear immediate price shock.

Copper in London edged higher on improved China trade data and property-support measures that may bolster near-term demand, with gains capped by a firmer US dollar. LME aluminium also rose slightly, while nickel was mostly flat. SHFE showed copper slightly down, nickel lower, and aluminium higher. | SMM reports national nickel pig iron output fell in April due to disruptions (Eid al-Fitr timing, slower Indonesia RKAB approvals, weak ore procurement, higher costs, and some maintenance). First Quantum halted production at Ravensthorpe after losses, LME inventories keep building, and Indonesian output remains discounted. Falling nickel-linked stainless surcharges are weighing on Outokumpu and other mill earnings, reinforcing bearish near-term sentiment.

The Philippines’ environment minister said the U.S. and China are among countries interested in collaborating on mining in the country, focused on nickel. She urged the Philippines to boost nickel processing capacity, which supports battery production for electric vehicles. The announcement is supportive for nickel sentiment but not specific on near-term output. | Globe Advisor says nickel prices rebounded from early-year lows, citing “overblown” Indonesian supply-glut claims, lower-than-reported nickel inventories, and renewed demand narratives tied to EVs and energy storage. It notes concerns about the traceability and sustainability of Indonesian nickel, but frames the near-term outlook as improving. | Tertiary Minerals received Zambia EPA EPB approval to begin exploration at its Mupala Copper Project, including soil sampling and surface mapping. The licence sits near major copper and nickel mines and adjacent JV interests involving Anglo American. The news supports incremental copper sentiment but remains early-stage with no production commitments. | Fenix Nickel Co. supports El Estor miners’ association in seeking U.S. government help to reopen Guatemala’s Fenix nickel mine and reemploy over 1,700 workers. The group asks for expedited U.S. export-license issuance, arguing restarting production would reduce local hardship and illegal migration pressures.

The report says shipping costs and the end of the Philippine rainy season lifted CIF laterite nickel ore prices slightly, while Indonesian nickel ore premiums remained high due to slow RKAB approvals and smelter restocking. Despite active shipments and inventory factors, it expects nickel ore prices to hold steady next week. | SMM reports refined nickel inventories in China’s bonded area at 4,460 mt, slightly lower, and expects further declines. UK/US sanctions on Russian nickel widened LME–SHFE price spreads, boosting export profits and encouraging movement of nickel plates into bonded areas. However, downstream demand remains sluggish, leaving social stocks higher. | Nickel sulfate prices are temporarily steady after a slight rise, with most trades at the lower end of the 30,500–31,000 yuan/mt range. Limited MHP supply and smelters’ losses keep discounts stable at 80% in Q2. April output is tighter than expected, so near-term prices likely remain stable. | Canada Nickel announced robust early metallurgical test results from the Mann Northwest property, reporting nickel recoveries of 58–59% and nickel/iron concentrate grades meeting expectations. Management says the results confirm process transferability from its Crawford project and supports continued variability and locked-cycle testing. The news is development-oriented, not production. | SMM reports May 11 battery-grade nickel sulfate at 30,948 yuan/ton, up 220 yuan/ton day-on-day. Spot supply is tight, but downstream procurement is weakening; nonetheless, strong nickel prices after the holiday have lifted raw-material costs. Mill sentiment remains firm and buyers’ acceptance rises, pushing short-term nickel salt higher. | SMM reports the May 13 average price of 8–12% high-nickel pig iron (ferronickel) at 967.5 yuan per nickel point, up slightly on the day. Indonesia’s nickel-ore approvals lag, smelter raw-material shortages persist, and domestic output remains constrained, while demand is cautious, leaving ferronickel stable and strong short term. | France is backing a planned private nickel (and cobalt) refinery near Bordeaux to strengthen Europe’s EV battery supply chain and reduce reliance on China. The €300m project would process 20,000 tonnes of nickel per year from 2028, covering part of France’s nickel needs by 2030, amid ongoing approvals. | Green Bridge Metals entered a definitive agreement to option an 80% interest in the South Contact Zone copper-nickel exploration portfolio in Minnesota. Acquisition depends on due diligence, regulatory approvals, and minimum $3 million financing, expected by May 30, 2024. It supports future exploration but not near-term output. | The briefing says China is preparing major stimulus financing via a ¥1tn bond sale, coinciding with copper surging back toward US$10,500/t. It also references LME restrictions on new Russian aluminium, copper and nickel to comply with sanctions, tightening supply and adding upside support.

Stellantis is reported to be in talks with Vale and Huayou Cobalt about investing in an Indonesian nickel smelter/HPAL facility to produce battery-grade nickel. The discussions aim to secure supply for Stellantis’ EV expansion, potentially affecting near-term expectations for nickel availability and demand. | Anglo American’s strategy update said it is exploring options to offload its steelmaking coal, nickel, and platinum businesses to become a simpler organisation. The move follows the prior rejection of BHP’s raised takeover offer. The report does not specify timing or production impacts, making near-term nickel effects uncertain. | Anglo American said it is reviewing options that could include demerging or selling its steelmaking coal, nickel, diamonds and platinum units amid a hostile takeover bid from BHP. It will explore options for its nickel mines, but the announcement does not indicate immediate changes to nickel production. | Copper prices hit their highest level since April 2022, rising on base-metal sector fund inflows tied to expectations of U.S. rate cuts, supply disruptions, and green-energy demand. Despite weaker-than-expected China Q2 consumption due to high prices, LME copper and SHFE copper still advanced, while aluminium rose slightly and nickel eased. | Anglo American announced a strategic review that could include selling or demerging parts of its nickel business as it fends off BHP’s takeover bid. It plans to explore options for its nickel mines, but provides no clear, immediate production changes, reducing direct short-term price pressure on nickel. | Copper prices hit their highest since April 2022 on the back of technical signals drawing fresh fund buying, despite reports of weak physical demand in China. LME three-month copper rose modestly. Aluminium edged up despite higher warehouse inventories, while nickel slipped slightly. | Indonesia’s outgoing government added five state-supported industrial parks for nickel processing, including HPAL plants targeting 120,000 tons of nickel in MHP per year, alongside plans for nickel and cobalt sulphate for EV batteries. The incoming administration pledged to continue the downstream policy, boosting nickel-related investment expectations. | SMM reports May 14 prices for 8–12% high-nickel pig iron around 968.5 yuan per nickel point, up slightly. Indonesia’s nickel ore approval and smelter raw-material supply remain constrained, while domestic smelter restarts are limited by costs. With tight inventory and cost support, ferronickel is expected to stay stable and strong short term, despite softer stainless demand. | SMM reported battery-grade nickel sulfate prices edging up on May 14, with spot still tight but downstream procurement activity weakening. Supply-demand conditions are described as weak and market activity low. Higher post-holiday nickel prices pushed nickel salt raw material costs up, keeping salt mill pricing sentiment supported despite loss thresholds. | Anglo American announced a major restructuring, exiting diamonds, platinum and coking coal and aiming to become a copper-focused company after pressure from shareholders and a BHP takeover bid. It also plans to sell or shutter its relatively small nickel business in Brazil. Investor sentiment shifts toward copper prominence as shares fell.

London copper rose 0.3% as a weaker U.S. dollar made dollar-priced commodities cheaper ahead of key U.S. inflation data that could steer Fed rates. CME U.S. copper hit a record on robust demand and fund buying. LME aluminium edged down slightly and nickel ticked up. | Western Metallica reports confirmatory rock-chip sampling at its Turmalina copper-molybdenum project in Peru, with level 5 sampling averaging 2.9% Cu and associated Mo values. The company highlights exploration potential at a past-producing mine. No nickel or aluminium developments or immediate supply effects are cited. | Nickel 28 reported Q1 2024 operating performance from its Ramu nickel-cobalt joint venture, including 8,282 tonnes of contained nickel production and higher nickel sales versus last year. It also cited lower net cash costs and guidance for full-year costs and production near 30,000 tonnes of contained nickel in MHP. | Stellantis is reportedly in talks with Vale Indonesia and Huayou Cobalt to invest in an Indonesian HPAL plant to process low-grade nickel into battery-grade metal for EV expansion. While deal size is unfinalised, increased nickel processing investment expectations in a China-dependent supply region are supportive for nickel. | SMM reports the SMM 8–12% high-nickel pig iron price at 968.5 yuan/nickel point, unchanged day-on-day. Indonesian nickel ore approval delays and weak smelter output persist, while domestic operating rates remain low due to cost constraints. Intraday saw higher volume and reduced open interest. The company plans EM surveys to target massive copper-nickel sulphides, citing historical grades up to 6.1% Cu and 1.35% Ni. | GT Resources announced a C$1.8 million flow-through private placement to fund up to 2,000 meters of drilling and downhole EM surveys at its Canalask nickel-copper project in Yukon. The program aims to test targets supporting historical grades (~1.35% Ni, samples up to 6.1% Cu) and expand the resource. | GT Resources announced a C$1.8 million flow-through private placement to fund July drilling and downhole EM surveys at its Canalask nickel-copper project in Yukon. The company plans to test a defined EM target and expand a historical resource estimate. The funding is exploration-focused and not production.

China’s steel and nonferrous lobbies criticized U.S. tariff hikes on certain Chinese imports, citing damage to global steel trade. Biden’s package includes steel and aluminum products, prompting China’s Commerce Ministry to promise countermeasures. The policy risk can shift aluminum trade flows and weigh on near-term prices. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. acquired additional contiguous claims at the Kenbridge Nickel Project and expanded its land position. The company also started 2024 baseline environmental studies to support provincial and federal permitting for advanced exploration and eventual mine development, but provides no production or near-term supply change. | Argus began publishing the INI (Indonesian Nickel Index) in partnership with PT Indeks Komoditas Indonesia, providing new Indonesian export nickel benchmarks. It aims to address market pricing dilemmas amid rising Class II volumes, growing EV battery demand, and shifting production/consumption hubs. No immediate supply-demand shock is cited. | Tartisan Nickel highlights its Kenbridge Nickel Project, citing a preliminary assessment report and management confidence in expanding mine life. The company frames the project as a cost-effective, modest-capex nickel source to capture growth in the nickel market tied to EVs and batteries. The news is supportive for nickel sentiment.

Nickel prices jumped sharply after political unrest in New Caledonia. Reuters reports police reinforcements as France moves to regain control of Noumea. With New Caledonia a major nickel mining region, investors feared supply disruptions, alongside funds inflows, pushing LME nickel up about 4.5% and SHFE nickel up over 5%. | France has declared a state of emergency in New Caledonia after a week of violent protests and is sending additional security forces. The unrest has disrupted nickel production, involving miners including Eramet. With New Caledonia a key global nickel supplier, the supply shock is likely to weigh on nickel prices in coming days. | The report says SMM’s 8–12% high-nickel pig iron averaged 973 yuan per nickel point on May 17, up from the prior day. It attributes firmness to Indonesia’s slow nickel-ore approvals, ongoing Indonesian smelter raw-material shortages, limited domestic output, and rising short-term demand ahead of steel mills’ storage cycle. | Nickel is reported to jump as unrest disrupts output from a major (No. 3) nickel miner in New Caledonia. Output cuts tighten short-term supply expectations for nickel, typically supporting higher settlement prices in the coming days as market participants price in reduced mine output. | Nickel surged to a nine-month high after unrest in New Caledonia disrupted a significant share of global nickel mine supply. Copper rose to a 25-month peak following China’s “historic” support steps for its failing property sector, lifting industrial metal demand expectations in the near term. | Nickel rallied to multi-month highs after riots in New Caledonia, a key nickel-producing region, raised immediate supply-risk expectations. Copper surged to a 25-month peak following China’s “historic” support for its ailing property sector and better factory data, increasing near-term demand sentiment. | Premium Nickel Resources reported new assay results from drilling at the Selebi North underground mine in Botswana, highlighting high-grade nickel and copper (plus cobalt) mineralized zones and continued down-plunge potential. The results support future resource estimation and studies, but they do not indicate imminent production or supply changes. | The article says big players are securing domestic nickel supplies despite a 2023 price drop, citing battery cathode demand and restrictions on top nickel ore exports. It details Alaska Energy’s major Nikolai resource upgrade, a proposed Canadian nickel processing plant, and a $19bn GM–LG Chem cathode materials deal with US-linked sourcing. | U.S. stocks were steady as markets reassessed expected Fed rate cuts. Despite rising Treasury yields, LME copper jumped to a 26-month peak after China announced new property-sector support. Nickel surged to its highest since Aug 2023 amid unrest in nickel producer New Caledonia. | Global markets firmed as investors weighed expectations for possible Fed rate cuts. Copper surged to a 26-month peak after China announced new support for its property sector. Nickel hit the highest since August 2023 as unrest in New Caledonia boosted prices, with LME nickel up sharply on the day.

Amid continued violence in New Caledonia, the local nickel industry is described as being in turmoil. The archipelago is also noted to have an economic situation worsened by months of falling nickel prices. New Caledonia holds about 20% to 30% of global nickel reserves, making unrest potentially disruptive. | Copper surged to record highs on Monday after China announced “historic” property support steps and industrial output beat expectations. Momentum/investment buying and a Comex short-squeeze lifted global prices, despite weak physical import premiums into China. LME and SHFE aluminium and nickel also rose sharply, reflecting broad base-metals strength. | Panicked traders are covering shorts as a growing supply squeeze lifts expectations for metals. Copper prices on the LME surged above $11,000/t for the first time, with NY and Shanghai also at record highs. Nickel spiked nearly 8% on Friday, reinforcing bullish near-term momentum. | Copper surged to record levels on the LME and COMEX, up on short position covering and speculative fund buying, with some Chinese players contributing. Analysts still cite China fundamentals and high ShFE inventories as concerns. Aluminium also rose modestly and nickel gained, suggesting supportive momentum across industrial metals. | Indonesia’s investment official said the government offered Tesla a plan to build an EV battery precursor/cathode factory in nickel-rich Indonesia, which Musk indicated he would consider. Indonesia supplies about 40% of global nickel. Any move to expand battery-related processing could boost expectations for nickel demand. | SMM reports nickel spot prices fluctuated upward on May 20 amid macro and fundamentals. Jinchuan and Russian nickel premiums are given, while nickel “beans” rose, with the beans-minus-sulfate spread reported around 9,264 yuan/ton and inversion versus nickel salt intensifying. Spot market difficulties are noted. | Power Nickel announced high-grade assay results from a Lion Discovery drill hole, including strong copper grades (e.g., up to 17.26% Cu) and measurable nickel (up to ~0.37% Ni), plus PGE and precious metals. It signals potential expansion of a nickel-copper resource, but remains exploration with no immediate production impact.

Copper prices eased from a record high as a firmer U.S. dollar weighed on LME pricing and traders paused after a strong rally. The report points to weaker Chinese import premiums and higher SHFE stocks. LME aluminium and nickel also fell, indicating broader metal softness despite copper’s earlier momentum. | Indonesia’s investment minister says the government proposed to Tesla’s Elon Musk building an EV battery and cathode-precursor factory in Indonesia. The proposal is framed around Indonesia’s large nickel reserves and its ambition to raise nickel supply share. The potential downstream battery investment supports near-term nickel demand expectations. | Copper prices edged higher near record levels, but gains were tempered by subdued physical demand. Chinese import premiums fell to zero and SHFE inventories remained elevated, suggesting fundamentals lag speculative momentum. The piece also notes small moves in aluminium and nickel, indicating mixed broad metal sentiment rather than a clear driver. | Core Nickel reports 2024 winter drill results from its Halfway Lake project, finding wide packages of nickel-bearing sulfide mineralization in ultramafic rocks, with nickel tenors up to ~17% in assays. The company plans additional VTEM and aeromagnetic work to refine targets for potential economic nickel deposits. | Nickel Pig Iron Daily Review reports SMM 8–12% high nickel pig iron averaging 977.5 yuan/nickel point, up on the day. Indonesia’s nickel ore approvals lag, raw material shortages persist, and smelter output remains weak with low operating rates. Demand from steel mills may rise near the procurement cycle, supporting near-term stability. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulfate prices rising to around 32,004 yuan/ton. Limited nickel-salt supply, low finished-product inventories, and high raw-material coefficients deepen profit inversion at salt plants, pushing them to raise prices strongly. Downstream demand is weaker but forced to accept higher prices due to precursor constraints and replenishment needs. | The report says Indonesian nickel ore premiums were stable and Philippine laterite ore transactions stayed active, with freight to China still high. It notes China’s nickel ore port inventories rose 210,000 wmt WoW, suggesting more supply entering ports, while NPI smelters showed little added buying interest, keeping prices steady. | SMM data show refined nickel inventories in China’s bonded zone fell to 4,160 mt last week, and the publication expects further declines. It attributes the tighter bonded supply to riots in overseas nickel raw material areas and rising nickel prices, while domestic social stocks rose slightly on weak downstream demand. | Shanghai Non ferrous Metals reports nickel sulphate prices rebounded to 31,000–31,500 yuan/mt. MHP supply remains tight due to downstream smelter losses and continued overseas hydrometallurgy project shutdown concerns, keeping the MHP discount elevated. Weaker orders and inventory pressure cap gains, but cost support should keep prices 31,500–32,000 yuan/mt. | Copper has surged, but the article warns the rally may be vulnerable. It cites heavy long speculative positioning, negative Chinese demand signals (Yangshan premium), strong contango, and higher-for-longer rates supporting the USD. It also mentions Cobré Panama closure/resumption uncertainty affecting supply tightness and possible downside correction risk. | Copper prices eased from Monday’s record high as investors questioned whether the rally—supported by speculative short covering—can be sustained amid subdued physical demand. China demand softness and refinery output rising also weighed. The piece also flags aluminium up on lower LME warehouse stocks and nickel down on the day. | DMCI Holdings says it is exploring new copper (and gold) mining assets and possibly an additional open-pit coal mine. It also notes nickel projects are currently not operational due to pending permits, with some expected by year-end. DMCI recently agreed to acquire Cemex Philippines.

Copper prices trended lower as a steady U.S. dollar weighed on demand and China’s physical market weakened after record highs. Higher SHFE warehouse inventories reinforced bearish sentiment. LME aluminium and nickel also edged down, reflecting broader risk-off moves in industrial metals as markets awaited Fed minutes. | New Caledonia’s second week of deadly riots has included road blockades and an airport shutdown, prompting a 12-day French state of emergency. She linked the situation to Alcoa’s recent alumina refinery shutdown at Kwinana, citing similar consequences for supply. | Violent unrest in French Pacific New Caledonia—home to 20–30% of global nickel reserves—has raised concerns of shipping and production disruptions. Nickel prices on the LME jumped sharply, with the article citing supply-risk fears, sanctions on Russian nickel, and broader critical-material shortage outlooks. | Prospect Ridge Resources announced new high-grade copper-bearing quartz vein showings on its Holy Grail property near the Copper Ridge zone, including samples up to ~14.8% Cu. The geology targets magmatic sulfide Ni+Cu±PGE mineralization, which may support bullish sentiment for nickel and copper. | Alaska Energy Metals secured an option to acquire the Bambino nickel-copper-PGE property in western Quebec and will begin exploration with soil sampling and a VTEM survey. The geology targets magmatic sulfide Ni+Cu±PGE hosted in gabbro sills. The announcement is exploration-stage, implying limited immediate supply effects. | Riots in New Caledonia, a major nickel producer, have halted mining and damaged SLN infrastructure, forcing mines to idle and the processing plant to run at minimum capacity. The disruption supports higher LME nickel prices by reducing output and potentially eroding a global surplus, despite some offset from growing Indonesian supply. | New Caledonia produces about 7% of global nickel, but the article cites near-term disruption risks: metallurgical plants teetering on collapse, Glencore stopping financing for a loss-making mine, and Goro reducing output after a tailings dam leak. With a global nickel glut already weighing on prices, disruptions add bearish sentiment. | Class 1 Nickel announced an updated mineral resource estimate for the Alexo North nickel sulphide deposit, with indicated resources rising to 42,600 tonnes at 0.92% Ni and a higher contained nickel figure versus the 2020 estimate. The company plans further drilling to upgrade and expand resources across the project. | Class 1 Nickel announced an updated NI 43-101 mineral resource estimate for the Alexo North nickel sulphide deposit, combining new and historical drilling data. The update significantly raises indicated resources versus the 2020 estimate and supports continued exploration at the Alexo-Dundonald Nickel Project. This is generally supportive for nickel sentiment. | Civil unrest in New Caledonia has halted mining and damaged/curbed output at Societe Le Nickel’s operations, while Koniambo idled its mine and processing plant. The disruption supported a rally in London Metal Exchange nickel prices to nine-month highs as analysts warned reduced supply could erode a global surplus. | SMM reports nickel pig iron prices (SMM8–12% high-nickel) holding steady but under pressure from heavier upstream sentiment. Limited Indonesian ore approvals and persistent raw-material shortages keep smelter output weak and operating rates low. Demand from steel mills may rise near the procurement cycle, supporting stable-to-strong ferronickel in the short term. | Nickel prices continued to ease in the morning, with the spot market difficult to trade. Jinchuan premium was 600-800 yuan/ton (avg 700), while the Russian nickel premium ranged -600 to -300 yuan/ton (avg -450). Downstream demand did not rise materially, keeping sentiment cautious despite recent-high levels. | Glencore and Rusal extended their long-term aluminium supply contract into 2025 despite previously low traded volumes. The extension is enabled because Rusal is not directly targeted by Western sanctions. The report also notes an LME ban on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel from April 13, reinforcing near-term supply-tight sentiment. | Deadly unrest in New Caledonia has raised concerns about nickel supply disruptions from a region holding 20–30% of global reserves. LME nickel prices jumped from about $18,510/t in early May to above $21,275/t, with broader worries of future shortages for battery and industrial materials. | Nickel prices surged after political riots in French-ruled New Caledonia, where the country is a top global nickel producer. The unrest led to deaths, airport closures, curfews, and troop reinforcements, raising fears of supply and logistics disruption. Nickel jumped about 5% then added further gains to a multi-month high.

Violent unrest in France’s Pacific territory of New Caledonia has followed constitutional voting changes and a 12-day state of emergency. New Caledonia supplies roughly 20–30% of world nickel reserves and most local exports, raising fears of disrupted production. The article says global nickel prices have surged. | Copper prices fell after hawkish Fed minutes supported a firmer U.S. dollar, making dollar-priced commodities more expensive. The drop was reinforced by weakening Chinese demand as earlier price gains reduced end-user consumption and lifted inventories. LME and SHFE aluminium and nickel also declined, reflecting broad risk-off pressure. | Riots in New Caledonia have halted nickel mining operations and damaged/limited processing at SLN, reducing output to minimum levels. The company already paid interim dividends in 9M 2023 and says it will not increase debt for dividends. Nornickel is a major nickel and copper producer. | Copper on the LME fell for a third straight session due to profit-taking and consumers shunning near-record prices. The unrest is tied to New Caledonia’s nickel-dependent economy and an industry downturn, with stalled rescue talks. Disruption risks likely weigh on nickel prices soon.

Copper prices were nearly unchanged as investors reassessed the rally that recently pushed LME prices to record highs, supported by fund buying and a U.S. Comex short squeeze but offset by dampened Chinese physical demand. LME aluminium and nickel fell slightly, indicating limited immediate directional impact beyond copper’s cooling momentum. | Talon Mining begins nickel exploration in Michigan’s Upper Peninsula, backed by about $20 million in U.S. federal funding. The company acquired a large land package and cites prior findings of up to 7.4% nickel, aiming to drill once targets are identified. It frames results for domestic EV-related supply chains. | Reuters reports BHP is racing to secure Anglo American’s copper assets in Chile and Peru, with Anglo investors seeking higher cash/price or a simpler deal structure. The copper mines targeted are expected to produce ~532,000 tons this year, but the dispute is about valuation and execution details, not immediate output changes. | Macron postponed voting reform in New Caledonia after yielding to rioters, in a territory described as nickel-rich. The unrest could disrupt local mining or logistics feeding nickel production, tightening near-term supply expectations. That scenario is generally supportive for nickel prices over the next days to four weeks.

Gladiator Metals reports assay results from previously unassayed historic drill core at the Cowley Park prospect, extending and confirming high-grade copper skarn continuity (notably several meters grading around ~1–2% Cu) with mineralization open under cover and at depth. It does not indicate imminent production changes impacting copper markets immediately. | Australia and the EU signed a critical minerals memorandum intended to smooth investment in new projects and build momentum for a “green premium” market. The article specifically cites nickel among targeted resources, which should support expectations for nickel demand and potentially tighten supply outlooks over coming weeks. | Canadian Copper Inc. said New Brunswick approved the transfer of the Murray Brook mining lease and title. The company frames the event as supporting a potential Caribou restart by providing a nearby ore resource. The release cites spot pricing used in its resource valuation, but no immediate production impact is stated. | Fathom Nickel reported new drill results from the Gochager Lake project, including multiple high-grade nickel intercepts with associated copper and cobalt, plus strong BHEM conductor evidence suggesting expansion to depth and along strike. The update is exploration-focused and does not indicate imminent production changes for nickel or copper supply. | Electric Royalties reports updates across its royalty portfolio, including Zonia copper-oxide progress toward a feasibility study and Kenbridge nickel project baseline studies and permitting steps. While these developments may support longer-run supply expectations, the article does not indicate near-term production changes, implying minimal immediate impact on copper and nickel prices. | The Fastmarkets podcast interview focuses on battery raw materials, including nickel. It explains how Indonesian nickel matte can feed nickel sulfate for China’s battery sector and notes that mixed hydroxide precipitate (MHP) production expansions depend on whether nickel economics remain viable. The piece links supply chains to sentiment but gives no definitive short-term nickel price direction. | Unrest in New Caledonia since mid-May has halted nickel mining and processing, stopping operations at SLN and Prony, idling Koniambo, and depleting ore reserves. Fuel blockages and possible shipping risk premiums further threaten supply. Given New Caledonia’s large global reserve share, the disruption should support higher nickel prices.

Copper prices rose as expectations of stronger Chinese demand improved, supported by new property-sector measures and an IMF upgrade to China’s growth outlook. Gains were capped by a steady/firm U.S. dollar and higher Chinese inventories. Aluminium and nickel also ticked up on the broader metals sentiment. | SPC Nickel reported high-grade channel sampling from its West Graham Ni-Cu project in Sudbury, including results of 0.94% Ni and 0.44% Cu over 17 metres and 0.62% Ni and 0.31% Cu over 38 metres. The company says results correlate with prior drilling and support development potential, but no immediate production impact is stated. | Copper Lake Resources reported winter drilling results from its Marshall Lake copper-zinc-silver VMS property in Ontario, highlighting high-grade copper and zinc intercepts and a persistent EM conductor with the deposit remaining open at depth. The update supports potential resource expansion but does not indicate near-term production. | SGS says it was contracted by The Metals Company to test and optimize onshore processing flowsheets producing high-purity nickel sulfate from seafloor polymetallic nodules. Bench-scale work also targets cobalt sulfate and mentions copper cathode production from nickel matte, supporting future supply expectations for nickel battery chemicals. | LME approved the first Indonesian refined-nickel brand (“DX-zwdx”) for delivery, adding a new source of Class I nickel. With Russia’s Norilsk constrained by sanctions and LME stocks rising to a two-year high, the shift toward Indonesian/Chinese deliverable supply is likely to weigh on nickel prices.

BHP published its Statement 1087 compliance assessment report for the Mt Keith Satellite Project, covering 28 Dec 2020 to 31 Dec 2021. The report states BHP Nickel West was compliant with all conditions, describing ongoing satellite mining operations as an extension to the Mt Keith Nickel Mine. No near-term operational disruption is indicated. | Shanghai aluminium hit a two-year high on early trade, supported by supply constraints linked to western sanctions on Russian metals, tight availability (including higher premiums to Japan), and robust demand from solar and EV sectors. Meanwhile, copper and nickel slipped as a firmer U.S. dollar weighed on base metals priced in dollars. | LX Pantos and Indonesian shipping firm KSA plan a joint venture to transport coal and other minerals from Indonesia, including nickel. KSA already moves large volumes and the venture expects to secure barges and expand vessels and transshipment facilities in stages. This could improve Indonesian nickel export capacity and near-term supply expectations. | Copper fell 2.7% on the LME as funds unwound bullish positions amid concerns that high interest rates will damp metals demand, especially from China. A firmer dollar and higher yields added pressure. Aluminium and nickel also declined, reflecting broader industrial-metals risk-off sentiment. | Eramet’s CEO said SLN’s New Caledonia nickel furnaces need ore supplies in the coming days because mining has been halted by riots and the Doniambo plant is running at minimum capacity. Without re-supply within about 10 days, operations face risk, tightening near-term nickel supply expectations. | Norilsk Nickel expects the global nickel market to remain in surplus, forecasting roughly 100,000 metric tons for 2024 and 2025. It says low-grade nickel will dominate the surplus. The company attributes persistence to supply growth from new Indonesian facilities and rising offerings of nickel and chemical compounds. | Anglo American said it has started formal processes to divest coal and explore options for its nickel operations after rejecting BHP takeover bids. Management indicated nickel mines could be mothballed and processes will follow regulatory steps. Such actions may tighten near-term nickel supply expectations and support nickel prices.

Weaker US economic data revised GDP down and reinforced “higher-for-longer” rate concerns, dragging US risk assets. The report says base metals traded lower. It also mentions Rio Tinto keeping a New Zealand aluminium smelter running with new electricity supply agreements, but overall the tone is bearish for industrial metals. | Copper rebounded on the LME, but gains were capped by profit-taking after May 20 record highs and by weaker physical demand in China. China’s May manufacturing PMI fell below 50. Aluminium and nickel also rose on the day, reflecting broader market moves rather than a single new metal-specific shock. | Reuters reports copper under pressure after China’s May manufacturing PMI fell below 50, worsening demand expectations for the top consumer. Despite earlier speculative gains and a modest monthly rise, profit-taking could push copper back below $10,000. LME aluminium eased, nickel edged up slightly.  | Amaroq Minerals reported updated results from South Greenland exploration, confirming a copper-molybdenum porphyry system and identifying 24 additional copper targets for its 2024 field season. The company also references copper-nickel sulphide targets within the same belt, but provides no near-term production or supply impacts. | Posco Group broke ground on a KRW 1.5 tn project with CNGR to build a nickel refining plant and a precursor cathode-material plant for EV batteries. The refining unit targets 50,000 mt/year of ~99.9% nickel and the precursor unit 110,000 tons/year. Production starts in 2026 to reduce import dependence. | POSCO and China’s CNGR held a groundbreaking ceremony for POSCO CNGR Nickel Solution’s nickel refinery and a C&P Energy precursor plant in Pohang. The joint ventures are intended to secure stable raw materials for rechargeable batteries, potentially supporting improved near-term expectations for nickel supply.

London copper rose 1.1% on supportive China and US data: China’s private manufacturing survey showed the fastest activity growth in about two years, and US inflation stabilization kept the Fed’s planned rate cuts on track. A tighter China copper import discount suggested improved physical demand, lifting copper and supporting aluminium and nickel. | Cobalt Blue Holdings describes virtual tour coverage of pilot-scale feedstock testing at its technology centre for a planned cobalt-nickel refinery in Australia. The company reports high cobalt extraction recovery and ongoing optimisation toward producing cobalt and nickel sulphate. Policy support for critical minerals is cited, but commercial timelines are not provided. | Nickel 28 Capital Corp. announced it received a management cease trade order from the Ontario Securities Commission after a voluntary application tied to delays in filing audited annual financial statements. The order restricts certain insiders from trading the company’s securities but does not affect non-insider shareholders’ trading. No production impacts are stated. | A Conference Board of Canada outlook says Sudbury’s economy is being weighed by soft nickel prices. It links the fall in nickel to worries about electric-car demand and, importantly, rising Indonesian supply, noting nickel prices are about half of March 2022 levels. Lower prices have hurt Vale and Glencore profits.

The article claims Power Nickel in Quebec has delivered major “miracle drill results” pointing to large deposits of strategic metals, explicitly including nickel and copper. It frames the discovery as a potential supply solution amid critical metal shortages, geopolitical risk, and high tariffs affecting EV-related supply chains, supporting bullish market sentiment. | Power Nickel reported high-grade drill assays from its Lion Zone, including intervals with 9.88% copper over 5.59m and 0.49% nickel over the same run, plus broader polymetallic results and CuEq context. The news supports project development but does not imply immediate supply changes. | Power Nickel reported high-grade assay results from its Lion Zone drill program, including 9.88% copper and 0.49% nickel over 5.59m (plus additional Cu and Ni-rich intercepts). The news supports project potential but provides no production, permitting, or supply/inventory change, so near-term metal price impact is likely limited. | Copper prices fell below $10,000 per tonne on the LME, down 1.5%, driven by weak demand expectations from China and persistently high Shanghai physical inventories. Softer U.S. data and rate-cut expectations supported a stronger dollar, adding near-term pressure. Aluminium and nickel also edged lower. | Canada Nickel said it completed infill drilling at its Deloro nickel project near Timmins, reporting nickel-bearing intervals and noting an initial resource estimate expected in July 2024. The update improves the project’s resource definition but does not indicate any near-term production or supply impact on nickel markets. | Talon Metals said it extended nickel-copper mineralization in Minnesota’s Raptor Zone by drilling 110 meters more strike, including 8.91 meters of nickel mineralization with 1.77 meters of massive sulphides in a new hole. Assays are pending, and exploration will continue with step-out drilling in 2024. | Talon Metals reported that follow-up drilling at Minnesota’s Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project intersected 8.91 meters of nickel mineralization, including 1.77 meters of massive sulphides, extending the Raptor Zone strike length. Assays are pending, but results support expectations of additional nickel-copper resources. | Talon Metals says it extended the Raptor Zone nickel-copper mineralization by 110 meters and intersected 8.91 meters of nickel mineralization, including 1.77 meters of massive sulphides, with assays pending. The update supports potential future nickel and copper supply, but it is exploratory. | Talon Metals reported that new drilling in Minnesota’s Raptor Zone extended nickel-copper mineralization and intersected 8.91m of nickel mineralization including 1.77m massive sulphides, with assays pending. The company plans further step-out exploration, signaling potential for a larger mineralized system but no immediate output change.

An Indonesian economist argues that nickel downstreaming largely benefits China, with most added value flowing abroad. The piece also alleges continued nickel ore flows to Chinese processors despite Indonesia’s 2020 export ban. These points could influence near-term nickel supply expectations, but no direct immediate price driver is quantified. | Copper prices fell after a strong rally as weak physical demand in China weighed on the market. LME three-month copper slipped below $10,000 and SHFE July copper dropped, while SHFE and LME copper stocks rose. Aluminium and nickel also eased, suggesting broadly bearish sentiment in base metals. | Copper prices near a one-month low as real demand fails to match strong electrification-driven expectations. Three-month LME copper slipped below $10,000, with sellers paying China to destock. Funds reportedly sold copper and bought aluminium. Aluminium was also down on the day; nickel fell alongside broader metals weakness. | Merdeka Battery Materials (MBMA) cut 1Q output at its Sulawesi SCM mine due to wet weather and limited contractor equipment, with limonite and saprolite supplies falling. Its RKEF smelters also produced less nickel in NPI and matte. Guidance for 2024 is steady, but near-term supply is lower. | Norilsk Nickel is upgrading its Nadezhda Metallurgical Plant in Norilsk, including replacing a flash smelting furnace and implementing a Sulfur Program to cut SO2 emissions. The work uses demolition robots and spider cranes to improve safety and efficiency, and management expects production volumes to rise after repairs. | Alaska Energy Metals said TSX Venture Exchange accepted an option agreement granting it the right to acquire 100% of the Bambino nickel-copper property in western Quebec and will commence exploration programs. The release is an early-stage acquisition/exploration update without any near-term production or supply impact. | Alaska Energy Metals said TSX-V accepted an option agreement giving it the right to acquire 100% of the Bambino nickel-copper property in western Quebec and to start exploration. The update is about future project development, with no immediate production or inventory impact for nickel or copper.

Copper futures rebounded above $10,000/t as the dollar softened on expectations of Fed easing and as signs of firmer China demand emerged. LME three-month copper rose 1.6%. LME aluminum and nickel also advanced, with Reuters citing renewed copper buying activity in China. | Silver One says it found additional copper and silver showings at its Phoenix Silver Project in Arizona, with select samples up to 7.79% Cu. The area is near Freeport McMoRan’s Miami copper mine and BHP’s Ocelot porphyry project, and drilling is targeted for Q3 2024. | Copper prices rebounded 1% to around $10,027/ton ahead of the ECB’s expected first rate cut, supported by optimism that lower rates may improve growth. Gains were capped by concerns over sluggish demand, especially in China where inventories rose and producer operating rates fell. LME aluminium and nickel also rose. | EV Nickel reported assay results from 148 surface samples at its Shaw Dome Project’s CarLang B target, finding that most samples grade around 0.24% Ni or higher, with grades up to 0.34% Ni. The company plans further summer sampling, but no production timeline is provided.

London copper eased as softer-than-expected physical demand from China weighed on prices. Markets also awaited U.S. jobs data for guidance on when Fed rate cuts may begin, lifting expectations of cuts after recent labor-market cooling signals. Other LME metals, including aluminium and nickel, were modestly higher. | China’s May unwrought copper imports rose 15.8% year-on-year to 514,000 tonnes, beating expectations despite weak physical consumption. Imports were up 17.4% from April. Analysts cited potential additional Russia-origin flows into China following Western sanctions in April, with DRC shipments also improving. | London copper fell after mixed China trade data. Better exports contrasted with slower imports and higher-than-expected unwrought copper imports, raising concern that Chinese inventories will grow; SHFE stocks hit a four-year high. Broader macro cues from an upcoming US jobs report and Fed cut expectations also influenced pricing; aluminium and nickel moved mildly. | Copper on the LME is poised for a third weekly decline, breaking below $10,000/t after mixed Chinese trade data. Better exports but slower import growth and higher unwrought copper imports raise concerns about rising inventories. Traders also await a U.S. jobs report for rate-cut timing, weighing on growth-linked metals. | Canada Nickel’s CEO Mark Selby argues the world demand for nickel is resilient, with growth mainly driven by stainless steel and some battery use. The piece also updates progress on exploration, permits, and plans for processing plants at Crawford, and suggests nickel prices may ramp later this year.

Copper fell to its lowest in over five weeks on a firm dollar and shifting expectations for Fed rate cuts after stronger U.S. jobs data. Physical demand constraints were noted, including a continued discount in China import premiums. Aluminium declined about 1%; nickel was nearly unchanged. | Copper rebounded from five-week lows, rising 0.6% after U.S. jobs data-driven selloff shifted focus to improving fundamentals. Traders expected support from China returning from holiday and cited better China PMI activity, while concerns remain from rising warehouse inventories and weaker Yangshan premium. Aluminium and nickel saw only modest moves. | Copper rebounded 0.6% after dipping to the lowest since May 2, supported by shifting focus to improving fundamentals and expected Tuesday buying as China returns from holiday. The prior sell-off was triggered by strong US jobs data and a stronger dollar. China stocks remain high and premiums weak. Aluminium and nickel saw modest declines/gains. | Power Nickel begins a 2024 summer drilling program of 20 holes and over 8,000 metres at its Nisk property in Quebec, targeting extensions of high-grade polymetallic sulphide lenses. The program focuses on both the polymetallic Lion Zone (PGE/Pd/Au/Ag/Cu) and nickel-rich Nisk Main. No production or shipments are mentioned.

Base metals fell as the yuan weakened, reducing Chinese buying power. LME/SHFE copper dropped, while aluminium eased amid high inventories, weak physical demand, and a wider LME cash-to-three-month discount reflecting non-tight nearby supply. Nickel moved less consistently, amid broader downside pressure. | Nornickel is reconstructing the pyrometallurgical line at its Nadezhda Smelter by dismantling flash smelting furnace No. 2 and installing a new unit. The smelter processes nickel concentrates and produces copper-nickel matte sent for further processing. The repair is planned to take ~60 days while improving reliability. | Indonesia’s government is reviewing whether to terminate permits for RKEF smelters producing ferronickel and nickel pig iron. Officials said policy aims to reserve limited nickel ore for EV-battery nickel products. Permit termination risk could reduce current nickel supply, affecting short-term nickel price expectations. | Orecap updates on Cuprum’s Thierry copper project in Ontario, highlighting recent flow-through financing, commissioning of remaining assays from Summer 2023, completion of an updated NI 43-101, and historical resource/PEA details. The project’s CuEq calculations assume copper and nickel prices, but no new supply or production impact is indicated. | Copper prices hit a seven-week low as the weaker yuan reduced purchasing power for China and as LME copper inventories rose after fresh inflows. The piece also describes a contango structure with no imminent shortages, while aluminium and nickel fell amid broader base-metal pressure. | Premium Nickel upsized a C$15m private placement to about C$27.5m to fund exploration and development of its Botswana assets, including the Selkirk nickel-copper-cobalt-PGM mine acquired in 2022. The offering is fully subscribed at C$0.78 per unit, with proceeds also for general corporate and working capital.

POSCO Group and CNGR broke ground in Pohang on a nickel refining joint venture producing 50,000 tons/year of 99.9% high-purity nickel and a precursor plant producing 110,000 tons/year. The complex is meant to supply cathode materials via POSCO Future M, with mass production slated for 2026. | Tsingshan plans to register and list an additional 50,000 tonnes of nickel cathode on the LME in the second half of 2024, following its earlier Dingxing listings. The move is aimed at boosting deliverable high-grade supply amid rising LME nickel inventories and growing surplus that weigh on nickel prices. | Tsingshan plans to register another 50,000 metric tons of nickel cathode on the LME in the second half of 2024, after listing 50,000 tons in Q2 from its Dingxing plant. The company expects to apply for 50,000 tons from another smelter this year, adding potential supply. | Kalla Group says it has concluded phase one of its nickel smelter and will begin gradual operations this year. Phase two of a Rp 10 trillion nickel processing and refining facility in South Sulawesi is targeted for completion in 2027, adding capacity and increasing ferronickel and nickel sulfate output, supported by renewable power. | Copper rebounded as the dollar steadied ahead of U.S. CPI and the Fed decision. Traders cited expectations of a dovish statement and lower currency pressure supporting dollar-priced commodities. LME copper rose 0.8% from recent lows, while aluminium was slightly lower and nickel flat, reflecting broad industrial-metal reaction. | New Caledonia’s nickel processing is effectively shut after weeks of unrest. Eramet’s SLN runs smelter furnaces at minimum capacity due to disrupted ore logistics, while Prony halts both its plant and nearby mine for security. Mining is suspended and a rescue package is uncertain, keeping output constrained. | Norilsk Nickel says sanctions have cut revenues and disrupted payments, deliveries and pricing, limiting copper plant sulfur-equipment upgrades. Separately, the US and UK have banned Russian copper, nickel and aluminium from LME/CME trading. Norilsk plans to move some copper smelting to China later, but the trading bans are immediate.

An “over-night” market note focuses on US CPI driving risk-on sentiment, but the key metals link is nickel-specific: reports say Indonesia is considering terminating permits for Rotary Kiln Electric Furnace (RKEF) smelters. That raises near-term supply risk for nickel refining, supporting expectations of firmer nickel prices. | Power Nickel Inc. plans a flow-through offering of up to $20 million to fund exploration on its Nisk property in Quebec, aimed at expanding its high-grade nickel mineralization. The financing is expected to close before end-June and supports drilling over the next 12–18 months, which may boost nickel sentiment. | Ardea Resources says its mineralised neutraliser (MN) testwork has improved nickel-cobalt inventory by about 16% at the Goongarrie Hub project and received a $1.1m federal grant. A Japanese consortium also committed funds for a definitive feasibility study, supporting development progress that may help nickel supply sentiment. | Copper prices paused after rebounding, pressured by the Fed’s delayed rate-cut timeline, which steadied the U.S. dollar. Reuters notes profit-taking and highlights rising LME inventories in Taiwan and South Korea. LME aluminium and nickel also fell on the same macro-driven risk/FX move. | Draganfly was selected by First Atlantic Nickel to deploy drone-based survey technology to explore the Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland, targeting awaruite (a nickel-iron alloy). The stated goal is to rapidly identify prospective zones to support 2024 exploration and drilling, potentially strengthening future nickel supply expectations. | Macquarie’s Jim Lennon says global nickel prices likely hit a floor as Indonesian nickel ore supply tightens after slower approval of mining permits and RKAB quotas fall short of demand. He expects excess to narrow, with nickel support from stronger steel demand and improving battery-sector growth. | First Atlantic Nickel selected Draganfly drones for aerial and magnetic geophysical surveys at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland. The drone-based sensing aims to identify high-priority nickel (awaruite) targets and speed exploration ahead of a 2024 drilling program, but it does not change near-term supply. | Fastmarkets’ nickel market round-up discusses the ongoing “relief rally” in LME nickel during H1 2024, but highlights resistance near the 50-month/50-week moving averages and the $20,000/t psychological level. It expects short-term rebounds to attract buyers, while maintaining a broader bearish tendency into mid-2024. | GT Resources completed a C$1.84m non-brokered private placement (flow-through shares) and mobilized exploration drill rigs for the Canalask copper-nickel project in the Yukon. The program targets EM Maxwell plate and downhole surveys to locate massive sulphide mineralization, including historical nickel and up to 6.1% copper assays.

Kevin Rudd criticized the London Metals Exchange for not supporting new nickel standards to counter China’s cheap dumping. He warned the practice could trigger a 20% reduction in global nickel supply sources, with officials suggesting current “doing business as usual” is harmful for industry and geopolitics. | Macquarie analyst Jim Lennon said global nickel prices may have hit a floor. He expects a recovery supported by strong demand from the steel sector and improving supply dynamics, as Indonesia’s slowdown in nickel ore production permits reduces the existing ore surplus. | Nickel prices on the LME edged up on Friday but were set to fall for a fourth straight week as LME inventories rose. Stock build is linked to Chinese producers ramping high-grade cathode output in Indonesia and China that can be delivered on the LME. | Copper prices fell as a stronger dollar and rising LME-registered inventories signaled a supply surplus, widening contango. Nickel dropped to its lowest in over two months, pressured by higher LME and SHFE stocks and increased China high-grade cathode output. Aluminium also eased amid the same bearish complex tone. | Reuters reports copper prices slipping and nickel hitting a two-month low, driven by a firmer U.S. dollar and rising LME inventories suggesting a supply surplus. Copper LME stocks jumped 28% in a month. Nickel is pressured by high warehouse stocks and China cathode output increases. Aluminium also fell. | Power Nickel announced up to a $20m flow-through offering to fund exploration at its Nisk property in Quebec, targeting eligibility for Canada’s 30% critical mineral exploration tax credit. The raise involves issuing 16m flow-through units at $1.25 with warrants. Closing is pending TSX Venture approval.

China’s primary aluminium production rose 7.2% in May to the highest level in nearly a decade, with output also up year-to-date. Producers cited strong demand growth prospects for solar and EVs and speculative buying. LME and Shanghai aluminium prices hit multi-month highs, implying bullish near-term pressure. | Copper fell to its lowest in almost eight weeks as weak Chinese industrial data clouded demand expectations. LME three-month copper slid to about $9,631/ton and LME copper stocks rose. The same China-focused demand worries coincided with declines in aluminium and nickel prices. | Indonesia’s energy and mineral resources ministry will investigate a second work accident at PT Indonesia Tsingshan Stainless Steel’s nickel processing plant in Morowali. Two workers were scalded during furnace slag cleaning, and a prior explosion killed 18 workers. The probe and possible sanctions raise near-term nickel production uncertainty. | The piece is an interview with Lifezone Metals’ CEO about the company’s Hydromet technology and its Kabanga Nickel Project in Tanzania. It claims the process could unlock new LME-grade nickel and also copper supply, but offers no near-term operational updates. A feasibility study is due by Q3 2024. | Cornish Metals signed a binding agreement to sell its 100% interest in the Nickel King and Opescal Lake Ni-Cu-Co properties in Canada to Northera for up to C$8m, with staged payments and Northera shares after listing. The deal is intended to refocus on the company’s tin project. | EV Nickel Inc. reports commencement of a 2024 diamond drill program on high-priority nickel targets including Langmuir #2 and targets within the Shaw Dome property, alongside broader geophysical and metallurgical work for its CarLang trend and existing nickel deposit. The update is exploration-focused with no production or supply impact stated.

Fortune reports Indonesia’s $5 billion nickel supply deal with Tesla and the government’s push to capture more of the nickel value chain via export restrictions. It highlights Indonesia’s large nickel reserve and refining buildout, but notes environmental risks and weaker EV demand as headwinds. | LME Clear has postponed planned updates to its margin models, including value-at-risk add-ons, while it continues a review of lessons from the 2022 nickel market blow-up. Market participants say the CCP is balancing margin outcomes for arbitrage trades versus corporate metals hedging. Immediate effects center on nickel trading conditions. | Copper inched up after falling to an eight-week low as bargain hunters supported bids, with LME three-month copper rising slightly. The rebound was capped by weaker-than-expected China industrial output and weak import premiums, while copper inventories rose. Aluminium and nickel also fell, reflecting subdued overall demand sentiment. | Talon Metals began drilling in Michigan’s Upper Peninsula at its nickel-copper properties, near the Eagle Nickel Mine and Humboldt Mill. The company is following historic high-grade targets and has partial funding support from the US Department of Defense. The release supports ongoing US nickel exploration rather than immediate production. | Talon Metals began exploration drilling at the Roland target on its Michigan nickel-copper properties, near the Eagle Nickel Mine and Humboldt Mill. The firm frames the work as follow-up to historic high-grade intercepts, partly funded by a U.S. Department of Defense grant, but provides no production or reserve updates.

Copper rose on the LME and SHFE, supported by supply worries including Anglo American’s planned pause of processing at Chile’s Los Bronces, while China demand concerns and higher exports capped upside. Soft U.S. dollar conditions also supported prices. Nickel and aluminium also edged higher as sentiment improved. | Saudi Arabia’s Vision 2030 is expected to lift nickel demand through new steel plants, EV/battery manufacturing and broader infrastructure “giga-projects,” areas that account for most nickel consumption. In parallel, investments such as Manara Minerals’ stake in Vale aim to raise nickel output, but demand growth is emphasized as the key driver for near-term market expectations. | Intrepid Metals reported additional near-surface drill intercepts at its Corral Copper property in Arizona, including multiple high-grade copper zones. The news may marginally affect sentiment around future copper supply, but it is exploration-only with no immediate production or trade/inventory impact. Nickel and aluminium are not addressed.

Orecap/Cuprum announced new step-out and infill drill results at the Thierry copper project, highlighting long intervals with high CuEq grades (including near-surface and deeper extensions). The resource also contains nickel, though Ni recoveries need metallurgical improvement, supporting a generally bullish outlook for copper and a secondary nickel read-through. | Orecap/Cuprum reported results from five additional drill holes at the Thierry copper project, highlighting long intercepts with copper-equivalent grades and reiterating the K1 open-pit inferred resource. The deposit also contains nickel, but the release is exploratory and does not indicate imminent supply changes. | Copper prices edged slightly higher on hopes of improved Chinese demand after recent declines, alongside supply concerns. Gains were limited by a stronger U.S. dollar and rising LME stockpiles to the highest level since January. China’s easing remains constrained, while aluminium and nickel also logged modest moves. | Vale said asset reviews and investments at its Sudbury and Salobo mines could lift 2026 copper output about 5% above its baseline scenario and raise nickel production by around 10%. The guidance suggests improved future supply, which can support bullish sentiment in copper and nickel markets. | Vale said investments and an asset review could lift its 2026 output. Copper production is projected to be about 5% higher than its baseline, with nickel production about 10% higher, citing potential gains from Sudbury and Salobo mine improvements. The update supports more favorable supply expectations. | Vale is set to unveil a $3.3 billion investment plan aimed at boosting production of copper and nickel. The move suggests higher supply expectations for both metals, which can influence short-term market sentiment depending on the timing and scale of output increases relative to demand and inventories. | Vale unveiled a $3.3 billion plan aimed at boosting output of copper and nickel. The announcement is directly tied to changes in near-term supply expectations for both metals, which can feed into market pricing over the next several days to a few weeks as production capacity plans are digested.

Copper futures were down on Friday due to a stronger dollar but were poised for a first weekly gain in five as physical demand picked up after prices hit a two-month low. LME copper found support around $9,500-$9,600/ton. Aluminium was nearly flat and nickel slightly lower. | Copper prices resumed their downtrend as concerns grew about surplus supplies and sluggish demand in China. LME three-month copper fell 1.5% and SHFE stocks surged, reinforcing an oversupply overhang. The Reuters piece also reported easing aluminium and nickel prices amid generally weaker industrial metal sentiment. | Vale plans to invest $3.3bn to expand copper and nickel production capacity in Brazil and Canada, targeting copper output around 500,000t by 2028. The program centers on upgrades at Salobo and Sossego mines and aims to boost productivity and lower costs, implying higher future supply and potential price pressure. | Vale plans to invest $3.3bn over four years to expand copper and nickel capacity, targeting about 500,000 tonnes of copper output by 2028 through upgrades at Brazil’s Salobo and Sossego mines. It also aims to lower costs via productivity improvements, but the timeframe limits immediate price impact. | The article says Saudi Arabia is ramping base-metals production and usage under Vision 2030. Copper futures inched lower as traders weighed a firmer dollar and concerns over dull demand from China. The piece cites rising SHFE copper stocks to 322,910 tons, suggesting ample supply. BASF said it is exiting its $2.6 billion battery-quality nickel project, noting improved global availability since the project began. Reuters reports copper futures edged lower as a firmer dollar and weak demand expectations from China weighed on the market. Nickel edged up; aluminium slightly declined. | Stillwater Critical Minerals announced final drill results from resource-expansion work at its Stillwater West Ni-PGE-Cu-Co project in Montana. The company highlights high-grade nickel and copper sulphide intersections and broader NiEq interval potential, supported by ongoing Glencore investment and technical involvement, improving sentiment toward nickel and copper supply prospects. | Premium Nickel Resources reported additional high-grade Ni-Cu-Co drill intercepts at the past-producing Selebi North underground mine in Botswana and said these are the last holes for its initial NI 43-101 compliant Mineral Resource Estimate, expected soon. The release implies continued resource confidence but no immediate production or market supply impact. | BASF said it will not proceed with its nickel-cobalt refining complex in Weda Bay, Indonesia, citing a changing global nickel market and evolving supply options for battery-grade nickel. Copper futures hover near a two-month low as lingering demand worries and uncertainty about China’s recovery weigh on prices. A firmer US dollar limits buying interest. Copper rose after stocks withdrawals in South Korea reduced perceived oversupply, with 8,000 tons of fresh LME warehouse cancellations suggesting inventories may fall. London copper is heading for its first weekly gain in seven weeks, supported by a softer dollar, hopes for U.S. rate cuts and stimulus, and improving physical demand in China. That supply-effect potential can shift sentiment in nickel pricing over the coming days to weeks. | New Caledonia’s Koniambo (KNS) nickel plant began mass layoff processes after being mothballed, with a deadline for a “cold” sleep state as ownership and buyer options remain unresolved. The country’s unrest has also targeted nickel sites, adding supply disruption amid a broader nickel-price crisis. | Canada Nickel reported improved testwork at its Crawford East Zone, including a locked-cycle result delivering 47% total nickel recovery and 60% nickel sulphide concentrate, with open-circuit tests averaging 28% higher recovery than modelled. A 130-tonne pilot plant began commissioning and will run until September to generate concentrate samples. | First Atlantic Nickel reports progress at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland, including a completed investor site visit, a bridge installed for year-round access, permits filed for additional road work, and expanded geological activity targeting awaruite nickel occurrences. The updates support ongoing exploration that may bolster longer-term nickel supply expectations. | Reuters reports copper fell on Monday due to concerns over weak Chinese demand and elevated inventories. LME three-month copper dropped about 0.7% to $9,045/ton, with LME stocks more than doubling since early June. Macro expectations around Fed rate cuts also influenced sentiment across metals. | Nornickel reported a 1% nickel output drop in H1 due to furnace repairs at the Nadezhda smelter but kept full-year nickel guidance at 184,000–194,000 tonnes. It also noted lingering constraints from delayed Western-sourced equipment and the US/UK nickel/aluminium/copper trading and import bans, with repairs resuming early August. | Fastmarkets reports China nickel sulfate prices edging down, citing slow demand, weaker liquidity, and continued declines in the three-month LME nickel cash price. It also links softer international nickel sulfate activity to BHP’s suspension at Nickel West and West Musgrave, weighing on market sentiment. | Vedanta Nico, a Vedanta subsidiary, partnered with US battery technology firm AEsir Technologies to supply nickel for next-generation nickel-zinc batteries for EVs, 5G telecom and critical infrastructure. The move highlights growing nickel demand for battery cathodes, potentially supporting near-term nickel price sentiment.

Tanzania agreed to pay US$90 million in instalments to Australia’s Indiana Resources to settle an ICSID dispute over cancellation of its Ntaka Hill nickel licence. The article describes the project’s nickel sulphide potential but offers no concrete near-term production impact, implying minimal effect on global nickel pricing. | Kenorland Minerals entered a definitive agreement to sell 100% of its South Thompson nickel project to Evolution Nickel, receiving Evolution shares and a 2% net smelter return royalty. Closing is expected before end-2024 and depends on Evolution raising at least C$4m. The release is about exploration development, not production. | FPX Nickel reports value engineering at its Baptiste project reduced estimated carbon intensity by 50% (to about 1.2 tCO2e/tNi), driven by trolley-assist haul trucks cutting diesel use ~50%. It also secured BC Hydro funding for electrification studies, improving economics and expected nickel operating competitiveness.

PTX Metals reported additional near-surface drill results from its W2 Cu-Ni-PGE project in Ontario, citing higher confidence in continuity of mineralized zones and new intersections with disseminated to massive sulphides. The update is exploration-focused and does not signal near-term production impacts. Gold assays are also pending next week. | PTX Metals released additional diamond-drill assay results from its 100%-owned W2 Cu-Ni-PGE project, expanding and confirming near-surface sulphide mineralization across several zones. Results increase confidence in continuity of historical zones. The release is exploratory and does not mention production, shipments, or new supply disruptions affecting prices. | Indonesia expanded its Simbara mining tracking system to cover nickel (and tin), following a corruption case and to improve governance. The move links production, sales, exports, royalties, and port clearance, targeting Rp 5–10 trillion in additional nickel-related royalties/PNBP. It should tighten oversight and reduce illegal supply. | Merdeka Copper Gold (MDKA) is shifting its mining energy use from coal to renewables such as solar, alongside B35 biodiesel for vehicles and feasibility work for hydropower and wind. The transition is tied to operations including the Wetar copper mine and MDKA’s nickel assets, framed as cost-efficient ESG that may support output continuity. | Indonesia’s Nickel Industries managing director said Indonesia may limit further Chinese investment in its nickel sector to avoid reliance on China and attract more Western capital. This policy risk can influence expectations for Indonesia’s nickel supply and near-term project development, offering support to nickel prices over the next weeks.

Copper prices fell at the start of August as concerns grew over weakening Chinese demand, supported by a private survey showing manufacturing contracted and an official PMI hitting a five-month low. Despite some support from a narrower export parity and retreating inventories, higher exports boosted inventories. Powell’s rate-cut hint offered limited offset.

Indonesia’s Geological Agency says it has identified more than 100 additional potential nickel reserve sites for further exploration. The government’s findings reinforce Indonesia’s role as the leading nickel producer, important for EV batteries. With output and export revenues already large, new prospective supply may ease supply concerns. | Indonesia’s Geological Agency says it has identified over 100 additional potential nickel reserve sites for further exploration. The findings reinforce Indonesia’s role as the leading nickel producer used in EV batteries, but the report does not point to near-term production increases or export changes, suggesting limited immediate price pressure. | London copper rose 0.4% but was set for a flat week as investors await US jobs data while China’s demand outlook remains downbeat. Copper inventories stay high. LME aluminium edged down. Nickel prices were supported by tight Indonesian ore supply, but overall the week’s direction appears muted. | Indonesia’s Geological Agency says it has identified more than 100 new potential nickel reserve locations and will continue exploration. The update strengthens Indonesia’s position as the leading global nickel producer used in EV batteries. With large existing output already, new prospects may support expectations for longer-term supply, mildly bullish for nickel. | Goldrea Resources announced LOIs to acquire 50% interests in additional mineral claims adjoining its Cannonball Project, which hosts multiple porphyry copper-gold and copper-gold prospects. The company says the geology matches Seabridge’s Golden Triangle corridor and highlights recommended drilling. No production volumes, inventories, or near-term supply changes are reported.

London copper edged up 0.2% after prior sell-offs, as some dip-buying emerged, but global demand concerns capped upside. The U.S. jobs report and weaker China activity fueled recession worries while expectations of Fed cuts supported industrial demand. LME aluminium and nickel rose slightly, showing mixed sentiment. | Copper prices tumbled as worsening China and U.S. demand outlooks sparked a sell-off. Weaker U.S. jobs data and technology earnings reinforced recession fears, while rising LME warehouse inventories weighed near term. The risk-off move also pushed aluminium and nickel modestly lower alongside copper. | Indonesia’s Geological Agency reported at least 100 additional, currently unexplored sites with potential nickel reserves. It said the findings strengthen Indonesia’s position as the leading global nickel producer, with nickel being used in EV batteries, and that further exploration will continue. | Copper fell sharply (up to 3.8% on the LME) as global financial contagion triggered a risk-off rush to cash out and turn bearish amid fears of a US recession. The selloff pressured other industrial raw-materials, reflecting weaker growth expectations and tighter financial conditions.

Eramet Indonesia’s director says the company, via PT Eramet Bumi Sulawesi, is looking to expand its role in Indonesia’s nickel sector and build partnerships for an end-to-end EV battery value chain. Eramet plans new mining-site work and permits, positioning against Chinese dominance amid Western EV supply concerns. | Asian Battery Metals reported interim reconnaissance results from Mongolia’s Yambat project, including evidence of an expanded magmatic copper-nickel system and new targets such as Copper Ridge. Mapping and geophysics guided sampling and a Phase 1 drilling campaign. Results are preliminary and plan further scout drilling, implying future supply potential. | Indonesia’s nickel hydrometallurgy intermediate product output fell 12.06% MoM in June due to abnormal weather, which reduced transportation efficiency of ore and materials and caused delayed raw material supply. August output is forecast to rise 9.73% MoM as weather improves, though levels remain below normal.

London copper fell 0.5% to a more than four-and-a-half month low as a firm dollar and looming U.S. recession fears pressured prices, alongside weak China demand concerns. LME aluminium also slipped 0.3% and nickel fell 0.5%, while Shanghai contracts were mixed, reflecting catch-up activity. | Reuters reports LME three-month copper fell 0.5% to a four-and-a-half-month low due to a firm dollar and worries about U.S. and Chinese growth. Shanghai copper rose 0.3% as higher prices spurred Chinese exports and narrowing spreads attracted cargoes. LME aluminium and nickel slipped slightly while SHFE aluminium rose and nickel edged down. | ESG-focused coverage includes Macquarie’s notes that several nickel mines are being suspended/closed amid weak pricing, which may tighten near-term supply. It also reports South32 is appealing WA EPA conditions at its Worsley alumina mine, potentially influencing aluminium supply/costs. Other sections are largely macro/energy/ESG themes. | The piece argues Indonesia’s nickel boom—driven by EV and battery demand—has caused major rainforest and coastal damage, citing relentless smelting smoke, mining craters, and pollution at processing hubs like IMIP. It notes Indonesia’s leading nickel output and smelting capacity, raising the risk of disruption or tighter oversight affecting near-term nickel supply.

The article reviews Minnesota Governor Tim Walz’s approach to copper-nickel mining and highlights that Twin Metals’ leases were pulled by the Biden Administration, effectively halting a planned mine near the BWCA. Walz is portrayed as hands-off and emphasizing federal environmental review, keeping project timing uncertain. | Copper edged up slightly on a softer U.S. dollar, but broader sentiment is weak as LME inventories jumped and global growth expectations deteriorated.
