Commentary notes rising TC/RCs suggest improving concentrate supply. Aluminium and nickel prices were also lower in the same session, indicating mixed but generally pressured industrial metals. | Copper prices steadied but remained under pressure as pessimism on global growth and risk-off sentiment weighed on base metals. LME copper inventories surged nearly threefold to 294,750 tons. Aluminium and nickel also fell on the day, reflecting weaker demand expectations outweighing improving spot refining charges. | Golden Arrow reported new step-out drill intercepts at its San Pietro IOCG project in Chile, including long intervals grading about 0.43–0.44% Cu with associated gold and cobalt. The release is an exploration update with no production or reserve change stated, so any near-term effect on copper prices is likely limited.

Copper rose Friday after U.S. jobless claims came in better than expected, easing recession-demand fears and supporting rate-cut optimism. It still faces a fifth straight weekly drop, with LME inventories at a high since 2019 limiting upside catalysts. Aluminium and nickel also climbed amid the broader rebound. | Ormonde notes Peak Nickel has resumed a 2024 drill programme at its Rodburn Ni-Cu-Co project in Scotland, targeting further definition and potential expansion of mineralization. The announcement includes Ni, Cu and Co grades used in Ni-equivalent assumptions, but provides no new price-driving results or production impacts. | SMM reports high-grade NPI (8–12% nickel) prices rose WoW on tight nickel ore availability and higher ore-driven smelter costs, limiting short-term supply increases. Easing rainfall in Weda Bay supports logistics, while stainless-steel peak-season demand lifts inquiries. Nickel prices consolidated with a rebound and upside bias, though fundamentals remain weak.

Copper prices opened the week softer as investors weighed ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty and awaited data for demand signals. Reuters cites economic worries including recession fears, with copper down on both LME and Shanghai. Nickel also slipped, while aluminium was steadier, reflecting cautious industrial demand sentiment. | Copper prices rose as traders covered shorts and looked to upcoming China and U.S. data for demand and rate-cut timing. LME copper was up about 0.9%, with cancelled warrants increasing and warehouse inventories still elevated. The report also said aluminium and nickel climbed on the same risk-on backdrop. | US and Indonesia discussed a possible Critical Minerals Specific Free Trade Agreement to increase access to Indonesian nickel for EV batteries. However, nine US senators sent a bipartisan letter urging scrutiny over labor, environmental issues, and Chinese dominance, potentially slowing or complicating the agreement and dampening near-term nickel sentiment. | LME data show Russian-origin aluminium’s share in LME-approved warehouses rose to 65% in July (from 50% in June), while Indian-origin fell. Total on-warrant aluminium stocks fell 23% in July. Russian copper and nickel shares declined. The piece also references the LME’s earlier ban on sanctioned Russian output. | LME data show Russian-origin aluminium’s share of on-warrant stocks rose to 65% in July while Indian-origin fell to 33%, alongside a 23% decline in total on-warrant aluminium stocks. The May Russian ban remains cited, with similar share changes for copper and nickel inventories. | Knights Bay Oryx’s Oman subsidiary has completed initial drilling at its nickel laterite project in Mineral Block 21, targeting nickel continuity and staged resource definition. The company has not yet released lab results, but sampled ore near small mines showed ~1% nickel and shipped material for metallurgical testing. | Nickel prices continued to plunge through July, with early August stabilizing near early-2024 levels. Stainless steel distributors report weak demand, and lower nickel-driven stainless surcharges reduce buyer incentives. U.S. manufacturing PMI stays in contraction, reinforcing soft end-use conditions. Outokumpu sees growth QoQ but still down YoY.

Terrain Minerals reports airborne geophysics at its Lort River project in Western Australia, identifying five conductors that may indicate “Nova-style” magmatic nickel-copper sulphide mineralisation similar to IGO’s Nova-Bollinger deposits. The company plans ground EM, soil geochemistry, and reconnaissance drilling, seeking a new nickel discovery. | Morgan Stanley flags nickel may be near its pricing floor: inventories are surging and demand remains challenging, limiting upside and keeping prices range-bound. For copper, spot has retraced and Macquarie cuts short- to medium-term demand and price forecasts, pointing to renewed near-term downside pressure despite M&A activity. | Morgan Stanley says nickel’s “bottom” may be near but elevated nickel inventories and challenging demand constrain sustained rallies, implying range-bound pricing. Macquarie expects copper volatility, cuts 2025–27 forecasts on weaker demand, and lowers 2024 Chinese and North American growth estimates. Overall, neither metal faces a clear immediate breakout. | Reuters reports copper prices fell as LME inventories climbed to near five-year highs and China demand remained sluggish. Traders cited exports of surplus copper into Asian LME warehouses and weaker Chinese macro data. Purchases from some physical consumers and hopes of a BHP Escondida strike limited losses, but overall sentiment stayed bearish. | Grid Metals provided a permitting and exploration update for its Manitoba projects. Donner’s advanced exploration permit for lithium was approved but bulk sampling is deferred due to lower lithium prices. For MM, the company plans to resume a ~5,000 m drill program targeting Cu-rich sulfide mineralization near the New Manitoba deposit.

Australia’s FIRB approved Japanese investors (Sumitomo Metal Mining and Mitsubishi) to participate in Ardea Resources’ Goongarrie Hub within the Kalgoorlie Nickel Project. The decision removes a JV condition, enabling continued feasibility-study funding and engineering work, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel development. | NewRange Copper Nickel said it will run four studies to upgrade tailings storage design, modernize water treatment (with $100 million planned), improve efficient production to potentially raise throughput, and explore electric ore transport. The copper-nickel project uses existing processing infrastructure and faces permitting work, but most permits remain active. | The report says developers of a stalled Minnesota copper-nickel mine plan studies that could result in significant changes. Such actions can influence market expectations for near-term copper and nickel supply. However, the provided text lacks details on whether production will resume, delay, expand, or be canceled, limiting direction.

China’s July primary aluminium output rose 6% to 3.68m tonnes, the highest monthly total in over 20 years, as smelters ramped up due to still-profitable operations. While prices recently fell and demand stayed weak, analysts expect profits to remain steady and output to rise further, weighing on aluminium sentiment. | NewRange Copper Nickel (PolyMet) developers say they will run studies over the next year on redesign options to improve environmental safeguards, reduce carbon emissions, optimize waste storage and water treatment, and change ore transport. Environmental groups argue the plan is flawed. No decisions or production timing changes are announced. | NewRange Copper Nickel (PolyMet) developers said they will run studies on environmental safeguards and cost/energy efficiency, potentially changing the open-pit mine and processing design. Any major changes would require additional reviews and permitting. The project’s further delays/uncertainty are likely a modest negative for near-term copper and nickel sentiment. | Nickel prices have fallen sharply, reflecting a supply glut in Indonesia and, increasingly, weaker EV battery demand. Europe and the US are seeing EV incentive cutbacks and slower downstream uptake of nickel-rich chemistries (NCM/NCA). Producers are impairing and delaying battery materials investments, reinforcing bearish sentiment for nickel.

Copper prices are set for their first weekly gain in six, supported by mine supply worries after a strike at BHP’s Escondida in Chile and stronger-than-feared U.S. retail sales and jobs data easing recession fears. Aluminium and nickel also ticked higher, reflecting improved base-metals sentiment. | Philippine laterite nickel ore prices increased over the past week, with medium-grade ore rising from about $45/wmt to ~$48/wmt. Demand from Indonesia, tight ore supply, and smelter restocking needs are cited, while policy rewards and expectations of further quota tightness could keep prices rising short term. | Flying Nickel Mining completed its merger, acquiring 100% of Nevada Vanadium, creating a wholly owned subsidiary. The company is primarily focused on advancing its Minago nickel project and a vanadium project. The corporate combination may affect longer-term sentiment, but no near-term production or supply changes are stated.

Copper prices rose as U.S. recession fears eased after strong U.S. data and China’s copper inventories fell. LME three-month copper climbed 0.9% and SHFE September gained 0.5%. The report also cites easing supply concerns at BHP’s Escondida after a wage deal, alongside gains in aluminium and a modest nickel rise. | Flying Nickel Mining has finalized the acquisition of Nevada Vanadium, making it a wholly owned subsidiary. The company is an exploration-stage miner focused on nickel sulphide and vanadium, including development of its Minago nickel project in Manitoba. The article details share issuance and governance changes, with no stated output timing changes. | Power Nickel reported final results from a winter drilling program at its Nisk project’s “Lion Zone,” saying the mineralized zone increased over 94% and providing assay intercepts. The company is pursuing further summer drilling to test depth and continuity, aiming to expand high-grade nickel-copper mineralization.

London LME three-month copper fell 0.4% as the previous rally was attributed to short-covering rather than new demand. Open interest declined and analysts noted limited contract rollover. Chinese physical demand improved slightly, and an Escondida strike was averted, easing supply fears. LME aluminium and nickel also declined. | SMM cited China Customs data showing China’s nickel ore imports fell to 3.4851 million wmt in July (-30.6% YoY), with laterite down sharply. Philippines shipments dipped due to weather, though constraints are easing and supply is shifting toward Indonesia. August is expected to see room for laterite growth despite domestic demand. | Falcon Gold reported delays from high water levels at its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland and the need for additional permitting to build 8 km of road. Equipment mobilization is pending completion by week-end, after which drilling will test multiple copper geophysical anomalies. No nickel operational changes were reported. | Falcon Gold reports delays and permitting for drill access at its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland, with roadwork expected to finish by week-end. The property is contiguous to Benton/Homeland Nickel’s Great Burnt Copper Zone. Reported past and recent drilling shows high copper grades, but no immediate production impact.

Zeb Nickel Corp. said South Africa’s DMPR granted an Integrated Environmental Authorisation for its Limpopo Zeb Nickel Project, clearing a major permitting phase after a lengthy EIA process. The approval supports progressing toward a mining right for nickel extraction, with additional associated copper mineralisation mentioned. | Reports say BHP is discussing potential investment with Indonesia’s government to expand/participate in Indonesia’s rapidly growing nickel processing industry. The backdrop is BHP suspending WA nickel operations due to global nickel oversupply from Indonesia’s export ban. Such investment talks may support longer-term nickel supply balance expectations near term. | Eco-Business says Indonesia’s nickel push faces major hurdles: low-grade product focus, ore supply gaps, heavy captive-coal emissions, and environmental/labor controversies. EU and US rules may reduce appeal of Indonesian processed nickel, and a $2.6 billion BASF–Eramet project was cancelled. The article warns of China dependence and possible nickel price weakness. | Zeb Nickel received integrated environmental authorisation from South Africa’s Department of Minerals and Petroleum Resources for its Bushveld complex project. The approval supports progress toward a mining right and allows activities under the approved EIA, with plans for further drilling to improve nickel grade and project economics. | Flying Nickel Mining entered a definitive arrangement to sell its Minago nickel assets in Manitoba to Norway House Cree Nation for C$8 million cash plus shares. The transaction is expected by mid-October 2024 and requires shareholder and regulatory approvals. The announcement is asset-specific and not linked to broader nickel supply or pricing changes.

Flying Nickel Mining signed a definitive deal to sell its Minago nickel project in Manitoba to Norway House Cree Nation, receiving $8m cash and a 11.4% share stake transfer. The company plans a rebrand and remains active in its Nevada vanadium interests. The transaction is unlikely to meaningfully shift near-term global nickel balances. | NewRange Copper Nickel advances permitting-critical studies for a potential NorthMet copper-nickel mine near Hoyt Lakes, including tailings, water treatment, and efficiency upgrades. The company is examining higher throughput (32,000 to 40,000 tons/day) to increase domestic copper and nickel supply, which could lift near-term price sentiment.

SMM reports the coefficient for high-grade nickel matte versus LME nickel rose to 87–88 as nickel prices fell and refining costs increased. It also cites weak downstream demand, substitution pressure from MHP due to high coefficients, and producers shifting toward NPI for better margins, leaving transactions sluggish. | Nornickel reported a 22% profit drop, blaming weak nickel prices, logistics disruptions in the Red Sea, and harder cross-border payments. Maritime restrictions caused nickel and copper stock accumulation and reduced sales. The firm cited a 28% LME nickel price fall in H1 and forecast a 2024/25 nickel surplus.

First Atlantic Nickel reports completion of Phase 1 infrastructure upgrades and ongoing sampling over a 30 km awaruite (nickel-iron alloy) trend in Newfoundland, ahead of a planned 2024 drilling program. The release highlights awaruite’s easier concentration versus sulfide nickel and potential to ease North American nickel concentrate shortages. | Lifezone Metals said it is starting an evaluation with the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation for political risk insurance for future investments in the Kabanga nickel-copper-cobalt mine and Kahama hydromet refinery in Tanzania. The project is expected to produce LME-grade nickel and copper, with a feasibility study progressing. | DRC authorities have halted or recommended stopping Chemaf Resources’s planned sale of its copper-cobalt mines to China’s Norinco, citing violations of Gecamines’ lease and farm-out contract terms. The dispute may disrupt near-term copper supply expectations from the DRC, though the final outcome is uncertain. | First Atlantic Nickel reported Phase 1 infrastructure upgrades at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland, including road/bridge improvements and expanded nickel sampling along a 30 km trend. The firm is also using LiDAR for better geological targeting and sending initial samples for analysis, aimed at developing awaruite nickel-iron resources. | South Pacific Metals reports exceptional surface trench assay results at its Osena gold-copper Osena project in Papua New Guinea, including 79 m averaging ~981 ppm Cu and localized higher copper grades. The findings support potential expansion of a porphyry system, but are early-stage exploration with limited near-term impact on copper prices and none on nickel or aluminium. | Fastmarkets reports China nickel sulfate prices are rangebound but generally supported. Tight nickel MHP supply (and firm MHP payable terms) and low salt production inventories help hold prices firm, while slower NCM cathode precursor demand limits gains. Higher LME nickel cash prices lifted the all-in nickel sulfate range. | Nornickel said it plans to transfer part of its copper-plant capacities from Norilsk to China via a joint venture, aiming to resolve market-access and sanctions-related issues. Experts warn this could disrupt Russia’s industrial base and concentrate flows. The relocation can affect near-term expectations for copper supply and related nickel-linked outputs.

Anglesey Mining says it has submitted an initial Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to restart underground mining at Parys Mountain, historically a major copper producer. The plan would process concentrate (~30% copper) for export, but requires significant investment and further approvals, making near-term copper impact uncertain. | The UK nuclear regulator (ONR) issued improvement notices to Sellafield after breaches of hazardous chemical controls involving nickel nitrate in an effluent facility. Contamination outside a glovebox could have exposed workers. The issues must be corrected by late 2024 and March 2025, without compromising nuclear safety.

Rusal reported a 41.6% jump in first-half profit as it increased production of primary aluminium and alumina and boosted shipments to China to record levels. It also notes the LME broad ban on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel amid U.S./UK sanctions. Improved volumes and rerouting are mildly supportive for prices. | Rusal’s first-half adjusted net profit rose 41.6% as it increased production and shipped record volumes to China. The firm faced demand pressure as Western buyers avoid Russia-made metal after Ukraine. The article notes April LME bans on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel and links improved output to higher prices for aluminium and alumina. | Argentina’s minister says the pro-business mining incentive regime (RIGI) should attract major foreign investment and help new lithium and copper projects start later this year. Copper is framed as crucial for EVs and AI, with large capex needs for projects, implying improved near-term supply/export expectations.  | Sumitomo’s Ambatovy nickel mine in Madagascar missed output targets after malfunctions, prompting an 89 billion yen write-down and a restructuring plan with a British court. Annual output was 31,000 tonnes vs 40,000 targeted. The company expects costs and production to improve once operations stabilize.

Ardea Resources executed a binding shareholders’ agreement with a Japanese consortium (Sumitomo Metal Mining and Mitsubishi) to fund a $98.5m definitive feasibility study and advance FEED for the Goongarrie Hub nickel-cobalt project in Western Australia. The step supports potential FID but does not change near-term nickel output.

KNS, a New Caledonian nickel producer involving Glencore, shut its furnaces over the weekend, moving into care and maintenance and laying off most of its 1,200 staff. The wider local nickel sector has been hit by unrest, with Eramet’s SLN operating minimally and Prony Resources suspending operations.

Canada Nickel filed an NI 43-101 technical report for its Deloro nickel sulphide project, confirming an initial mineral resource estimate effective July 17, 2024 with no material differences from earlier results. The update is a regulatory disclosure tied to future exploration progress, but no near-term production changes are stated. | EV Nickel Inc. reported drill-hole results from its Langmuir #2 nickel zone, including intersections from 1.07% to 5.11% Ni and higher-than-expected nickel sulphide tenor (~12% to >40% Ni). The company plans further drilling to expand the mineralized envelope and advance its nickel strategy toward battery supply. | Mink Ventures reported positive results from a summer sampling program at its Warren nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario. Reference samples confirmed mineralized zones with meaningful nickel grades and significant copper assays, supporting drill targeting for a planned diamond drilling phase after freeze-up. | Mink Ventures reported positive grab-sample assays from the summer sampling program at its Warren nickel-copper-cobalt project in Ontario, highlighting multiple zones with copper and nickel grades. The company plans winter access and diamond drilling after freeze-up to test refined IP/EM targets. Near-term impact on metal prices appears limited. | Fastmarkets highlights Indonesia as the core of global nickel supply and processing, with expanding intermediate and refined capacity and rising ore imports. It links this supply build-out to much lower nickel prices, citing the LME three-month contract down 47% since January 2023 and Western producer closures in a low-price environment.

Koniambo Nickel SAS said negotiations for Glencore’s 49% stake are continuing, but its furnaces were shut over the weekend and operations were already halted. Employees were laid off, and other New Caledonia nickel players are running at minimal capacity or suspending. These disruptions point to near-term nickel supply weakness. | Indonesia’s deputy minister said nickel prices should remain stable near current levels as additional capacity from new plants offsets rising EV-driven demand. He noted ore supply risks from declining nickel content and potential policy tweaks to nickel tax holidays amid the 15% global minimum corporate tax regime. Overall, near-term pressure appears mixed.

Canada Nickel said it received a Letter of Interest from Export Development Canada for up to US$500 million of long-term debt financing to support the Crawford Nickel Sulphide Project in Ontario. The funding is subject to due diligence and permits, but the milestone improves prospects for future nickel supply. | Canada Nickel said Export Development Canada is considering a loan of up to $500 million for the Crawford project in Ontario and that the company is seeking remaining funding and permits for construction. It also plans a nickel-processing plant in Ontario to process about 80,000 tonnes annually, supporting nickel supply expectations. | The piece argues global copper demand is rising from electrification and energy transition while supply faces constraints: declining ore grades, labor disruptions, fewer high-quality projects, and a large projected deficit by 2030. It details BHP’s expansion in South Australia (including refining) but says uncertainty remains about fully closing the gap. | China has filed a WTO complaint against Canada over Canada’s new tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles and 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum, with Canada’s measures set to start next month. Analysts warn retaliation could raise costs and pressure markets, adding near-term uncertainty for aluminum supply and prices. | Canada Nickel said it received an LOI for up to US$500M in financing support from Export Development Canada to fund its Crawford nickel-sulphide project, alongside expectations of qualifying for additional critical minerals and carbon capture tax credits. The company aims to secure remaining project funding and begin construction after permits.

Terrain Minerals says a recent airborne VTEM survey identified five bedrock sulphide conductors, including two within its “eye” target in a mafic/ultramafic intrusive setting, a geological style associated with nickel/copper deposits. It frames this as underexplored and potentially economic, though no drilling results are yet provided. | Falcon Gold mobilizes drill crews to begin an inaugural drilling campaign at its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland after prior access delays. The release cites high-grade prior copper drilling, outlines testing of geophysical targets, and notes ongoing permitting for additional drilling. | Falcon Gold says drill crews and equipment are being mobilized to its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland after spring access delays from high water. The firm completed 3 km of road. The property hosts reported copper-rich zones, with prior results from neighboring drilling indicating strong copper grades. | Anglo American has hired financial advisers to sell two nickel mines in Brazil (Barro Alto and Niquelandia in Goiás), producing about 38,000 tons of nickel annually. The country head says discussions with potential buyers are ongoing but no deal is expected this year, implying limited immediate supply-market disruption.

Power Nickel said its summer drilling at the Lion Zone discovery intercepted sulphides and expanded the mineralized footprint, including massive/semi-massive chalcopyrite, with the high-grade portion extending west and deeper. The company expects assays later in September and plans a larger winter drill program, but no production changes are announced. | Nth Cycle began operations of its first U.S. commercial-scale nickel/cobalt scrap refining system in Fairfield, Ohio, using electro-extraction to produce nickel cobalt MHP. The modular plant can process up to 3,100 tonnes of scrap and target 900 tonnes of MHP annually, aiming to reduce reliance on China. | Xanadu Mines reports continued field work at its Sant Tolgoi copper-nickel exploration project in western Mongolia. Detailed mapping, geochemical sampling, and geophysical modelling are underway, with multiple shallow Cu-Ni sulphide targets identified and early geochemical/geophysical results expected in Q4 2024. No production or supply disruption is mentioned. | Xanadu Mines reports field progress at the Sant Tolgoi copper-nickel exploration project in Mongolia, including geological mapping, geochemical sampling, and geophysical modelling, with multiple shallow Cu-Ni sulphide targets identified. Early geochemical/geophysical results are expected in Q4 2024, but no production or resource changes are announced. | Canada Nickel announced positive drilling results from 48 holes across seven properties in the Timmins Nickel District, reporting multiple nickel intercepts including higher-grade sections (e.g., up to 2.68% nickel over 6.5 metres). The company is progressing toward publishing additional resources by mid-2025, which is supportive but not immediate supply-changing news.

Nth Cycle began operations of its Oyster system in Fairfield, Ohio, a commercial-scale plant to refine nickel scrap (black-mass) and produce nickel-cobalt MHP. The start of domestic recycling/refining capacity aims to reduce U.S. reliance on China and improve sourcing compliance. This is generally supportive for nearby nickel market sentiment. | Vedanta urges India to renegotiate CEPAs with Japan and South Korea to remove import duties on nickel sulphate used in EV batteries. Nickel sulphate shipments currently face 3.9% (Japan) and 5% (South Korea) taxes. Easing tariffs could boost India’s export demand for nickel sulphate. | LME data show copper stocks up 29% in August and nickel open-tonnage stocks up 6.7%, with China-origin both rising. Aluminium stocks were broadly unchanged, with on-warrant volumes slightly higher. Rising inventories point to reduced prompt tightness, weighing on short-term base metal prices despite some copper inflow trend softening. | LME data show copper warehouse stocks up 29% in August and nickel open-tonnage stocks up 6.7%, with China-origin volumes rising. Copper cancellations increased as China import premiums improved. Aluminium stocks were broadly steady though. Overall, higher inventories suggest reduced near-term supply tightness, bearish for copper and nickel. | Fastmarkets reports nickel premiums in Europe and China largely unchanged week-on-week, even as LME three-month nickel slipped below $16,000/tonne. European briquette premiums narrowed on lower freight costs and ample availability, while cathode premiums stayed stable amid quiet demand from electroplating and muted import conditions. | Electra Battery Materials says North America’s EV growth will remain strong and will increasingly favor nickel-bearing battery chemistries, with “high nickel” expected to dominate. It highlights potential Indonesian exports of MHP for nickel sulfate production and related refinery plans, though some delegates flag investment uncertainty from higher rates. | Putin said Russia should consider limiting exports of uranium, titanium and nickel in retaliation against the West. The article highlights Russia’s role as the world’s largest producer of refined nickel through Nornickel, implying potential supply disruption that could weigh on nickel prices in coming weeks. | Power One Resources completed three diamond drill holes (1,300m) at its Pecors Ni-Cu-PGM project near Elliot Lake, Ontario, with assays pending. The company targets magnetic anomalies interpreted to host magmatic Ni-Cu-PGM and references historical Cu and Ni intercepts over 12m, but provides no new confirmed results. | Power One Resources completed three diamond drill holes (1,300m) at its Pecors Ni-Cu-PGM project in Elliot Lake, Ontario. The company did not provide assay results yet, and drilling targets include EM conductors and magnetic anomalies. A historical uranium estimate is described as non-NI 43-101 compliant. | Power One Resources announced completion of three diamond drill holes at its Pecors Ni-Cu-PGM project in Elliot Lake, Ontario. Assays are pending and targets focus on geophysics tied to potential magmatic-hosted nickel-copper mineralization. No resource update, production, or supply disruption is reported.

Esperance Port workers face potential job losses as major customers shut down over the next year, including First Quantum Minerals’ Ravensthorpe nickel operation and Mineral Resources’ Yilgarn iron ore project, with Arcadium Lithium also mothballing Mt Cattlin. The reduced export outlook affects 2025 trade volumes and associated sentiment. | Vladimir Putin said Russia should consider limiting exports of uranium, titanium and nickel in retaliation for Western sanctions. Reuters reports his comments pushed nickel prices higher and lifted shares of uranium mining firms, signaling market expectations of tighter supply from Russia. | Vedanta urged India to negotiate changes to trade pact “rules of origin” with Japan and Korea to remove import duties on nickel sulfate used in EV batteries. Japan’s tariff is 3.9% and Korea’s 5%. Lower tariffs could boost exports and support nickel demand expectations. | Canada Nickel says it received an additional support letter from a financial institution for up to C$500 million of long-term project debt toward development of its Crawford nickel sulphide project in Ontario. Together with an Export Development Canada letter and expected refundable tax credits, the company advances its financing package timeline. | Magna Mining agreed to acquire KGHM’s Sudbury Basin base-metal assets, including the operating McCreedy West copper mine and adjacent nickel-copper operations. Magna plans to continue production after closing and re-invest profits into development and exploration. The deal adds near-term production expectations for copper and nickel. | Putin suggested Russia may limit exports of nickel (along with uranium and titanium) as retaliation for Western sanctions. With Russia a major nickel exporter and a large share of LME-registered nickel linked to Russian origin, markets reacted quickly—Reuters reported a 2.6% jump in three-month LME nickel right after the comments. | The piece claims Indonesia’s nickel mining and smelting expansion is accelerating to support EV battery demand, citing major investment deals and large processing plans. It also details environmental damage and pollution impacts, but provides no immediate regulatory action or production stoppage that would reduce near-term nickel supply.

Core Nickel Corp. announced a non-brokered flow-through private placement to raise up to $400,000. Proceeds will fund VTEM and geophysical surveys and part of a winter drilling program on its 100%-owned Mel and Odei River nickel exploration properties in Manitoba.

A New Caledonian consortium has made an eleventh-hour offer to take over and potentially restart the moth-balled Koniambo (KNS) nickel plant, idling since late August. The proposal could change the project model and target lower output, but depends on Glencore’s handling of KNS’s US$13.7 billion debt. | Reports say Sibanye-Stillwater is the most probable bidder for shares in New Caledonia’s Prony Resources, a nickel plant whose operations were disrupted by unrest and market pressure from Indonesia. Separate coverage notes proposals to restart the moth-balled Koniambo (KNS) nickel plant. Such developments could lift short-term nickel supply expectations.

Sibanye is studying New Caledonia’s Prony Resources as a potential source of mixed hydroxide precipitate (MHP) to feed its Sandouville, France plant for battery-grade nickel. The effort follows Prony’s search for investors and its operations being halted since May due to unrest, with Sibanye also exploring Indonesia sources.

Flying Nickel Mining Corp. entered an amended arrangement agreement to sell its Minago nickel project and related assets in Manitoba’s Thompson Nickel Belt to Norway House Cree Nation’s subsidiary. The amendment clarifies closing and transfer mechanics; completion remains subject to shareholder, court, and TSXV approvals. | BHP’s planned suspension of nickel operations in Western Australia has prevented it from committing nickel volumes to new trading platforms, disrupting plans by GCHL and delaying Abaxx’s nickel sulphate contract. Sources cite the shutdown as a setback, prompting the new venues to seek alternative supply, affecting short-term nickel market liquidity expectations. | Flying Nickel Mining Corp. entered an amended arrangement agreement to sell its Minago nickel project and related assets in Manitoba for C$8 million cash plus share consideration to Norway House Cree Nation. The update clarifies closing/transfer mechanics and remains subject to shareholder, court, and TSXV approvals. No direct market-wide nickel supply impact is stated.

Perseverance Metals reported a new nickel sulphide zone at its Lac Gayot project in Quebec, including 1.61% Ni over 5.10m, alongside minor copper. The company also highlights that historical drilling may have missed deeper extensions. These are exploration results, not near-term supply changes, but they can support nickel sentiment. | Gide advised KGHM International on selling a portfolio of Sudbury Basin mining assets to Magna Mining, including the active McCreedy West copper mine and several inactive copper mines and exploration projects. The deal is valued around C$30 million and expected to close by end-Q1 2025, with regulatory support provided.

Major miners’ ratings were upgraded following broker reviews. Morgan Stanley lifted long-term copper forecasts, saying low prices may boost demand, refined supply growth should slow, and inventory drawdowns could follow; this supports higher copper. Nickel is mentioned with challenged demand but possible supply cuts, making its near-term direction less clear. | Premium Nickel Resources filed an NI 43-101 Technical Report for its Selebi Main and Selebi North Cu-Ni-Co sulphide deposits in Botswana, reporting larger inferred/indicated resources and contained nickel and copper. The company notes ongoing drilling to upgrade resources and potential future studies, without any immediate production change. | The U.S. Department of Energy selected REV Nickel for negotiations for up to $145 million to expand processing at Michigan’s Eagle Mine, producing nickel concentrate and processing battery-related feedstocks. The plan aims to extend mine life and eventually supply nickel for EV batteries, boosting expectations for domestic nickel availability. | The US Department of Labor added Indonesian nickel to a forced-labor/child-labor suspect commodities list, citing alleged forced labor in nickel industrial parks. The US embassy said the designation carries no sanctions or trade implications, aiming mainly to raise awareness. As a result, near-term nickel pricing impact appears limited. | Marquette County projects involving Eagle Mine and Revex Technologies were selected by the U.S. Department of Energy for up to $145 million in award negotiations. Facilities will process residual nickel in mine waste and produce nickel concentrate, while also extracting other metals from lithium-ion battery waste.

Core Nickel announces a $1.5 million flow-through private placement to fund Canadian exploration expenditures on its Thompson Nickel Belt nickel properties in Manitoba. The company highlights historical nickel resources and nearby mills and states it will renounce qualifying expenditures by Dec. 31, 2024. | Nickel Industries completed a 51% stake purchase in Indonesia’s Siduarsi nickel-cobalt project in West Papua. The company can raise its ownership to 82.5% if feasibility approval is granted. The deposit is suited to HPAL and could feed MHP and potentially nickel sulphate/cathode production, boosting nickel supply expectations. | Lifezone Metals, with BHP, starts the project financing process for Tanzania’s Kabanga Nickel Project and receives a Letter of Interest from the U.S. DFC, with Societe Generale named lead financial advisor. The update signals advancing funding for a major nickel deposit, supporting bullish sentiment for nickel. | Nornickel opened an R&D centre in St. Petersburg to study nickel-containing cathode active materials for EV batteries, with potential decisions within a year on producing cathode materials. The company is a major high-grade nickel producer and is positioning for an EV nickel supply chain despite weak profits and sanctions. | Revex Technologies was awarded $195 million in grants to invest in its REV Nickel Project in Michigan. The project will produce nickel concentrate and other critical minerals using mine tailings and spent lithium-ion batteries. Funding is expected to create jobs and support sustainable reshoring, potentially improving nickel supply expectations. | Core Nickel Corp. (CNCO) reports strong interest in a nickel flow-through private placement and upsizes the offering to 7.5 million FT units at $0.30 each for up to $2.25 million. The news is about financing and exploration properties, not nickel output or supply constraints. | Indonesia’s SOE minister inaugurated production at PT Freeport Indonesia’s Gresik smelter, saying nickel export value could rise to $40bn from $1–2bn, reflecting expanded refining and downstream migration. He also mentioned the government pushing further downstream of copper and gold, signaling broader copper processing intent.

Overnight markets: investors awaited the RBA, but commodity news drove metal focus. Gold hit a record. Copper rose on renewed expectations China may pursue stronger stimulus to bolster growth. Separately, Nickel Industries acquired 51% of Indonesia’s Siduarsi nickel-cobalt project, supporting a constructive nickel narrative. | The U.S. Department of Energy awarded Revex Technologies up to $145 million to expand manufacturing for EV battery supply chains, partnering with Eagle Mine to extract nickel in new ways. The company will focus on workforce readiness and community benefits, with construction of new facilities targeted for next year. | China’s nickel ore imports in August 2024 fell 10.79% YoY to 4.93mn mt, including laterite down 11.24% YoY, though shipments from the Philippines rose MoM on smelter restocking. Overall supply growth stayed limited as Indonesia bought nearly 2mn wmt of Philippine laterite, keeping nickel ore tight. | Tanzania’s Kabanga Nickel project, backed by US support and BHP, is discussed alongside investment and development of a planned Kahama refinery. The project targets first production in 2025 and includes projected annual outputs of 40,000 tonnes nickel and 6,000 tonnes copper, supporting constructive sentiment for nickel markets despite the later start. | GT Resources reported initial 2024 drill assays from its Canalask nickel-copper project in Yukon, including 2.12% nickel over 19.9m (and a higher-grade interval within). Copper and cobalt were also detected, while drilling outcomes are aimed at expanding mineralization. The results are broadly supportive for nickel sentiment.

Western Metallica announced the start of a first drilling program in October at its Luz Maria copper (Cu-Mo) porphyry prospect within the Caña Brava project in Peru, following permits. The news is an exploration sentiment driver with no immediate supply or demand change; nickel and aluminium are not addressed. | GGL Resources announced Teck’s option agreement for GGL’s Le Champ copper-molybdenum-gold porphyry target in Nevada, including cash option payments and a production-decision milestone. The release supports potential future copper/molybdenum development but provides no near-term output, so any immediate copper price impact is likely limited.

Carnaby Resources reported results from a VTEM magnetic survey at the Greater Duchess copper-gold JV, identifying multiple new undrilled conductive targets and highlighting the Mohawk corridor with prior 21m at 2% copper. Management expects immediate drilling focus and possible resource expansion, but no near-term output change is provided. | Next Week At A Glance highlights broad material-sector support from PBOC stimulus, but the only direct industrial-metals link is Nickel Industries buying a 51% stake in Indonesia’s Siduarsi nickel-cobalt project. This can marginally lift near-term nickel supply expectations, supporting prices modestly. | Villagers in Indonesia’s Sulawesi protest nickel exploration and HPAL smelting operations, citing land loss, environmental and safety concerns, and potential livelihood disruption. The story links Indonesia’s expanding nickel supply—including HPAL smelters—to persistent oversupply and weak price outlooks, implying bearish near-term sentiment for nickel. | The article investigates Indonesia’s nickel industry in Sulawesi, visiting nickel mines, smelters and local communities. It describes environmental pollution from mining and coal-powered smelting, unsafe working conditions, and alleged illegal land seizures. The narrative links the nickel boom’s social and environmental risks to potential challenges for nickel production and costs.

Power Nickel announced assay results from the first five holes of its summer 2024 drilling at the polymetallic Lion Zone discovery. Reported intervals range from about 5–12 metres with copper-equivalent grades between 2.36% and 5.93%, which may strengthen expectations for future nickel and copper resource development. | Power Nickel reported assay results from the Lion Zone discovery, including multiple copper- and nickel-bearing intercepts (e.g., several meters of %Cu and sub-% Ni, plus CuEq). While positive drill results can support sentiment for future nickel and copper supply, the impact is likely small given the exploration stage. | Tsingshan cut Indonesia ferronickel (nickel pig iron) output due to persistent nickel ore shortages linked to delays in mining quota approvals. Nickel production costs rose and some furnaces reportedly switched to other output. Philippine ore prices jumped sharply, while Chinese NPI prices rose only modestly, implying bearish near-term nickel sentiment. | QC Copper announced an all-share acquisition of Cuprum, adding the Thierry Copper Project and increasing its copper resource base. The Thierry asset historically produced copper and contains minor nickel, with references to past concentrate shipments. The deal is positioned for future development, not immediate production or supply changes. | Orecap announced QC Copper’s offer to acquire Cuprum in an all-share deal, adding the Thierry copper project and increasing QC Copper’s copper resource base. The transaction, expected to close by end-December 2024, strengthens Canada’s copper developer footprint. Thierry also historically produced concentrate with minor nickel content.

Nearctic Nickel Mines Inc. announced it has divested all remaining interest in Ungava Mines Inc., ending its indirect interest in UMEI. UMEI is a plaintiff in an ongoing action against the Quebec government alleging disguised expropriation. The update mainly changes company exposure, not disclosed nickel output or timing. | BHP said it will temporarily suspend Nickel West operations due to global nickel oversupply and high costs, which has pressured nickel prices. The piece argues the event could benefit Nico Resources’ Wingellina project via lower competitive pressure and investor attention, but impact on near-term nickel tightness is unclear. | Canada Nickel says it has started submitting the federal Impact Statement for its Crawford nickel sulphide project and expects to complete it within six weeks, targeting permits in 2025. The company frames the process as advancing permitting progress. This can modestly support near-term nickel market sentiment via improved supply outlook. | Prairie Operating reportedly gained additional production and increased acreage via a Nickel Road M&A transaction. The update points to improved nickel resource access and/or output potential. In the near term, incremental supply expectations can weigh on nickel pricing, with no clear information here affecting copper or aluminium.

Power Nickel released new drill assay results from the Lion Zone at its Nisk property, highlighting longer and higher-grade intersections with notable nickel and copper grades. The company says the discovery appears substantially bigger than previously envisioned, improving confidence in mineral continuity and supporting expectations for future Ni-Cu supply. | Power Nickel reported assay results from three additional drill holes at its Lion Zone polymetallic discovery. Hole 70 returned 32m of 0.18% Ni plus 3.62% Cu and other metals, alongside additional Au and Ag. The update strengthens the perceived resource potential and is mildly supportive for nickel and copper sentiment. | Grid Metals outlines 4 km of copper-dominant mineralization with associated nickel at its MM copper/nickel project’s Eagle gabbro target in Manitoba. The company plans inaugural drilling and additional geophysical surveys. It provides grab-sample grades but no new resource estimate or production impacts. | Grid Metals reported surface copper/nickel mineralization along a ~4 km Eagle gabbro corridor at its MM project in Manitoba, with grades up to 1.3% Cu and associated nickel/cobalt/PGM values. The company plans geophysical work and inaugural drilling this fall after permits, but no production or supply changes were announced. | Granite Creek Copper reports discovery of a new “Gap Zone” at its Carmacks copper-gold-silver project in Yukon, with copper mineralization in 3 of 4 holes and results pending from sampled sections. The company suggests potential to expand resources and mine life, but this is exploration news without immediate supply impact.

Lifezone Metals signed an MOU with JOGMEC to supply nickel from the Kabanga Nickel Project in Tanzania to Japanese battery demand. The arrangement supports JOGMEC’s acquisition of cleaner metals and references hydromet refining at Kahama. It signals incremental nickel supply visibility, with a likely supportive tone for nickel prices.

Elliott Associates lost its appeal against dismissal of its lawsuit against the LME over billions of dollars of cancelled nickel trades. The LME annulled $12 billion in nickel trades in March 2022 after a record price spike. The Court of Appeal upheld that the LME acted lawfully in cancelling to prevent a “death spiral.” | Elliott Investment Management’s UK appeal was rejected after the LME halted a runaway short squeeze in the nickel market. The decision follows the exchange’s controversial move to stop squeeze-driven volatility. Such regulatory intervention can cool speculative pressure and weigh on nickel prices near term. | BNN Bloomberg reports that Elliott has lost a UK appeal over the LME’s nickel market shutdown. The decision highlights ongoing issues around trading or settlement of nickel on the exchange, which can worsen near-term supply/availability expectations and pressure nickel prices over coming sessions. | A Court of Appeal upheld the LME’s decision to suspend and cancel nickel trades in March 2022 after a dramatic price spike. The court said the LME acted lawfully to prevent a potential “death spiral.” The article notes nickel’s importance to stainless and batteries, but the judgment is retrospective.

EV Nickel reported maiden drilling results at the CarLang trend, intersecting near-surface nickel mineralization in new zones (B, C and E) over more than 7 km of strike. CarLang C delivered the highest grades to date, including up to 0.96% Ni over short intervals, extending the potential resource footprint. | First Atlantic Nickel Corp. began core drilling at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland after summer sampling expanded visible awaruite (a naturally occurring nickel-iron alloy) targets along a 30 km trend. The company aims to test new, widely spaced nickel targets, with results pending, implying limited immediate market impact. | EV Nickel Inc. announced maiden diamond drilling at the CarLang trend intersected three new zones of near-surface nickel mineralization, including the highest grades to date. Results included intervals up to 0.96% Ni and a 7km strike extension, with mineralization appearing magmatic. The discovery remains open for expansion.

Nickel 28 Capital Corp. announced confirmed cash distributions from its 8.56% interest in the Ramu nickel-cobalt project, including about US$4.0 million for H1 2024 and repayment of part of its construction debt. Management attributes distributions to efficient operations despite low nickel and cobalt prices, with timing pending confirmation. | The interview says Indonesia’s nickel industry is scaling into an EV battery-material ecosystem, citing commissioned precursor and lithium refining capacity and existing copper-foil operations near Freeport. It also notes forced-labor/environmental allegations and IRA rules limiting subsidized EV inputs from highly foreign-controlled entities, plus LFP’s nickel-free shift. | Canada Nickel received a C$4.38 million conditional contribution from Canada’s Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund to fund electrical infrastructure pre-implementation studies for its Crawford nickel sulphide project. The studies run April 2024 to June 2025, supporting project development and eventual nickel production, but it’s early-stage financing.

Core Nickel Corp. announced a non-brokered private placement of up to $252,000 to fund general working capital, including potential flow-through unit proceeds. As a nickel explorer, the financing may support future exploration, but it provides no immediate production or supply impact, suggesting limited near-term effect on nickel prices.

BHP is placing its WA Nickel West division on care and maintenance for at least three years, cutting jobs and effectively closing parts of Australia’s nickel supply chain. The report links the decision to global nickel oversupply and falling prices, alongside other mine closures including First Quantum’s Ravensthorpe. | BHP has shut down production at its Kalgoorlie Nickel Smelter, placing it on care and maintenance after decades of continuous operation. The closure follows broader shutdowns in Western Australia’s nickel mines, with nickel export values forecast to fall sharply. Job losses underscore tightening local supply prospects for nickel. | Suvo Strategic Minerals reports lab trials showing nickel slag can be converted into high-strength, low-carbon cement. The nickel slag is supplied by Indonesia producer PT Huadi Nickel-Alloy Indonesia, which has large stockpiles. Further trials aim to qualify additional cement properties, supporting potential offtake arrangements.

Eramet cut its 2024 targets for Comilog and PT Weda Bay Nickel amid weaker carbon steel demand in China and fewer nickel ore sales permits from Indonesia than expected. Nickel ore external volume for PT WBN was revised down to 29 million wet tons, indicating reduced supply expectations. | Vale reports a six-year high in iron ore output, and the article states that its copper and nickel production also increased. Stronger production trends can improve near-term supply expectations, which generally supports prices for copper and nickel over the following days to weeks. | Vale reported six-year-high iron ore output, and the title also notes that copper and nickel production climbed. If investors expect improved near-term supply from Vale, it can pressure copper and nickel prices over the following days as market balances adjust.

Grid Metals reports receiving permits and commencing a maiden drill program at the Eagle gabbro within its MM copper/nickel project in Manitoba, with geophysical surveys running in tandem. Assays are expected by mid-November/early next month. The update is exploratory and does not change near-term supply. | Grid Metals reports the initiation of its maiden drill program at the Eagle gabbro within its MM copper/nickel project in Manitoba, supported by permits and geophysical surveys. Management expects initial assays by mid-November and cites potential copper-dominant mineralization. The update is exploratory and not directly tied to near-term production changes. | Michigan DNR acquired 8,392 acres of mineral rights in Porcupine Mountains Wilderness State Park in a mineral-for-mineral land exchange, citing prospective copper and nickel resources. The article stresses the transaction does not impact Highland Copper’s Copperwood project, which would access minerals outside the park with no surface disturbance. | Global Ferronickel Holdings (FNI) is in talks with Asian and European firms to build a nickel processing facility, adding to existing plants in Palawan and Surigao run by Nickel Asia. The government is also seeking incentives and clearer rules for critical-mineral processing, which supports nickel supply outlook sentiment.

Indonesia’s Energy and Mineral Resources Minister Bahlil Lahadalia said the nickel downstream industry relies on foreign workers to speed progress. He cited faster smelter construction by Chinese workers and described efforts to repatriate foreign labor when local skills are available, though transitions cannot happen instantly. | Indonesia’s mining minister said the government will manage nickel ore supply and demand to support prices, using smelter prices and demand as parameters for market balance. He cited quota-approval delays that forced some smelters to import ores from the Philippines, highlighting near-term supply-control efforts. | SMM reports Indonesia’s Ministry of Mines adjusted PT WBN’s laterite nickel ore sales quota (2024-2026), raising annual sales volume to 32 mln wmt. Markets reacted with bearish expectations for Indonesian nickel ore premiums, though current prices stay stable due to already-signed orders. Indonesia may soften premiums later this month; Philippines remains firmer.

SMM data show China’s September nickel ore imports fell 7.4% MoM and 23.5% YoY, with declines across both sulfide and oxide. Philippine ore remains the main source, but Indonesia’s RKAB quota progress expected in October may reduce Philippine demand and shift exports toward China, with possible typhoon-driven shipping delays.

SMM reports nickel prices have fallen sharply, with SHFE dropping below 126,000 yuan/mt and LME trading around $16,300–$16,500. The main drivers are improved Indonesian nickel-ore supply expectations via RKAB approvals and broader macro risk aversion ahead of the US election. High-grade NPI remains firmer but may retreat on weakening stainless fundamentals.

Anglo American said Q3 copper output fell 13% year on year because of a planned closure of a processing plant at its Los Bronces mine in Chile. It also flagged a potential coal-division sale. Nickel and platinum guidance was raised, but the reported copper production decline is the main near-term pressure. | Anglo American said copper production fell 13% quarter-on-quarter due to the planned closure of Chile’s Los Bronces plant, partially offset by higher grades at El Soldado and improvements at Quellaveco expected later. Nickel output rose 6%, though Codemin refinery issues partially offset gains. | Anglo American said Q3 copper production fell to 181Kt versus 209Kt, citing planned closure of Los Bronces, partially offset by higher grades at El Soldado. Nickel production rose and full-year nickel guidance was raised to 38-39Kt. Annual copper outlook remains within 730-790Kt, tempering market impact. | Anglo American said Q3 copper output fell to 181Kt from 209Kt due to the planned closure of the smaller, costlier Los Bronces plant, partially offset by higher grades at El Soldado. Nickel output rose to 9.9kt and full-year nickel guidance was raised, while copper guidance stayed broadly within 730–790Kt. | Core Nickel Corp. announced changes to its board, welcoming Paul Reid and Marc Pais, and noting upcoming early-2025 drilling/testing plans on its Thompson Nickel Belt nickel projects near existing mills. The article is primarily corporate governance with no immediate production, supply, or inventory impact for nickel prices. | Talon Metals announces its maiden drill hole at the Boulderdash target in Michigan intersected 99.92 meters of copper-nickel mineralization, including intervals with stated copper and nickel equivalents. The company plans additional drilling and EM surveys. The discovery is early-stage, near existing nickel operations, but not production. | Talon Metals announced its first drill hole at the Boulderdash target in Michigan intersected 99.92 meters of copper-nickel mineralization, including high-grade CuEq/NiEq intervals, and plans additional drilling and EM surveys. The release frames it as a potential domestic critical minerals resource near an existing U.S. nickel mine, but provides no production. | Indonesia’s new government targets higher economic growth and plans downstream nickel capacity expansion via hydrometallurgy, which could support nickel demand and output. At the same time, the government aims to raise fiscal revenue and may adjust taxes (higher VAT, potential CIT reduction), creating mixed effects on mining and processing costs, so near-term price impact is uncertain. | NewRange Copper Nickel held an open house to build momentum for plans to develop two copper-nickel deposits near Hoyt Lakes. The project follows Glencore/Teck’s earlier PolyMet efforts that faced permit reversals and lawsuits. NewRange seeks to reapply for key permits in 2025, with mining not expected before 2030. | NewRange Copper Nickel held an open house to build momentum for two copper-nickel deposits near Hoyt Lakes, with plans to salvage and refurbish an old concentrator. The project faces permitting and legal setbacks, including a revoked Clean Water Act permit, and operations are not expected before 2030, making near-term price effects limited. | Fastmarkets says Chinese nickel pig iron (NPI) prices climbed 20-30 yuan to 1,030-1,050 yuan per nickel unit, the highest since Nov 10, 2023. A major stainless steel tender lifted NPI offers on rumors of joint feedstock price hikes, citing Indonesia ore supply shortages and higher production costs.

Nickel premiums were flat globally for full-plate cathodes, 4×4 cathodes, and briquettes, even as the LME three-month nickel contract hit its lowest 5pm close since mid-September. LME warehouse stocks in Rotterdam are ample and import arbitrage losses narrowed slightly. | Flying Nickel announced a final court order for an arrangement with Norway House Cree Nation to acquire the company’s Minago nickel project in Manitoba for $8 million cash and related share surrender and royalties. Closing is expected by end of October, subject to customary approvals. No operational or supply changes are detailed.

Stillwater Critical Minerals signed a letter of intent for Granite Creek Copper to acquire a 90% interest in the Duke Island Cu-Ni-PGE project in Alaska. The announcement is mainly a project ownership and advancement plan, with no near-term production impact specified, limiting immediate effects on copper and nickel prices.

The piece argues that Indonesia’s nickel downstreaming push—despite rapid smelter growth and cheap feedstock—may not translate into significant battery capacity or higher nickel value. It notes most nickel output still goes to stainless steel and that foreign competitors are bypassing steps. Beijing’s advice to keep EV components in China raises stakes. | Perseverance Metals reported new high-grade nickel discoveries at its Lac Gayot project (up to ~1.54% Ni in surface samples) and highlighted an airborne HeliTEM2 survey over the property. The company continues nickel-copper-PGE exploration, but provides no production or confirmed near-term supply changes. | Perseverance Metals reported new high-grade nickel discoveries at its Lac Gayot nickel-copper-PGE project in Québec, including up to 1.54% nickel in surface samples, plus an airborne HeliTEM survey. The company also identified spodumene-bearing pegmatites, but the direct market impact is mainly nickel-copper exploration sentiment. | Sterling Metals reported high-grade copper and molybdenum values from surface grab samples at its Copper Road porphyry target in Ontario, with an ongoing 3D IP survey to guide drilling. The article is copper-related but contains no production or reserve/timing updates, limiting any near-term price impact. | Sterling Metals reported initial Phase 1 exploration results at its Copper Road project in Ontario, with surface grab samples returning up to 15.9% Cu and 4.84% Mo, plus evidence of a ~2 km footprint of porphyry mineralization. The news is development-focused and unlikely to materially move near-term copper prices, especially beyond sentiment.

Saudi Arabia’s Ma’aden/Manara says it is in advanced talks over acquiring a minority stake in a Zambian copper mine, expecting a deal by year-end. Prior Reuters sources linked the stake purchase to First Quantum Minerals’ Zambian copper and nickel assets, but no immediate operational changes are mentioned. | Pan Global initiated an IP geophysical survey at the Bravo copper target within Spain’s Escacena Project to refine drilling locations. Gravity, soil geochemistry, and helicopter EM anomalies suggest concealed VMS sulphide mineralization possibly hosting copper. Financing is on track, but no drilling results or output changes are announced. | BeMetals announced 2024 shallow aircore drilling at Zambia’s Pangeni Copper Project, extending the Nkala Zone footprint and identifying several new copper anomalies, with further core results expected in coming weeks. The news is exploration-focused and does not indicate immediate production or market supply changes for copper. | Glencore reported lower nickel production in the first three quarters of 2024, driven by Koniambo’s move to care and maintenance. Higher output from Australia’s Murrin Murrin partly offset the decline, and with IRA-compliant briquettes becoming more constrained, the nickel briquette premium in the US rose sharply. | Reuters reports companies are restarting shuttered copper mines and other abandoned assets to fast-track additional supply for the clean-energy and AI demand outlook. Examples include Canada, Quebec and Spain reopenings, with output targets over the next years. The revival is largely driven by recent high copper (and nickel) prices. | Talon Metals announced discovery of 99.92 meters of copper and nickel mineralization at the Boulderdash target in Michigan’s Upper Peninsula. Using surface prospecting, geophysics, and maiden drilling, the company says results confirm the source of mineralized boulders. It highlights potential domestic critical mineral supply. | Flying Nickel Mining Corp. announced the closing of an $8 million sale of its Minago Nickel Project to Norway House Cree Nation via a purchaser entity. The transaction transfers the project and related obligations and cancels NHCN’s shareholding. It signals asset repositioning but provides no immediate production or supply figures affecting nickel prices.

MIT researchers assess nickel’s growing role in EVs and renewables and the risks in its supply chain, emphasizing Indonesia’s dominance and coal-based processing’s high emissions, plus land, pollution, and social impacts. Policymakers are urged to enforce sustainability standards, but no immediate market action is announced.

SolGold began geotechnical investigations at its Cascabel copper-gold porphyry project in Ecuador, including drilling and seismic assessments to refine ground conditions for mine infrastructure. The pre-feasibility plan targets 3.2Mt copper (0.60%) and schedules further feasibility work. The step is incremental and not a near-term copper supply shock. | Copper and other metals reportedly gained as the U.S. dollar weakened and markets built hopes for China stimulus. The combination tends to improve risk sentiment and expected industrial demand, generally supporting prices for industrial metals like copper, aluminium, and nickel over the coming days. | Sumitomo is resuming operations at its Ambatovy nickel project in Madagascar after production was paused by a pipeline rupture. The mine has missed prior output targets and faced write-downs, and a nickel export revenue decline is noted. With the market already weak, restored supply expectations may support nickel prices.

L&F says it will start producing NCM cathodes with 95% nickel content for BEV lithium-ion batteries by end-2024, with shipments to cell manufacturers beginning in December. The company cites technology progress from N90 to N95 and expects more volume, supporting incremental nickel demand sentiment. | Inomin and Sumitomo have signed a non-binding term sheet to jointly develop the Beaver-Lynx nickel project in British Columbia. Sumitomo could earn up to 80% via C$8m in exploration spend in two phases. Early results suggest significant nickel volumes, though the JV remains subject to due diligence. | South Korean materials maker L&F plans to mass produce NCM battery cathodes with 95% nickel content starting in December and supply Korean battery makers and US EV manufacturers. The move suggests higher near-term consumption of nickel-rich cathode materials, which can lift short-term nickel demand expectations and support nickel prices. | Canada Nickel announced it intersected 3.9 metres of massive sulphide within 12.7 metres of mineralization at its Bannockburn Property near Timmins, Ontario. The company said this is the first massive sulphide drilled in the Timmins Nickel District, suggesting improved prospects for nickel mineralization at the site.

Poseidon Nickel said its Windarra tailings agreement with Encore Minerals is now unconditional after meeting conditions. The project—about 720 km from Perth—includes processing gold tailings using glycine leaching technology and could begin production, receiving upfront payments and royalties. Better development prospects likely support nickel sentiment near term.

The piece notes Trump’s US election win could bring tariff hikes on China, pressuring base metals in the short term. Offsetting that, brokers see an accelerating copper deficit from electrification demand and expect aluminium supply deficits. Nickel is expected to remain weak with rising LME inventories and softer demand. | The piece links Donald Trump’s US election win to potential tariff hikes on China, a short-term headwind for base metals. It also highlights RBC’s view of accelerating copper deficits from electrification and Wilsons’ forecast of an aluminium swing to deficit as China’s supply growth is constrained. Nickel remains pressured by inventories and soft demand.

Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt is seeking about $2.7bn financing for a Ford-backed HPAL nickel processing facility in Indonesia, using ore from Vale’s Pomalaa Block mine to produce battery-grade nickel. The fundraising comes while nickel prices are subdued near a four-year low due to weaker stainless and slower EV demand growth.

Indonesia’s PT Vale Indonesia and GEM signed a collaboration for a US$1.4bn HPAL project in Central Sulawesi aimed at a net-zero processing site for nickel. The project targets at least 60,000 tonnes of contained nickel in MHP annually, alongside R&D, ESG initiatives, and community development. | Canada Nickel announced assay results from drillhole BAN24-18 at its Bannockburn project, including 3.95% nickel over 4 metres and up to 4.65% nickel over 1 metre. The interval also reports minor copper (0.40% over 12 metres). The update supports bullish near-term sentiment for nickel.

Premium Nickel Resources extended the study phase for its Selebi Mines project by one year to complete an economic study and a US$25 million milestone payment. It continues drilling and metallurgical/engineering work, and reports historic drill core data including nickel and copper to support an upcoming resource estimate. | GEM and PT Vale Indonesia agreed on a $1.4bn nickel HPAL facility in Sulawesi, aimed at producing at least 60,000t/year of contained nickel in MHP for battery use. The project includes R&D and ESG components and may expand into anode/precursor production downstream.

Cumberland Resources Nickel Corp. announced a non-brokered private placement of units priced at $0.03, raising up to $750,000. Net proceeds will be used for property development and working capital related to its St. Anthony nickel property in Newfoundland. The financing could support near-term project plans, slightly improving nickel sentiment. | Indonesia’s hotel association raised concerns about a nickel project on Gag Island in Raja Ampat, arguing mining could harm the marine ecosystem and tourism. Antam’s unit bought 30% of a nickel smelter for $102 million, potentially accelerating mining. The piece highlights environmental risks more than immediate supply impacts. | QC Copper completed a cost-effective resampling program on historical drill core at the Thierry Copper Project (K1 Zone) to better delineate disseminated chalcopyrite/pyrite mineralization and potentially expand resources. Cuprum owns the project; a related business combination is expected to close in December. No immediate production or supply impacts are described. | Markets are watching for the incoming US administration to tighten IRA Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) rules, likely limiting Chinese-linked nickel from qualifying for EV tax credits. Allegations of nickel misreported as IRA-compliant are also driving scrutiny. Analysts expect automakers and battery makers to rush to secure non-FEOC nickel units, tightening supply expectations. | Nickel prices have slid toward a four-year low as weaker-than-expected electric vehicle demand and China’s real estate downturn weigh on expectations for battery-metal demand. The move highlights how softer end-demand in EVs and construction can quickly pressure nickel pricing, erasing prior gains tied to the EV boom.

Class 1 Nickel filed an NI 43-101 Technical Report supporting an updated Mineral Resource Estimate for the Dundonald South nickel sulphide deposit, including higher indicated nickel tonnage and continued drilling plans. While it does not change production, the update is supportive for near-term nickel sentiment and expectations.

New Caledonian nickel producer Prony Resources will restart operations from Monday after a six-month shutdown linked to riots. The restart follows restored power, water and road access, though no production timeline is provided. Wider unrest has disrupted other local producers, so supply recovery could support nickel prices. | High-nickel pig iron prices are plunging further, with SMM 8-12% NPI averaging 1,005.2 yuan per nickel point and the Indonesian NPI FOB index also falling. Weak stainless demand/off-season and sluggish procurement coincide with macro pressure (Fed/LEM), pushing both NPI and pure nickel downward and widening the NPI/electrolytic nickel discount. | Indonesia’s nickel ore imports climbed to 9.3 million tons in January–October 2024, versus 161,917 tons a year earlier. Reuters links the surge to record purchases from the Philippines since April as smelter demand increased, while Indonesia delayed mining quotas amid heavy rains disrupting mining activity. | SMM reports Indonesian laterite nickel ore premiums are flat and prices stable amid uncertainty over RKAB quota consumption by year-end. In the Philippines, limited northern shipments and firmer FOB/CIF mid-grade nickel ore pricing are gradually strengthening prices, though weakening domestic high-grade NPI tempers restocking. Overall nickel ore sentiment is supportive short term.

The article discusses nickel price momentum and volatility, citing oversupply from Indonesia and mine curtailments elsewhere, alongside weaker Chinese economic/demand conditions. It notes Chinese stimulus briefly lifted nickel prices in September, but prices retreated after the initial surge, implying near-term bearish pressure. | The piece highlights nickel’s volatile first half and a Q3 oversupply situation driven by increased laterite supply from Indonesia, prompting curtailments in other regions. It cites weak Chinese demand despite stimulus-induced price support in September, noting prices retreated again. It frames Carpenter Technology as a nickel alloys producer. | The piece reviews nickel market conditions, citing oversupply of nickel from Asian/Indonesian sources and weak Chinese demand due to a strained real estate sector and deflation. It notes brief price support from Chinese stimulus in September, but prices later retreated, keeping nickel pressure negative. | The article reviews nickel market conditions affecting “best nickel stocks,” noting strong H1 momentum but Q3 2024 volatility. It cites laterite nickel supply growth from Indonesia/Asia driving oversupply, while weak Chinese demand from deflation and real estate pressures offset battery demand growth, and September stimulus only temporarily supported prices before retreating. | The piece highlights strong but volatile nickel momentum in H1 and Q3 2024, attributing weakness to oversupply of laterite nickel from Indonesia and mine curtailments in other regions, while Chinese demand remains constrained by a difficult real-estate backdrop. A September stimulus briefly supported prices, but they retreated again. | The article reviews nickel price momentum and volatility, citing oversupply of laterite nickel from Indonesia and weaker Chinese demand from stainless-steel and broader economic deflation. It notes short-lived support from Chinese stimulus in September. It also mentions LEG uses nickel-based alloys but provides no direct nickel supply/demand catalyst. | The piece reviews nickel market conditions and ATI’s positioning. It highlights H1 strength but Q3 volatility tied to oversupply from Indonesia, mine curtailments elsewhere, and weak Chinese demand. Even after Chinese stimulus briefly supported nickel prices in September, prices retreated, implying ongoing downside risk for nickel. | The article discusses nickel’s H1 momentum but highlights Q3 2024 volatility driven by Indonesian supply growth, mine curtailments elsewhere, and persistent oversupply. It notes China demand softness from macro issues despite stimulus measures that briefly lifted nickel prices before prices retreated again. | The article reviews nickel price momentum and volatility in 2024, citing Indonesian laterite supply, nickel oversupply, and China stimulus-induced swings in demand. It also highlights Vale’s involvement in nickel and copper and an agreement for a $1.42B Indonesian nickel plant, but provides no clear short-term market disruption.

The piece says a Trump-era, more protectionist trade environment could make an Indonesia–US free-trade agreement unlikely. It also notes the IRA favors EV supply chains in US/FTA countries, affecting Indonesian nickel shipments that are widely sent to China, while Tesla may gradually reduce nickel dependence. | Indonesia’s Energy and Mineral Resources Ministry defended local smelters’ imports of nickel ore despite abundant domestic resources. It said the imports follow individual company strategy and help extend domestic nickel supply as reserves deplete. Indonesia also remains a major producer but imported much nickel ore from the Philippines in 2023 and 2024. | Prony Resources plans to restart operations at the Goro mine and battery-grade nickel plant in New Caledonia after a six-month suspension from local riots. The workforce return revives a major regional nickel source (with battery-grade nickel and cobalt output), potentially lifting short-term supply expectations and weighing on nickel prices. | Talon Metals’ drilling at the Boulderdash target in Michigan intersected substantial copper-nickel sulphide intervals across several drill holes, including up to 110.3 meters of mineralization with up to 40% sulphides, and indicators of possible massive sulphides. Assays are pending; results could modestly support copper and nickel sentiment. | Vital Battery Metals reported assay results from its Sting Copper Project, including a grab sample with 9.3% Cu and soil sampling showing a 300+ meter Cu–Zn–As anomaly. The update supports exploration potential but does not indicate near-term supply changes, limiting likely immediate effects on copper prices and none on nickel or aluminium.

SMM reports Indonesia is considering changes to the non-tax state revenue (PNBP) interpretation and resource fee structure for laterite nickel ore. A proposed 2% royalty for ore below 1.5% grade is aimed at supporting battery materials. It also updates RKAB approvals and quota volumes for 2024-2026. | Xanadu Mines announced four new high-grade copper targets at its Sant Tolgoi project in Mongolia, with surface rock-chip assays up to 2.1% Cu and geophysical anomalies pointing to mineralised intrusions. The planned reconnaissance drilling will test for massive sulphide copper and possibly nickel, but no production is yet confirmed. | Electra Battery Materials received a ten-year exploration permit from the US Forest Service covering 91 drill pad locations for its Idaho Cobalt Belt projects, including the Iron Creek copper-cobalt deposit. The permit adds regulatory certainty to resume exploration, but the news is more developmental than an immediate supply change. | Xanadu Mines announced high-grade surface copper assay results from its Sant Tolgoi project in Mongolia, identifying four drill targets based on geochemistry and geophysics, with planned reconnaissance drilling in Q2 2025. Assays show copper up to 2.1% and very low nickel (<0.06%) at surface, implying exploration potential only.

Ivanhoe Mines announced the official reopening of the Kipushi zinc-copper-germanium mine in the DRC. The restart is expected to bring additional copper supply from the existing Kamoa-Kakula/Kipushi complex over time.
