Ceremonial confirmation supports near-term optimism on copper output and project ramp-up. | Alaska Energy Metals reported assay results from three drill holes at the Canwell claim block, intersecting wide zones of nickel-bearing polymetallic mineralization and providing evidence of sulfide/metallic-hosted nickel. The company plans further drilling, but the update is not production-related, limiting immediate supply impact.

Xanadu Mines announced strong surface copper assay results from its Sant Tolgoi Project in Mongolia and identified four drill targets. The company plans 3,000m of reconnaissance drilling to test for high-grade massive sulphide copper and nickel, with follow-up drilling expected in early 2025.

Aldebaran announced an updated mineral resource estimate for its Altar copper-gold project in Argentina, showing a substantial increase versus 2021, including 22.01 billion pounds of copper. The company also plans the next study work (PEA in Q2 2025), adding near-term development optimism for copper.

Minnesota’s Department of Natural Resources has delayed its decision on a key permit for a proposed copper-nickel mine. The delay may push back timelines for potential future production, improving near-term supply expectations. This is supportive for both copper and nickel prices, though magnitude is uncertain while the project remains pending. | Minnesota DNR delayed its decision on a key permit for a proposed copper-nickel mine. The delay likely postpones potential production timelines, increasing near-term uncertainty around future supply. That typically supports bearish sentiment for copper and nickel as markets recalibrate expectations for when the project could contribute output. | First Atlantic Nickel applied for additional drill permits and plans a direct access route to accelerate winter drilling at the RPM Zone. The discovery hole intersected visible awaruite (nickel-iron alloy) mineralization from surface to depth. Phase 2 drilling is planned pending permits and assay results. | The article is focused on cobalt but repeatedly ties it to copper and nickel. It forecasts copper prices to remain strong, supporting further mined supply growth in the DRC, while noting nickel’s short-term price weakness despite Indonesia’s expanding HPAL-linked output. Net signals for copper vs nickel are mixed.

Prosecutors allege nickel trading businessman Ng Yu Zhi ran a fraud in which investors were promised discounted nickel purchases from Poseidon Nickel Limited and profitable resale. The Envy entities allegedly never bought or sold nickel, instead using new investor funds to pay earlier ones, via forged contracts and documents. | GT Resources announced a Phase I BHEM geophysical survey and reconnaissance work at its North Rock copper-nickel-PGE project in Ontario. The program targets historic holes to find net-textured to massive sulphides beneath shallow drilling, with a historic copper grade noted, but no near-term production impact. | GT Resources announced a BHEM survey and reconnaissance at its North Rock copper–nickel–PGE project in Ontario, targeting historic holes around the Beaver Pond zone. The work aims to identify new sulphide conductors beneath shallow drilling. It is exploration activity with no immediate production or market flow impact.

A Singapore businessman, accused of running a S$1.46 billion nickel investment scam, allegedly promised profits from buying and selling physical nickel but prosecutors say no nickel trades occurred. Instead, earlier investors were paid with new funds. The case centers on fraud, money laundering, and lavish personal spending.

A Singapore trial describes a $1.5 billion “nickel trading” scam in which 947 investors were duped after claims that nickel could be bought at a discount from an Australian mine and resold at profits. Prosecutors say no nickel was bought or sold; payments to earlier investors came from new money. | Minnesota’s DNR is postponing a decision on permits for NewRange Copper Nickel’s proposed copper and nickel mine on the Iron Range. The state has paused consideration until August next year due to regulatory and legal challenges, leaving output uncertain and potentially constraining future supply expectations. | Minnesota’s DNR is postponing its permit decision on the proposed NewRange Copper Nickel project in northeastern Minnesota, pausing further consideration until August next year. The delay stems from regulatory and legal challenges, potentially pushing back future copper and nickel supply. | Indonesia is seeking investors to fund delayed bauxite downstreaming and smelter development, after earlier plans were hindered by financing and several smelter projects were postponed. With a June 2023 ban on raw bauxite ore exports and only four of 12 smelters ready, the push to accelerate processing may support aluminium supply expectations.

Western Australia’s nickel industry has “collapsed” this year with multiple mine closures and job losses, attributed to cheaper Indonesian nickel flooding global markets. Analysts expect nickel to remain oversupplied until at least 2029. While green/nickel-cobalt projects continue, the current market dynamics point to weak pricing near term.

VR Resources says drilling has begun at its Empire copper–gold project in Ontario, targeting strong IP (chargeability) anomalies identified by a recent DCIP survey and related EM conductivity features. Results are preliminary and no resource or production changes are disclosed, limiting near-term implications mainly to copper sentiment.

CaNickel extended an option agreement with Cobalt One and Blackstone to purchase the Bucko Lake nickel sulfide mine, shifting the option deadline to January 3, 2025. If exercised and approvals are obtained, the buyers would advance permitting and development toward commercial production, improving nickel project outlook despite current market challenges. | SMM highlights Indonesia’s potential RKAB quota policy refinements and tighter supervision of environmental/compliance rules, alongside SIMBARA-driven application/approval delays that could affect Q1 supply. It also notes execution risks from weather and uncertainty over sustained nickel ore inflows from the Philippines, affecting supply tightness for next year.

Lifezone Metals reported a December 2024 mineral resource update at its Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, upgrading high-confidence Measured and Indicated resources and adding contained nickel and copper. The release improves resource confidence and discusses potential partner/offtake, but it is not a production or reserve change. | Lifezone Metals announced an upgrade of measured and indicated mineral resources at its Kabanga nickel project in Tanzania, adding contained nickel and reporting higher-confidence resources. The resource update also includes associated copper grades. Because it does not change near-term mine supply or production, any effect on copper and nickel prices should be modest. | Lifezone Metals announced a December 2024 Mineral Resource Update for its Kabanga project in Tanzania, upgrading Measured and Indicated resources with higher contained nickel and copper grades, plus a revised nickel-equivalent cut-off. The release supports longer-term project value but provides no near-term production or supply impact. | Canada Nickel reported assay results from drill BAN24-20 at its Bannockburn nickel sulphide project, including 1.11% nickel over 10.1 metres (and up to 1.32% Ni over 4.4 metres). The company is continuing follow-up drilling and BHEM surveys, but no production or supply changes are announced. | Interros, Nornickel’s largest shareholder, objected to Rusal’s London lawsuit aimed at influencing Nornickel’s dividend policy. The dispute could force sensitive disclosures under geopolitical conditions. Nornickel is a major nickel producer, while Rusal uses dividends to service debt; however, no immediate production or trade changes are cited. | Ford is positioning for EV growth by investing in Indonesia, the world’s largest nickel producer. The move is linked to a $4.5 billion nickel processing project with Vale and Huayou, targeting battery cathode supply. Indonesia also pushes higher local-content rules for EVs, reinforcing nickel demand expectations.

Power Nickel says shareholders and courts approved the spin-out of its Golden Ivan and certain Chilean exploration assets into Chilean Metals Inc., with dividends payable in the new shares. It also reports drilling success at the Nisk Project, extending the Lion Zone 550 meters along strike, and adds a strategic advisor. | High-grade NPI prices (8–12%) fell to 952.2 yuan/mtu and Indonesian NPI FOB declined. Weak stainless steel spot and seasonal surplus weigh on NPI demand, keeping refined nickel rangebound amid limited supportive signals. The NPI vs refined nickel discount narrowed slightly, but overall nickel sentiment remains pressured. | Marvel Discovery Corp. says it is returning to Ontario’s Blackfly gold project and Quebec’s Duhamel nickel-copper-cobalt project, discontinuing uranium options. The release cites historical Ni/Cu/Co mineralization and corridor potential but provides no production or definitive near-term supply changes, implying limited immediate impact on copper/nickel prices.

GT Resources reported 2024 drill results from the Canalask Nickel-Copper Project in Yukon, including copper-rich skarn-like mineralization and nickel intersections (e.g., up to ~2.4% Ni and ~1.84% Cu in sections). The company frames results as expanding exploration targets, with no immediate production impact. | GT Resources reported final 2024 drill results at its Yukon Canalask nickel-copper project, including copper-rich and nickel-rich intercepts (skarn-like Cu-Au and peridotite-hosted Ni with PGE anomalism). The findings expand geological upside but do not imply near-term production changes, limiting impact on short-term copper and nickel prices. | Benton Resources reports recommenced drilling at its Great Burnt copper deposit and expands the South Pond gold-copper zone to over 2.3 km, including sulphide intersections with assays partly pending. It also describes a nickel target from an ultramafic anomaly with limited rock sampling. Overall, this is exploratory and small scale. | Norilsk Nickel expects a ~150,000 tonne nickel market surplus in 2024-2025, mainly in high-grade nickel, driven by new Indonesia capacity and China cathode nickel inflows, alongside growing exchange stocks. The company also notes many producers are unprofitable at current prices, implying further selling pressure unless demand tightens quickly.

Indonesia’s Energy and Mineral Resources Ministry said it plans further limits on nickel product output to avoid oversupply and keep prices higher, while protecting domestic miners. It did not specify timing or the exact restriction terms, but the measure could tighten global nickel supply given Indonesia’s market dominance. | Canada Nickel reported positive exploration drill results from Mann West and Mann South, including nickel intercepts above 0.3% over multiple intervals and a new discovery. The company also noted anomalous PGM horizons and overall drilling progress on 14 properties, targeting additional resources in 2025. | ING flags persistent downside risks for nickel, citing Indonesia-driven supply growth after the 2020 ore export ban, a continuing global surplus, and rising LME Class 1 stocks with more Chinese/Indonesian deliveries. Demand headwinds include weaker stainless steel activity and slower EV/battery growth, keeping prices under pressure. | Canada Nickel Company reports positive exploration and assay updates from drilling at the Mann West/Central/South properties in Ontario’s Timmins Nickel District. The program intersected nickel sulphide mineralization (pentlandite/heazlewoodite) and is expected to support a first resource estimate in Q1 2025, boosting nickel supply expectations.

Grid Metals and Teck signed an option/JV agreement to explore the Makwa Ni-Cu-PGM-cobalt project in Manitoba. Teck can earn up to 70% by funding exploration expenditures and cash payments, with an initial drilling campaign anticipated. The update may influence sentiment for nickel and copper, but not near-term supply. | Barksdale Resources said its Sunnyside drilling in Arizona intercepted copper-dominant semi-massive sulfides in a CRD-style target, including chalcopyrite-rich intervals, with drilling continuing deeper. Assays are pending, but the copper-rich discovery and proximity to known mineralized districts could support near-term copper sentiment. | EV Nickel reports QEMSCAN/EPMA results for a new zone confirming nickel sulphides (millerite and heazlewoodite) that could improve recovery rates and produce higher-grade nickel concentrate, with chalcopyrite also present as a dominant copper mineral. The company plans follow-up metallurgical flotation tests and 2025 drilling to define a resource.

The piece discusses copper market fundamentals and focuses on BHP as a top copper stock for hedge funds. It cites copper surplus/supply and China demand headwinds, but highlights BHP’s rising copper output and a new Argentina copper joint venture, which supports near-term copper sentiment. | Grid Metals signed an option/JV agreement with Teck Resources to develop the Makwa nickel project in Manitoba. Teck may earn up to 70% by funding C$15.7m in expenditures with staged cash payments and further exploration commitments. The deal may support future nickel supply, but near-term price impact is likely limited. | Canada Nickel says Taykwa Tagamou Nation will invest $20 million via convertible notes, potentially taking an 8.3% equity stake, and gaining a board seat for its Crawford nickel-cobalt project. The funding reduces uncertainty amid junior mining capital crunches and supports expectations for future nickel supply, modestly bullish for nickel.

SMM reports stable nickel ore pricing this week: Philippine FOB prices around $29/wmt for 1.3% ore, and Indonesia premiums $15–17/wmt. However, declining high-grade NPI prices in China and high restocking costs add downward pressure. Indonesian policy rumors on restricting output may offer limited support. | Taykwa Tagamou Nation will invest $20 million via a convertible note in Canada Nickel, taking an 8.4% interest in the company. Proceeds support the Crawford Nickel Sulphide Project, with conversion expected in January 2025. The deal reinforces development momentum for a major Canadian nickel mine. | Mink Ventures announced the start of a diamond drill program at its Warren Ni-Cu-Co project in Ontario, targeting geophysical anomalies at the Shaft, North and SW zones. The zones show prior copper and nickel mineralization associated with IP/EM responses. The work is intended to maintain momentum toward earning full ownership; no production changes are reported. | Mink Ventures began diamond drilling at its Warren project in Timmins, Ontario, targeting geophysical anomalies associated with nickel, copper, and cobalt mineralization. The company highlights prior massive sulphide and disseminated sulphide assays at the North, Shaft, and SW zones, aiming to test untested IP/EM targets. | Taykwa Tagamou Nation (TTN) will invest $20 million via a convertible note into Canada Nickel’s Crawford nickel sulphide project, taking an 8.4% equity interest and a board nomination right. The financing is expected to close in January 2025, supporting continued development toward construction decisions. | Canada Nickel announced it has secured $20 million in financing from Ontario’s Tagamou Nation as part of a new partnership tied to the company’s advancing mine construction. By strengthening funding for development progress, the news can reduce perceived near-term project risk and be mildly supportive for nickel markets. | The piece argues Indonesia is ramping nickel output, expanding LME and SHFE exchange inventories substantially (with limited price support). Coupled with soft stainless demand, buyer reluctance to forward purchase, and ongoing downside bias in the NAS 304 surcharge, it points to bearish near-term nickel price pressure.

First Atlantic Nickel said it will acquire 100% of the RPM-South claims adjacent to its RPM nickel discovery in Newfoundland, enabling further exploration for depth and extensions. It resumes Phase 1 core processing and has submitted Phase 2 drilling permits. The release also promotes awaruite’s processing advantages for nickel supply security. | Taykwa Tagamou Nation will invest C$28.6m via a convertible note to advance Canada Nickel’s Crawford nickel sulphide project in Ontario. The partners plan to support development in the Timmins Nickel District as the project moves toward a construction decision, potentially improving near-term nickel supply expectations.

Westwin Elements announced first successful production of nickel powder at its Lawton, Oklahoma demonstration plant, designed to make about 20 tons per year via a carbonyl process. The company plans to produce Class 1 samples for customer qualifications to support a more domestic nickel supply chain, reducing foreign dependence. | NorthX Nickel announced an agreement with Magna Mining to sell its Sudbury portfolio of nickel and base metal assets. The transaction streamlines NorthX toward its Grasset nickel project, while Magna assumes related liabilities and operator/royalty obligations. Closure is expected by end of Q1 2025, subject to approvals. | Nickel 28 reported results for the quarter ended Oct. 31, 2024. Its Ramu nickel-cobalt JV produced and sold contained nickel, but a mechanical failure shut one acid plant, cutting production capacity roughly in half until end-February 2025, lowering 2025 nickel-in-MHP guidance to ~29,000 tonnes.

Indonesia is reportedly weighing deep cuts to nickel mining quotas, potentially reducing ore mined next year to about 150 million tons from 227 million this year. The move aims to support nickel prices amid smelter complaints of ore shortages and higher reliance on imports. | The report’s headline says Indonesia is considering deep cuts to nickel mining to support higher prices. By signaling reduced near-term nickel supply, the move would likely tighten market expectations and provide upward pressure on nickel prices over the coming days to weeks.  | A Trump victory is expected to loosen U.S. and state constraints on Minnesota mining. The piece highlights efforts to restart Twin Metals’ copper-nickel project and advance NewRange’s long-stalled PolyMet-era mine via renewed permitting. Environmental groups warn of renewed legal challenges, but policy winds could aid timelines. | Minnesota mining firms are expecting a Trump administration to reverse the Biden-era Superior National Forest mining moratorium and reduce permitting friction. Twin Metals hopes to restart copper-nickel mining, while NewRange Copper Nickel may reapply for required federal and state permits. Environmental opponents warn of renewed court battles.

The article links nickel’s 2024 price plunge to Indonesia’s outsized role in global nickel supply and its competitive, low-cost production, alongside alleged environmental and labor issues. It cites closures or imminent shutdowns at mines and smelters and Vale job cuts due to falling nickel benchmarks, indicating bearish near-term sentiment.

Indonesia is considering limiting nickel ore production to stabilize international prices amid oversupply and weaker demand. Officials cite oversupply as a key driver of falling nickel prices and note recent declines linked to demand weakness in stainless steel and battery markets. Timing is not specified, but supply restraint is the core policy signal.

Indonesia’s Energy and Mineral Resources Ministry said it has not yet discussed plans to curb next year’s nickel production quota, though companies were reportedly weighing deeper cuts. The statement points to ongoing uncertainty rather than immediate supply restraint. Nickel prices have been falling amid a stronger dollar, weak demand, and ample supply.

West Australia’s downturn in lithium and nickel has led to mine closures and reduced market value for local producers. At the same time, the labour layoffs appear to benefit gold miners, as staff availability and strong silver demand improve hiring and output prospects. The nickel impact is via shuttered production.

Tartisan Nickel Corp. completed a 149-line km airborne geophysical survey over its Turtle Pond nickel-related claims and closed a $500,000 financing at $0.18 per unit. Proceeds will fund exploration and development of its Kenbridge Nickel Project. No near-term production, inventory, or trade impacts are indicated.

Prices for high-grade nickel pig iron (NPI) fell (SMM and Indonesian FOB). Rising Indonesian supply and reduced stainless steel spot demand during maintenance weaken purchasing, while smelter losses from ore-driven cash costs lead to output adjustments. Pure nickel faces macro headwinds and underperforming demand, keeping nickel bearish. | SMM reports Indonesian laterite nickel ore CIF/FOB premiums stable-to-weak while policy signals remain awaited. High-grade NPI prices are continuing to decline with sluggish transactions, despite rising December output. Smelters have over two months of inventory and refined nickel production is ramping up, supporting expectations of nickel oversupply.

Nickel prices are slumping as EV demand expectations weaken and oversupply persists, with Chinese firms promoting nickel-free LFP batteries instead of nickel-based chemistries. At the same time, Harum Energy’s Indonesian projects expand nickel refining capacity, which could further weigh on near-term nickel balances.

Sumitomo Metal Mining will buy the remaining 15.625% stake in the Philippines’ Coral Bay Nickel HPAL smelter from Nickel Asia, raising its ownership to 100%. The plant has 24,000 t/y nickel capacity. The move strengthens SMM’s nickel processing footprint, likely supporting nickel supply expectations in the near term. | Canada Nickel announced agreements to create ExploreCo and secure surface rights for its Crawford nickel sulphide project near Timmins, Ontario. It consolidates mining and access rights to streamline development and permitting toward a 2025 construction decision. The transactions are subject to TSX Venture Exchange approvals. | Philippine mining officials expect stronger 2025 demand for copper and nickel as EVs and renewable-energy technologies scale. Copper output and production value rose sharply in the first nine months of 2024, while nickel output fell year on year, suggesting potential supply tightness. Net-zero policy supports demand outlook despite governance uncertainty. | Philippine mining officials expect EV and renewable-energy growth to raise 2025 demand for nickel and copper. They cite falling nickel ore output earlier but improved metallic production overall, with copper output up sharply in 2024’s first nine months. Net-zero policy support is framed as a continuing catalyst. | Nickel Asia Corp., the Philippines’ largest nickel producer, said it is negotiating with Sumitomo Metal Mining to sell its entire stake in Coral Bay Nickel Corp., which operates an HPAL plant. The company will reclassify the investment and stop recognizing equity gains or losses, but the deal’s impact on near-term supply is unclear.

Nickel Asia plans to sell its entire 15.63% stake in Coral Bay Nickel to Sumitomo, retaining a 10% interest in Taganito. Coral Bay is the Philippines’ sole HPAL processing plant. The move follows equity losses linked to higher costs and lower LME nickel prices, amid a nickel glut.

Indonesia is weighing cuts to nickel mine quotas for 2025, potentially reducing output from 272 mln to 150 mln tons. Macquarie said such cuts are unlikely, but if implemented they could remove up to 35% of global nickel supply, creating upside risk for prices amid prior Fitch downgrades. | The public can comment until Feb. 7 on Canada Nickel’s Crawford Nickel Project environmental impact statement, covering a long-running open-pit nickel-and-cobalt mine. The assessment flags habitat and water impacts and mitigation measures. It’s in phase 2 of a 5-phase process, with decisions still far off.

Indonesia’s mining ministry set a nickel ore mining quota of around 200 million metric tons for 2025. The quota may be reduced if miners fail performance checks, especially regarding reclamation and environmental management. A higher quota generally supports nickel supply expectations, weighing on prices near term. | Macquarie warns that proposed Indonesian nickel mine quota cuts could slash output from 272mt in 2024 to about 150mt, removing more than one-third of global supply. Even if a major cut is unlikely, lower-than-expected production is seen as an upside risk for nickel prices. | High-nickel pig iron prices in China and Indonesia weakened, with SMM NPI falling weekly and the Indonesian FOB index declining. Stainless steel maintenance reduced procurement, while Indonesia’s new capacity and supply variability increased. The NPI discount to electrolytic nickel widened, and macro/FX pressure plus possible oversupply sustain bearish nickel sentiment. | SMM reviews Indonesia’s nickel market: nickel ore domestic trade improved amid policy uncertainty, with supply stabilizing and ore premiums potentially rising. However, Indonesian high-grade NPI FOB fell $0.7/mtu WoW, with seasonal stainless demand weakness. Refined nickel production is ramping up, with more output expected in 2025.

Abaxx plans to launch a physically deliverable lithium carbonate contract within weeks after starting trading of its nickel sulphate futures. The exchange’s physically deliverable nickel contract begins in Singapore, with settlement linked to the LME nickel price and possible new delivery points. The story focuses on contract mechanics rather than nickel fundamentals. | MetalMiner reports bearish nickel market conditions, with Indonesia blamed for a supply glut. Nickel prices fell below $15k/mt to a multi-year low and exchange inventories rose sharply (LME and SHFE at highs). Indonesia is considering cutting mining quotas, but tighter supply may not be immediate, keeping prices under pressure. | Reuters notes the LME has fully recovered from its 2022 nickel crisis, with nickel inventories rising sharply and trading volumes rebounding. The exchange expanded approved nickel brands and broader stock/liquidity trends supported higher activity across base metals, while copper and aluminium are referenced largely as part of the overall inventory cycle.

Reuters says the LME has fully recovered from the 2022 nickel crisis, with 2024 trading volumes surging and LME nickel inventory rising to around 230,000 mt by end-November 2024 from under 40,000 in May 2023. Higher stocks and improved delivery options point to easing tightness. | Pan Global plans to accelerate 2025 exploration in Spain, including imminent drilling at the Escacena and CaÌrmenes projects. The program targets copper-tin-silver extensions at La Romana and copper-cobalt-nickel mineralization at CaÌrmenes. No immediate mine output or trade disruption is cited. | Reuters reports the LME has fully recovered from the 2022 nickel crisis, with nickel inventories rising to nearly 230,000 tonnes and new nickel brands approved, boosting confidence. It also cites higher exchange stocks across base metals (including aluminium) and fluctuating copper volumes tied to fund flows, implying mixed near-term price signals.

Hannan Metals reports a 1.2 km induced-polarization chargeability anomaly at its Sortilegio copper-gold porphyry prospect in Peru and states the target is drill-ready with permits for Q2 2025. This is positive for long-term copper optionality, but it does not affect current supply or inventories. | First Atlantic Nickel says Newfoundland’s Mineral Lands Division has granted permits for Phase 2 drilling and a southern access trail at its Atlantic Nickel Project. The program will expand the RPM discovery area and target extensions toward Chrome Pond and Pipestone. The firm is preparing for drilling and expects Phase 1 assay updates soon.

The article argues that Indonesia’s rising nickel output is driving higher deforestation, pollution, and mining-related disasters in Sulawesi. It warns that weak sustainability practices increase social and environmental risk, potentially leading to stricter oversight or cost burdens. The study suggests near-term sentiment risk for nickel. | Terrain Minerals has begun reverse-circulation drilling at its Lort River copper-nickel project, targeting Nova-Bollinger-style sulphide conductors based on EM data. The company expects RC drilling to finish in late January and assays in March, but the report is exploratory with no immediate supply effects for nickel or copper prices. | Port Kembla Copper has sold its former smelter site to developers, enabling rehabilitation of about 300 nearby homes after inspections found historical heavy-metal contamination. The smelter operated from 2000 to closure in 2003, and remediation work will begin post-sale. The news is localized and does not change current metal supply. | The piece argues that nickel mining in Indonesia’s Sulawesi is linked to nearly double deforestation rates near mines (2011–2018), plus higher pollution and mining-related disasters like landslides and flash floods. It frames these harms as potential drivers of stricter oversight and disruption risk for nickel operations, impacting supply expectations. | Zambia’s mines minister says Saudi Manara Minerals is exploring investments in the country’s critical-minerals projects, including copper and nickel assets, with discussions already held and a potential deal likely this year. The government also seeks up to 30% stakes in new projects and is expanding copper trading arrangements, boosting revenue expectations. | Petrosea won a $1bn contract from Vale Indonesia to mine nickel ore in Central Sulawesi, with a scheduled March signing and 10-year term. The deal sits within Indonesia’s ongoing restructuring of nickel investment, including HPAL capacity plans and potential battery-grade partnerships.

Ardea Resources is advancing the $98.5 million definitive feasibility study for its Goongarrie Hub nickel-laterite project in Western Australia, with multiple rigs and contracts for metallurgical and water studies. The DFS targets economics for long-term production, but it remains pre-FID with updates expected later in 2025. | Korea Zinc said its nickel sulfate manufacturing technology for cathode metal compounds was designated a South Korean national strategic technology. The move provides government tax and administrative support and supports investment in a large all-in-one nickel refinery planned to start in 2026, reducing costs and improving nickel supply readiness. | Global nickel production is expected to fall 7.4% in 2024 to about 3.4 million tonnes as major producers cut output amid declining prices and operational disruptions. Indonesia, the Philippines, and New Caledonia face notable declines, with regulatory approval delays contributing to reduced mine output. | SMM’s weekly Indonesia-1.17 nickel market review says Indonesian domestic laterite nickel ore premiums generally fluctuated higher, with ample supply and smelter inventories above two weeks. Demand improved on quota/stockpiling effects. NPI prices rebounded, losses eased, and nickel ore CIF prices are expected stable with an upward trend into the next weeks. | A US- and EU-backed Lobito Corridor rail project linking Angola’s Lobito port with the DRC and Zambia aims to speed shipments and expand freight capacity. It notes current copper concentrate flows from Ivanhoe Mines and expects much higher train throughput, supporting copper logistics. Nickel-linked investments are mentioned but are less immediate. | South Korea designated Korea Zinc’s nickel sulphate manufacturing technology as a national strategic technology, expanding eligible support and tax deductions. The company plans to start full-scale operations at a >500bn won all-in-one nickel refinery in 2026, aiming for up to 90bn won cost savings. The move strengthens domestic battery nickel supply competitiveness. | The opinion argues that mining consolidation may resume, with major buyers focused on copper assets such as Rio Tinto/BHP targets and Anglo’s Collahuasi. It emphasizes a global copper shortage from electrification, implying tighter supply. It also notes BHP’s plan to mothball nickel operations, potentially supporting nickel sentiment.

Anglo American plans to sell its Brazil nickel assets, Barro Alto and Niquelandia, by H1 2025 as part of a restructuring after BHP’s takeover bid. Analysts expect obtaining a good price will be hard given a weak nickel price, implying continued bearish sentiment for nickel.

BHP said copper production rose 17% year-on-year to 510,700 tonnes in the second quarter, with Chile’s Escondida delivering a 22% increase to a ten-year record. However, guidance for copper South Australia was lowered after a weather-related power outage. BHP also noted a USD300m impact from WA nickel following temporary suspension. | Noronex reported multiple strong copper-silver drill intercepts at its Fiesta project in Namibia, including 16m at 1.3% copper equivalent and other wider, higher-grade hits. The company also highlights silver’s potential to improve concentrate payability. Overall, the news is positive for copper sentiment but unlikely to affect near-term market prices materially. | Infinico Metals announced the start of a fixed loop fluxgate electromagnetic survey at its Nicobi Ni-Cu-Co property in Québec to refine conductive targets for future drilling. The company cites prior drilling results showing nickel-copper sulphide mineralization, but no production or operational changes are reported. | Infinico Metals began a fixed-loop fluxgate surface electromagnetic survey at its Nicobi property to refine conductive targets for potential Ni–Cu sulphide drilling. Prior drilling at Showing A intersected nickel and copper mineralization, but follow-up closed it off. The release focuses on exploration targeting rather than production or supply changes. | Alaska Energy Metals reported 2024 exploration results at its Angliers-Belleterre project in Quebec, outlining multiple undrilled VMS and Cu-Ni targets with anomalous copper and nickel in soil, rock, and VTEM results. The company plans further geophysical modeling and diamond drilling to test mineralization at depth. | Fathom Nickel reported 2024 exploration results expanding the historic Gochager Lake nickel sulphide footprint and validating magmatic mineralization at Albert Lake, plus plans for further EM surveys and drilling in 2025. The company expects improving nickel prices and notes Indonesia production curbs, but no near-term supply disruption occurs. | Indonesia’s mineral authorities are considering cutting domestic nickel production so supply better matches domestic and export needs and avoids further sustained price declines. As the world’s leading nickel miner, Indonesia’s potential quota reductions could tighten supply expectations and support international nickel prices, amid recent sharp losses.

Reuters column says Indonesia’s nickel sector has flooded markets and drove nickel to four-year lows, but Jakarta plans to cut 2025 ore mining quotas to 200 Mt from 240 Mt. The quota outlook has coincided with a modest LME 3-month nickel rebound (+3% since early January), though inventories remain elevated. | Trump’s administration launched an “America First Trade Policy” investigation under Section 232 focused on imported steel and aluminum. The review assesses exclusions/exemptions and could lead to adjusted tariffs or import restrictions after scheduled reports. The announcement is conditional and timing is uncertain, limiting immediate directional impact.

Reuters columnist says Indonesia is pivotal for nickel’s recovery after 2024’s price bust. The Indonesian mining ministry plans to cut nickel ore quotas to 200m tons from a planned 240m, helping lift LME 3-month nickel by ~3% since early January, though rising inventories and weaker EV battery demand limit follow-through. | The Trump administration has launched an investigation under Section 232 into whether to adjust tariff restrictions on imported steel and aluminum, reviewing exclusions and potential new import limitations tied to national security. Prior 232 tariffs included 10% aluminum in many cases, with possible further changes.

Canada Nickel announced positive exploration drill results from its Midlothian and Deloro properties, with multiple holes intersecting shallow, mineralized ultramafic rocks and reporting nickel grades (e.g., 0.27% over 371m including 0.30% over 39m at Deloro). It also describes ongoing infill work at Nesbitt. This supports bullish nickel sentiment for the near term. | SMM reports Indonesia’s 2025 RKAB quota for nickel ore at 298 million wmt and active ore trading, with smelters starting February procurement. High-grade NPI FOB/inland prices recovered on tighter circulation ahead of Chinese New Year, but quota approval and rising LME nickel inventories restrained refined nickel gains, implying mixed near-term direction. | High-nickel pig iron prices increased this week, with the Indonesian NPI FOB index also rising. Supply was slightly tightened by weaker domestic production due to smelter losses, while tight circulating resources and long-term contract coverage supported demand. The NPI-to-electrolytic nickel discount narrowed, implying buoyant nickel sentiment. | COBCO commissioned its first batch of NCM precursor cathode active materials lines in Jorf Lasfar, Morocco, targeting 120kT/year PCAM capacity. The NCM (nickel-containing) production ramp-up and planned closed-loop recycling expansion point to higher near-term demand for nickel materials used in battery cathodes. | An environmental group says Canada Nickel’s proposed Crawford Project could harm freshwater by releasing effluent into the North Driftwood River, a relatively small stream. The group is reviewing the large impact statement and has concerns about long-term effects over a 41-year mine life, prompting ongoing scrutiny.

Core Nickel Corp. published its 2024 year-end review and outlines 2025 exploration plans for its Thompson Nickel Belt projects, including planned VTEM, re-sampling, and a 4,000–5,000 m drill program at the Mel and Halfway Lake areas. The update targets resource expansion and does not affect near-term nickel supply directly.

Terra Metals says it will re-commission its copper and cobalt acid leach plant by end-Q1 2025 and commission a 1 million tpa flotation plant by end-Q2 2025 in Zambia. It also plans 400 MW solar capacity to power operations. The expansion improves copper production outlook and supports bullish near-term pricing.

Core Nickel Corp. started helicopter-borne VTEM electromagnetic surveys on its Mel and Odei River nickel projects in Manitoba and plans further drilling after survey results. The news updates exploration targeting deeper nickel sulphide potential, with no changes to production, exports, or supply affecting near-term nickel markets. | Tanzania and Burundi signed an agreement with two Chinese firms to build a $2.15 billion railway connecting the two countries and carrying metals, including battery-mineral nickel, to Dar es Salaam port. The 282 km line is expected to move about three million tonnes of minerals annually, improving nickel transport capacity.

A report and experts warn that expanding nickel mining in Raja Ampat, Indonesia, is damaging a UNESCO-protected marine region. Satellite data show mining pit area grew sharply since 2020. Despite a March 2024 court ruling requiring extra protection for small islands, permits continue, raising risks to nickel production timelines. | Glencore reported 2024 output declines across several commodities, including copper. Copper production fell to 951,600 tonnes, down 6% from 2023 and at the low end of its guidance. Management expects to provide 2025 guidance in February, with improved H2 performance supporting some offset. | Burundi and Tanzania agreed with Chinese firms to build a $2.15bn 282 km railway to Dar es Salaam to move minerals, including battery nickel. Financed with African Development Bank support, the line is expected to haul three million tonnes annually. Improved export logistics could lift near-term expectations for nickel supply. | A report says nickel mining expansion in Indonesia’s Raja Ampat region has rapidly increased land use and raised fears of deforestation and sediment pollution harming coral ecosystems. Although mining is still operating, growing scrutiny and backlash around nickel projects could threaten near-term supply continuity, supporting nickel prices. | NorthX Nickel Corp. announced a non-brokered subscription receipt financing to fund general working capital and to support a proposed acquisition of its Sudbury Basin nickel and base-metal assets. The escrow release depends on transaction conditions. The update is corporate/financing-focused with no direct production or pricing details, implying limited near-term nickel price effect.

The Nickel Institute comments on the EU “Competitiveness Compass,” arguing that EU regulatory inconsistencies—particularly revisions to nickel environmental quality standards proposed under the Water Framework Directive—could harm producers by constraining investment and competitiveness. It calls for streamlined permitting, reduced regulatory burden, and affordable energy to sustain EU nickel supply for batteries and the energy transition.

Reuters column citing a January base-metals analyst poll expects aluminium to lead in 2025 on a projected supply deficit, with cash prices set to rise. Copper upside is tempered by tariff-driven macro risk, especially for China demand. Nickel remains bearish on ongoing oversupply and rising stocks, limiting near-term net impact.

The article argues that rapid EV and renewable-storage growth is tightening global battery-metal supply chains. It highlights strong demand for nickel sulfate for NCM/NCA cathodes and notes irregular supply from producers such as Indonesia and the Philippines, alongside environmental and production-quote constraints. It implies continued nickel price volatility and upside bias. | The piece frames the energy transition as long-run support for copper, aluminum, and nickel and cites SMM’s January 20, 2025 price levels. It notes copper premiums weakening ahead of Chinese New Year due to softer consumption, while nickel premiums fall on abundant supply and reduced activity, implying mixed short-term pressure. | A report by C4ADS says Chinese firms control about 75% of Indonesia’s nickel refining capacity, dominated by Tsingshan and Delong. It raises concerns over supply-chain control, environmental risk, and possible disadvantages for automakers amid tighter U.S./EU trade policies. Indonesia’s response is not detailed, implying no immediate supply shock. | A New York judge allowed Trafigura to subpoena records from major banks in a nickel-related fraud case to trace where funds paid for purported nickel cargoes were transferred. The underlying claim is that worthless material was shipped instead, but the story contains no direct supply or inventory impact.

A report says Chinese firms control about 75% of Indonesia’s nickel refining capacity, with Tsingshan and Jiangsu Delong owning over 70%. Concerns focus on supply-chain resilience and possible disruptions from restrictive trade policies. Reuters also notes Chinese moves to cut stakes via partnerships and Indonesia’s push for more domestic control. | Podium Minerals completed the acquisition of EV Nickel’s Range Well nickel project, integrating it with its Parks Reef PGM project in Western Australia. The combined assets also reportedly include copper alongside nickel and cobalt. The deal involves issuing shares and a royalty to Johnson Matthey, but no near-term production impact is specified. | Fastmarkets’ technical note argues LME nickel’s bearish setup may be temporary. It describes a potential inverse head-and-shoulders pattern with a neckline near $16,000/t and suggests that only a monthly close below $14,500/t would invalidate the bullish bias. Otherwise, it expects a limited rebound/short-covering rally in coming weeks.

High-nickel pig iron (NPI) prices rose this week, with SMM 8–12% NPI averaging 944.2 RMB per nickel point and the NPI FOB index also increasing. Resumed smelter operations, limited Indonesian/Philippine output, and a Philippines raw-ore export ban strengthened sentiment, narrowing the discount to electrolytic nickel. | SMM reports Indonesian domestic trade laterite nickel ore activity rebounded post-holiday, with premiums rising (1.6 grade $17–18) amid ample supply and February restocking by pyrometallurgical plants. High-grade NPI prices strengthened, with the FOB index up WoW and improved smelter economics, supported by positive sentiment and export-ban news.

Trump announced a 25% tariff on all steel and aluminium imports to the US, likely including Australia, prompting diplomatic efforts for an exemption. The piece notes aluminium export volumes and likely competitive pressure on manufacturers. The tariff threat is a near-term negative for aluminium price expectations; copper and nickel aren’t directly addressed. | Asian markets were mixed, but LME base metals mostly rose after President Trump pledged 25% tariffs on all U.S. steel and aluminum imports. Aluminum futures gained 0.6% and copper 1.2% on the day, while nickel fell 0.5%. Analysts expected limited broader impact on emerging Asia exports. | Trump’s proposed 25% tariffs on all U.S. imports of steel and aluminium are rattling Asian producers by threatening higher U.S. import prices, disrupted trade flows, and lower volumes. Reports cite aluminium supply-tightening concerns and falling iron-ore sentiment, while copper and nickel are not directly addressed. | Trump says the US will impose a 25% tariff on steel and aluminium imports, with details expected soon. Australia’s industry urges a carve-out, citing past exemptions. Even with exemptions, the risk is weaker demand if tariffs slow China, pressuring overall industrial metal sentiment, especially aluminium. | Trump said the US will impose 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminium imports starting this week, with further “reciprocal” measures. The tariff backdrop includes potential retaliation from China and warnings that tariffs will raise US costs, likely pressuring near-term industrial demand and sentiment for copper, nickel, and aluminium. | Trump signed executive orders imposing a flat 25% tariff on all steel and aluminum imports into the US “without exceptions.” The piece also discusses possible US consideration of tariff exemptions for Australia and European warnings of retaliatory tariffs. Overall, aluminum faces direct near-term price support from higher import costs. | Donald Trump says the US will impose 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminium imports, including from Canada and Mexico, and is planning reciprocal tariffs almost immediately. The move escalates trade policy and could trigger retaliation, likely pressuring industrial metals demand expectations and prices near term. | Trump said the U.S. will impose 25% tariffs on all steel and “aluminum, too” imports, including from Canada and Mexico, with additional reciprocal duties later. Markets fell and partners warned of industrial impacts. Aluminum-focused expectations likely deteriorate near term; copper and nickel only face indirect macro/trade pressure. | Trump has signed executive orders imposing a flat 25% tariff on all US steel and aluminum imports “without exceptions or exemptions.” The news covers potential tariff exemptions for Australia, expected limited impact comments from ThyssenKrupp, and EU retaliation threats—driving near-term expectations for aluminum trade costs and prices, with only indirect effects for copper and nickel. | Trump announced executive actions raising US tariffs on aluminium and steel to 25% from 10% as China’s levies against the US take effect. Canada is the dominant source of primary aluminium imports to the US, while Mexico supplies much of the aluminium scrap and alloy. Higher tariffs likely pressure aluminium markets near term. | Trump plans to announce 25% tariffs on all imported steel and aluminum entering the U.S., including shipments from Canada and Mexico. Canada supplies major steel and is the top aluminum metal source. The move follows prior tariff threats and could tighten U.S. aluminum availability, but details remain limited. | Trump said 25% tariffs will be imposed on all steel and aluminium imports starting Monday, including shipments from Canada and Mexico. He referenced earlier 2018 tariffs and national security claims, as well as the 2019 deal that previously prevented supply surges. The new tariffs may raise costs and prices for aluminium. | Trump removed exemptions from U.S. steel tariffs so all steel imports face at least 25%, and raised 2018 aluminum tariffs to 25% from 10%. The policy change is meant to protect domestic industry and reset trade, likely increasing aluminum import costs and near-term price support. Other updates are unrelated to metal prices. | Norilsk Nickel said its 2024 IFRS net profit fell 37% to $1.8 bln as EBITDA declined and revenues dropped 13% amid weaker market prices for nickel and platinum group metals. The company reported lower free cash flow and higher net debt, while continuing investment and debt management plans. | Stocks in steel and aluminum producers jumped after President Trump said he plans 25% tariffs on imported steel and aluminum and will announce retaliatory duties on countries that impose tariffs on U.S. goods. Canada is a major exporter, contributing about 40% of U.S. aluminum imports. | Nornickel reported 2024 net profit down 37% to $1.8 billion, citing Western sanctions pressures and low prices for its key metals. The company noted payments/equipment access constraints, redirected sales to Asia, and faced repairs disruption at its Nadezhda smelter. It also warned it sees a nickel surplus in 2025. | Trump plans 25% tariffs on all US steel and aluminum imports, targeting China-linked trade flows and expanding an already tariff-heavy stance. The piece notes China’s overcapacity and rising exports depressing prices globally, which has triggered retaliation and further trade friction likely to pressure industrial metals pricing in the near term. | Trump signed executive orders imposing 25% tariffs on all US imports of steel and aluminum with no exceptions or exemptions. The US may also consider exemptions for Australian products. Europe and France signaled retaliatory measures. The tariff news is likely to raise near-term expectations for aluminum supply constraints in the US market. | President Trump orders 25% import duties on all steel and raises aluminium tariffs from 10% to 25%, covering about $50B of imports. Tariffs are expected to be passed through to U.S. buyers, pushing up prices for products using these metals and likely lifting nearby aluminium pricing pressure through higher import costs and substitution effects. | Trump signed executive orders imposing 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the U.S. from all countries, effective March 12, with Canada threatening “clear and calibrated” retaliation. The broad tariff scope includes downstream products and is expected to disrupt aluminum trade flows.

Trump says he will halt producing pennies. The article notes pennies have used mostly zinc since 1982, with only about 198 metric tons of copper embedded in 2024 pennies—around 0.02% of annual U.S. copper production. Any change should not meaningfully move copper prices. Nickel effects are speculative; aluminium is not mentioned. | Aluminium prices fell as investors worried about a possible global trade war after Trump imposed 25% tariffs on U.S. steel and aluminium imports. LME three-month aluminium dropped 0.6% and SHFE aluminium fell 0.5%. Copper moved modestly on tariff expectations, while nickel declined amid broader market caution. | Trump’s administration is imposing new tariffs on steel imports (25% minimum) and also includes a 25% tariff on aluminum from March 12. Industry leaders warn these duties could tighten short-term supply and raise input costs for automakers, likely supporting near-term aluminum prices, while copper and nickel impacts are not specified. | The AP reports new Trump tariffs: all steel imports face at least 25%, alongside a 25% aluminum tariff. Auto executives warn these duties will raise costs through supply-demand tightening and higher component prices. Producers may seek more capacity and face short-term supply constraints, supporting higher aluminum prices. | CNGR said it will end investment in a nickel refinery joint venture with Posco Holdings, leading to liquidation of Posco CNGR Nickel Solution. The move follows weak global EV demand and slower battery installation growth, reducing the outlook for the planned nickel sulphate and battery-precursor facility in Pohang. | The Philippines’ Senate has passed a bill to ban nickel ore exports to push downstream processing. The measure is under bicameral review and would only start five years after signing. As the second-largest laterite nickel ore exporter, any progress fuels expectations of tighter ore supply and potential support for nickel prices. | Denmark’s EIFO says it sold its exposure to Horizonte Minerals’ Brazilian nickel project after the Araguaia development failed. Management linked the collapse to plunging nickel prices in early 2024 and sharp cost overruns. EIFO removed the project from its books, emphasizing tighter monitoring for future nickel deals. | The IEA and C4ADS warn nickel supply chains are vulnerable because Indonesia’s refining capacity is heavily influenced by Chinese companies and concentrated ownership. If Indonesian refined nickel supply is disrupted, alternative sources may fall far below demand. The news raises near-term tightness concerns for nickel prices amid escalating geopolitics. | Trudeau warned that a U.S.-Canada trade war tied to Trump’s steel and aluminum tariffs could cost jobs, with duties potentially stacking to 50% for steel and additional levies including on energy and critical minerals. The Section 232 tariff escalation directly raises aluminium trade frictions and demand uncertainty in the near term. | First Quantum’s CFO said the company will not rush to sell a minority stake in its Zambian copper mines and sees no set timeline. It is seeking a long-term partner, with Manara reported in advanced talks. The move is linked to funding Kansanshi expansion, after Cobre Panama’s shutdown. | Trump announced 25% US tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports, with Canadian products facing additional stacked levies. Canada’s steelmakers and aluminum producers in places like Hamilton (Ontario) and Quebec are bracing for a major slowdown and potential retaliatory measures, raising short-term downside risk for aluminum prices. | Trudeau said Canada will respond “unequivocally” to Trump tariffs, even with stacked levies. The U.S. announced a 25% tariff on all steel and aluminum imports starting March 12, bringing steel/aluminum to 50% total. Canada plans countermeasures and cautions against trade escalation.

President Trump signed proclamations expanding Section 232 tariffs on steel articles (25%) and raising aluminum import tariffs from 10% to 25%, revoking alternative arrangements and terminating product-specific exclusions. Tariffs apply to imports from all countries effective March 12, 2025, after interim impacts, aiming to protect U.S. steel and aluminum industries. | Power Nickel priced a C$40m best-efforts private placement of Quebec flow-through shares to fund Canadian exploration. Proceeds will expand work at the Nisk nickel-copper sulphide project in Quebec, including Nisk and Lion zones and targets, supporting sentiment for future nickel and copper supply. | Trump’s proclamation expands 25% tariffs on steel and increases aluminium tariffs from 10% to 25%, applying to all countries from March 12, 2025. It also revokes prior exemptions for steel and aluminium from several allies and for various derivative products. Importers must reassess supply-chain cost impacts.

Yukon officials say the court-appointed receiver at Eagle Gold Mine has started discharging on-site melt-prep water with elevated copper that is non-compliant with water regulations. Testing found contamination reaching Haggart Creek and exceedances for cyanide, plus detections of nickel and other contaminants. The issue is being managed via settling pond construction. | St. Albert businesses are bracing for an international trade war after the U.S. imposed a 25% tariff on imported steel and aluminum. A local steel fabricator reports supplier price increases and bidding uncertainty. A brewery expects higher aluminum can and possibly other input costs, with potential Canadian retaliation risks. | President Trump issued a proclamation imposing 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the U.S. effective March 12, terminating prior exclusions and expanding to derivative products. Foreign trade zone and duty drawback benefits are limited. The breadth increases near-term trade and demand risk that can pressure industrial metals sentiment. | SMM reports Indonesian nickel ore premiums for domestic trade and the Indonesian NPI FOB index have risen, supported by restocking demand, tight available ore due to rainy-season disruptions, and reduced smelter operating load. Even with weak stainless demand, trader procurement and narrowing smelter losses keep high-grade NPI prices firm. | Trump expanded tariffs on steel and aluminum imports (25% on both, with aluminum’s rate raised), expected to raise input costs for Wisconsin manufacturers. Equipment makers estimate higher steel costs, while beverage producers note aluminum sourcing adjustments. The tariff shock implies near-term upward pressure on aluminum prices; copper and nickel are not mentioned.

Trump issued proclamations expanding Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum imports into the US. For aluminum, the duty rate rises from 10% to 25%, exemptions and product exclusions are ended, and more “derivative” aluminum products are added within a defined mechanism effective March 12. This should support higher aluminum prices short term. | The article outlines February 10, 2025 US proclamations ending steel/aluminum tariff exemptions and exclusions and imposing across-the-board duties, including raising aluminum tariffs from 10% to 25% for most sources and extending 25% duties to additional “derivative” aluminum products from March 12, 2025. | Ontario Rivers Alliance raised concerns with Canada Nickel’s Crawford Project review, citing a lack of detail on cumulative impacts, tailings “in-process” carbon capture claims, and water/effluent management. The objections could increase regulatory uncertainty and potential project delays or added costs, which may pressure near-term nickel sentiment.

Anglo American said it will sell its nickel business to MMG for up to $500 million, including $350 million at completion and potential earnouts/cash tied to project development. The divested assets produce about 38,000 tonnes of nickel annually. The restructuring targets a focus on copper and iron ore. | Anglo American agreed to sell its nickel business to MMG, covering two ferronickel operations in Brazil and related growth projects, for up to $500 million (including an initial $350 million on completion and contingent payments). Management framed it as portfolio simplification, implying limited near-term effect on nickel output. | Anglo American agreed to sell its nickel business to MMG for $350 million on completion, plus a $100 million price-linked earnout and $50 million contingent payment tied to final investment decisions. The assets include ferronickel operations in Brazil and greenfield projects, as part of a portfolio simplification. | Anglo American said it will sell its nickel business to China’s MMG for up to US$500 million. The transaction could shift long-term nickel supply ownership and production strategy, but it is not an immediate disruption or demand shock, so near-term nickel price impact is likely limited. | Anglo American said it will sell its nickel business to MMG for up to $500m as part of restructuring away from nickel and toward copper and iron ore. The divested assets produce about 38,000 t/year of nickel. The company expects cash at completion plus earnouts tied to development. | Anglo American agreed to sell its nickel division to MMG Singapore Resources for up to $500m, including $350m upfront and price-linked payments. The deal follows nickel selling near a four-year low, blamed on Indonesia/China supply oversupply, a stronger US dollar, and softer electric-vehicle demand. | Indonesia Battery Corporation (IBC) plans to build a nickel-based EV battery recycling plant by 2031, targeting recycling over 99% of components. Nickel volumes later recovered, limiting immediate market disruption. | Britain’s financial regulator fined the LME about £9.2 million for its handling of the 2022 nickel crisis, saying the exchange failed to ensure its systems were adequately equipped. In March 2022, the LME halted nickel trading and canceled trades due to extreme pricing volatility. | The FCA fined the London Metal Exchange £9.2m for inadequate systems and controls during the March 2022 nickel market disruption, when Russia’s invasion of Ukraine triggered a rapid nickel price surge. The regulator found junior staffing and disabled price bands worsened the speed of the move, leading to investor risk exposure. | The UK FCA imposed a £9.2m fine on the London Metal Exchange over disorderly “nickel market chaos.” The regulatory action centers on how nickel trading was handled, raising near-term concerns about market functioning and price discovery that can weigh on nickel sentiment and liquidity.

SMM’s Indonesian market review says nickel ore supply remains tight as rainy season disruptions in Sulawesi/Philippines delay recovery, and NPI smelter inventories are low. Policy signals about earlier PNBP implementation may increase mine sales costs but be passed to downstream buyers, supporting prices. High-grade NPI averages edged up and refined nickel ramp-up continues. | High-grade nickel pig iron prices rose this week (SMM 8–12% up to 1016.5 yuan per nickel point; Indonesian NPI FOB up). Tight nickel ore/transport constraints, low smelter inventories, and improving smelter economics support higher pricing, even with weaker stainless steel demand. Overall nickel price downside is seen as limited. | The piece revisits the March 2022 LME nickel crisis, arguing regulators and the exchange lacked visibility into large OTC positions, especially linked to Tsingshan. It notes OTC and exchange interaction drove price volatility, alongside trade cancellations and legal actions. It concludes no current evidence of wrongdoing or new immediate market change.

Reuters reports hedge fund Elliott is looking at additional action against the LME after trades in nickel were cancelled. While details are limited in the excerpt, the dispute centers on nickel market operations at the exchange, which can raise near-term uncertainty and pressure nickel pricing. | Elliott says it may take additional steps regarding LME-voided nickel trades. The claim points to ongoing legal/market-recovery uncertainty after regulators voided transactions, which can weigh on nickel sentiment and liquidity in the immediate term as traders reassess risks and exposures.

Elliott Associates is considering further action against the LME after the FCA fined it about £9.2m for failing to handle severe nickel market stress during the March 2022 spike that saw trades cancelled. The FCA cited inadequate preparedness and staff training, while courts previously found LME acted lawfully. | The article (a Reuters member piece) states that the LME was fined for failing to act quickly during a nickel crisis. While this is clearly connected to nickel market disruption and oversight, the excerpt provides no specifics on follow-on measures or supply/demand changes that would drive a near-term directional price impact. | Indonesia’s EV push is threatened by regulatory complexity and ESG/safety concerns in nickel mining and processing. The piece cites investor withdrawals (e.g., BASF, Eramet) and safety incidents at nickel smelters, potentially reducing appetite for Indonesian nickel processing capacity. It also discusses looming tariff uncertainty affecting EV investments and downstream demand. | Brazilian Nickel’s CEO says the company will use heap leaching on its Piauí nickel laterite project, aiming for lower capex/opex than HPAL and stronger participation outside the China–Indonesia supply chain. The interview also highlights potential nickel deficits later this decade, but current news is mainly financing/offtake progress. | President Trump’s March 20 executive order expands U.S. support for domestic critical mineral mining using Defense Production Act financing, expedited permitting, and buyer/offtake facilitation. The order adds copper to the critical mineral list and AEMC links it to its nickel-dominant Nikolai project, improving near-term investment expectations for nickel and copper.

A sludge collapse at a Chinese-funded nickel plant in Indonesia has reportedly killed one person and left two missing. The accident is likely to disrupt operations at the nickel facility, increasing short-term supply uncertainty and potentially pressuring nickel prices lower over the coming days to weeks. | The article title reports a sludge collapse that killed one worker at a Chinese-funded nickel plant in Indonesia. Such an incident can halt or delay operations and maintenance, reducing near-term nickel output. That supply disruption risk is bearish for nickel prices over the coming days and weeks.

First Atlantic Nickel announced assays from drill hole AN-24-03 at its RPM Zone, reporting an average 0.25% nickel over 216 meters (including 0.27% nickel in the final 21 meters) with visible awaruite mineralization. The company plans higher-powered Phase 2 drilling to test deeper/westward extensions, implying favorable nickel potential. | Pan Global Resources reported underground channel samples at the Profunda target in Spain’s Cármenes project, including 5.2% copper over 5m and other high-grade Cu intervals. Nickel is also present in several samples (up to 0.53% Ni over 1m), supporting bullish sentiment for copper and some nickel. | Class 1 Nickel reported an updated NI 43-101 mineral resource estimate for the Dundonald North nickel sulphide deposit, citing higher tonnage and contained nickel, plus increases in contained copper and cobalt. The update is based on historical drilling and remains inferred/out-of-pit, with no near-term production timeline.

SMM launched new weekly price points for Indonesia local laterite nickel ore (1.3%, 1.4%, 1.5%), with values published every Friday. This week, multiple nickel ore grades increased WoW, including 1.4% and 1.5% ex-factory and FOB assessments, suggesting firmer nickel ore pricing. | SMM reports continued tight Indonesian nickel ore supply, with pyrometallurgical ore prices and April premiums expected to rise, aided by low smelter inventories and strong PNBP policy expectations that lift miners’ costs. Rainy-season disruptions and flooding constrain supply. NPI remains supported by higher costs, while refined nickel projects ramp up gradually. | China’s GEM becomes the majority owner of Bahodopi Nickel Smelting Indonesia (BNSI) via a Rp 1 trillion share subscription, diluting PT Vale Indonesia to 49%. BNSI develops a nickel processing facility in Morowali. The report says operations and business continuity won’t be affected. | High-nickel pig iron prices (SMM8-12%) rose WoW, supported by cost pressures from higher nickel ore and improved cost support amid tight feedstock availability. Indonesian flooding in Morowali is expected to increase pure nickel costs. The piece expects continued strong NPI and upward nickel price correction. | Fastmarkets reports alumina prices fell in 2025 amid excess supply and weaker demand, while bauxite remains influenced by Guinea supply security. It also notes a US move to impose 25% tariffs on imported aluminium, lifting domestic sentiment and aluminium premiums. Market participants watch further tariff signals and trade-flow impacts.

South Pacific Metals reports successful completion of a Papua New Guinea warden hearing renewing exploration licence EL 2310 for its Kili Teke Cu-Au project and plans to begin on-ground exploration. The update is copper-linked via project development, but no production or supply change is announced, limiting near-term price impact.

Bellezza claims a patented “fusion” process could enable graphene interconnects in CMOS chips at low temperatures, potentially replacing copper circuits as copper interconnects reach size limits. The method uses iron/nickel plating substrates treated to form martensite crystals. The article provides technology claims but no market-scale deployment. | 1844 Resources announced an extension of the outside date on its option agreement with Nickel North for the Hawk Ridge nickel/copper project, citing delays in preparing an updated geological report. The unit private placement details are also provided, subject to exchange approvals. No production or supply changes are described.

High-nickel pig iron prices edged up to 1030.25 yuan per nickel point, supported by low smelter inventories and cost support. However, weak downstream stainless demand and rising raw material costs reduce procurement willingness. Indonesia policy delays and a stronger USD pressure pure nickel sentiment. Next week discounts may widen, implying bearish risk for nickel. | MINE’s April 2025 issue reviews Indonesia’s rapid nickel growth and highlights upcoming challenges, including decreasing production quotas and plans to raise royalties. These policy shifts could reduce near-term nickel supply availability and influence market expectations, potentially providing upward support for nickel prices. No similar copper or aluminium signals are discussed.

A Singapore venture capitalist testified that he invested millions into an alleged nickel scheme run by Ng Yu Zhi after checking nickel prices and being assured returns. Prosecutors say there was no real physical nickel trading; investor funds paid earlier investors. The fraud unraveled in early 2021, damaging trust and nickel-related trading confidence. | Indonesia dominates global nickel supply, with rising production over 2018–2024 and major Chinese-linked investment. Despite a recent nickel price decline linked to faster supply growth and slower EV demand, the government is considering production quota cuts (and royalty increases), which could reduce supply and lift prices. Mentions recent support from tightening expectations.

7NEWS Spotlight reports that Indonesia’s nickel mines are unsafe and heavily polluting, funded by Chinese capital, and accuses them of harming Australia’s nickel industry. The debate follows over EV-driven nickel demand and Australia’s ‘Future Made in Australia’ nickel tax credits, with calls to reconsider Indonesia arrangements and improve standards. | SMM reports US tariff policy has driven nickel prices down, and delayed Indonesia’s PNBP-related royalty announcements. With weaker downstream nickel product prices, support for Indonesia’s April laterite nickel ore premiums is weakening. SMM expects mainstream April premiums of $24-25/wmt may fall in late April as renegotiations begin after Lebaran.

Avebury Nickel Mine’s boss says cheap Indonesian “dirty nickel” crushed nickel prices and shuttered Australian production. He urges policy changes and LME certification to separate “clean” and “uncertified” nickel for EV batteries and other buyers, which could revive higher-quality nickel demand and lift prices.

Rio Tinto says Trump’s administration could help unblock its Resolution Copper project in Arizona, citing plans to speed permitting and boost critical-mineral output. The article highlights potential upside for U.S. copper supply and notes another U.S. project (Twin Metals) that includes nickel, though details are secondary. | The EU member states approved retaliatory tariffs worth $23 billion in response to Trump’s 25% tariffs on imported US steel and aluminium. Retaliation will enter in stages starting April 15, May 15, and December 1. The EU prefers a negotiated “zero-for-zero” deal, but talks remain uncertain. | Nickel prices have fallen sharply to levels last seen since 2020, driven by reciprocal tariff announcements that spooked growth expectations and triggered a broad sell-off in base metals. The article warns that renewed recession fears could add volatility. It also notes lower nickel and copper would reduce the 304 stainless surcharge. | Canada’s Conference Board warns Sudbury’s economy is under pressure as nickel prices remain weak. It cites Indonesia-led oversupply, demand uncertainty around electric cars, and nickel-displacing lithium-iron-phosphate batteries. Vale’s layoffs and reduced output reflect soft nickel markets, with only slight price improvement expected.

First Atlantic Nickel closed a C$1.07M charity flow-through private placement to fund Phase 2 drilling at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland. The company highlights its nickel-iron alloy (awaruite) and proceeds timing/renunciation mechanics, but the financing itself is unlikely to materially move global nickel prices immediately. | New Caledonia’s Koniambo nickel mine and factory shut down in August after Glencore exited the operation, with smelters turned off last year. The closure halted production of high-grade nickel (up to ~60,000 tonnes/year) and leaves thousands unemployed. Buyers may not emerge soon, extending the supply shock.

High-nickel pig iron prices (SMM 8-12%) fell RMB 19.35 per nickel point week-on-week to RMB 1,010.9, the largest decline this year. The drop is linked to weaker stainless-steel demand on macro concerns from reciprocal tariffs, cautious procurement, and expectations of continued nickel price pressure despite some supply and cost factors. | SMM’s Indonesian nickel market review says global nickel prices fell sharply on renewed Trump tariff policy and PNBP expectations, dragging down high-grade NPI prices and pressuring profits. Indonesian nickel ore premiums are stable due to tight supply, but demand weakens. Refined nickel supply also continues ramp-up.

CNBC discusses whether Trump’s reciprocal tariffs could trigger a commodities “super-cycle.” It cites higher copper front-month prices but warns tariffs may raise slowdown risks that pressure copper short term. It says nickel is unlikely to surge, while aluminium is among materials expected to be repriced upward as trade tensions persist. | Ardea Resources completed 287 infill reverse circulation drill holes at its Goongarrie Hub nickel project in Western Australia, reporting multiple high-grade nickel intercepts at Big Four and Scotia Dam. Management says results support potential resource upgrades and pit optimization work in its ongoing definitive feasibility study, which can strengthen near-term nickel sentiment. | The piece outlines U.S. reinstatement of full tariffs on steel (25%) and aluminum (25%) with a higher China rate and a 90-day pause that excludes steel and aluminum. It emphasizes continued elevated tariff burdens until at least July 9, 2025, likely weighing on aluminum-linked demand and prices near term.

Clean Energy Transition Inc. filed an NI 43-101 Technical Report supporting an updated Mineral Resource Estimate for its Aurora Nickel Project in Ontario. The Indicated Resource totals 10.06 Mt at 0.42% nickel, with open-pit and underground deposits. The company frames the update as strengthening the low-carbon nickel opportunity. | Talon Metals said drill hole 25TK0561 at its Tamarack nickel-copper project intersected 16.09 meters of mixed massive sulphide with 10%–80% sulphide content, potentially extending mineralization at depth. Management links the result to earlier BHEM modeling success and suggests potential tonnage addition as feasibility work progresses. | Talon Metals announced a mixed massive sulphide intercept (16.09m) at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project, with sulphide content indicating higher nickel grades. The company frames results as extending the CGO East Zone below the current resource and supporting potential early tonnage, but it remains feasibility-stage exploration news. | Kincora Copper announced an expanded earn-in/JV with AngloGold Ashanti to broaden drilling across the Nyngan, Nevertire and Nevertire South projects in NSW, targeting copper-gold porphyry systems. The update notes ongoing scout drilling and prior anomalous copper results, but remains exploration-focused with no production or logistics changes. | Bloomberg reports a production halt and investigation around Indonesia’s Morowali nickel hub after a landslide at a tailings area tied to PT QMB New Energy. Fatalities occurred, traders expect reduced output, and nearby producers cut production. Indonesia’s large share of global nickel supply raises immediate bullish pricing pressure.

The piece describes repeated deadly incidents and alleged systematic safety shortcomings at Tsingshan’s IMIP nickel processing and stainless steel facilities in Indonesia, including a December 2023 nickel-plant explosion and another fatal smelter accident in February 2025. Despite production resuming quickly after one death, the accidents heighten perceived supply-risk and oversight concerns.

Indonesia’s nickel smelters association asked the government to delay higher mineral royalty fees until LME nickel prices reach $17,000/ton, above current levels (~$15,600). The new royalties (14%–19% on ore; higher rates on nickel pig iron and matte) would raise costs amid weakening nickel prices. | Indonesia plans higher nickel royalties, replacing a flat 10% levy on nickel ore with a variable rate of 14%-19% linked to government-set price levels. Lower-grade ore for battery-grade nickel faces a reduced 2% royalty. The change takes effect shortly, while miners warn of margin strain and output cuts. | Realtor.com reports that Trump’s 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum imports could raise construction costs for newly built residences. Builders may pass higher costs to consumers or delay finishing construction, reducing housing supply. This could push rents higher in multiple US metros as tariffs dampen development plans.

Indonesia dominates global nickel output and is pushing downstream processing after a raw ore export ban, building smelters and HPAL capacity. Nickel’s role in EV cathodes underpins demand growth from automakers. However, expanded refining could also increase refined supply, making the net near-term price effect uncertain. | Copper rose to a two-week high as a weaker U.S. dollar encouraged CTA/fund buying and traders covered shorts, breaching key moving averages. However, sentiment remains cautious because Trump’s import tariffs threaten growth and industrial metal demand.
